```py
  119 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  120 | # Check digits (real algorithms — SPEC AC4). isbn implements both ISBN-10 (mod 11,
  121 | # 'X'=10) and ISBN-13 (EAN mod 10). issn is mod 11 with 'X'=10.
  122 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  123 | def _digits_only(s, allow_x=True):

  125 |     if allow_x:
  126 |         return ''.join(c for c in s if c.isdigit() or c == 'X')
  127 |     return ''.join(c for c in s if c.isdigit())
  128 | 
  129 |

  132 |     digit). Empty / wrong-length → False (a non-empty ISBN must be valid)."""

  135 |         total = 0
  136 |         for i, c in enumerate(s):
```

```py
  152 | def issn_checksum_ok(raw):
  153 |     """True iff ``raw`` is a structurally valid ISSN (8 chars, mod-11, 'X'=10)."""

  165 |         else:
  166 |             return False
  167 |         total += (8 - i) * d
  168 |     return total % 11 == 0

  171 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  172 | # 920 branching (SPEC §2.2). Masks: exact value ('J') or prefix ('NJ*' ≡ v920:'NJ').
  173 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  174 | def _mask_matches(record_type, mask):
  175 |     if mask.endswith('*'):
  176 |         return record_type.startswith(mask[:-1])
  177 |     return record_type == mask
  178 | 
  179 | 
  180 | def branch_applies(branch, record_type):
  181 |     """Does a rule's ``branch`` admit this ``record_type`` (= v920)?
  182 | 
  183 |     No branch => always applies. ``negate=False``: applies when v920 matches any
  184 |     mask. ``negate=True``: applies when v920 matches NONE of the masks."""
  185 |     if not branch:
  186 |         return True
  187 |     masks = branch.get('match', [])
  188 |     hit = any(_mask_matches(record_type, m) for m in masks)
  189 |     return (not hit) if branch.get('negate') else hit
  190 |
```

```py
  192 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  193 | # Dictionary lookup over the A5-seeded vocab store (does NOT re-implement seeding).
  194 | # A store exposes ``vocabulary_values(name)`` -> [{'code':..., 'active':...}, ...].
  195 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  196 | def _dict_has(store, vocab, code):
  197 |     """True iff ``code`` is an active value of ``vocab`` in this tenant's store.
  198 | 
  199 |     Case-insensitive on the code (ИРБИС dictionary codes are matched loosely). If
  200 |     the store can't answer (no vocab tables / missing dict), returns None so the
  201 |     caller can treat it as 'unknown' rather than a false violation."""
  202 |     if store is None or not code:
  203 |         return None
  204 |     try:
  205 |         rows = store.vocabulary_values(vocab, active_only=True)
  206 |     except Exception:
  207 |         return None
  208 |     if not rows:
  209 |         return None
  210 |     code_l = code.strip().lower()
  211 |     return any((r['code'] or '').strip().lower() == code_l for r in rows)
  212 |

  214 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

  218 | def _eval_when(rule, record):
  219 |     """Applicability predicate (SPEC ``when``). Currently supports ``present``
  220 |     on the rule's own field/subfield (the only ``when`` shape this slice needs).
  221 |     Returns True if the rule should be evaluated."""
  222 |     w = rule.get('when')
  223 |     if not w:
  224 |         return True
  225 |     if w == 'present':
  226 |         return present(record, rule['field'], rule.get('subfield'))
  227 |     if w == 'absent':
  228 |         return not present(record, rule['field'], rule.get('subfield'))
  229 |     # Anything richer is an A1 expression — be permissive (evaluate the rule).
  230 |     return True
  231 | 
  232 | 
  233 | def evaluate_rule(rule, record, ctx):
  234 |     """Evaluate one (already enabled, already branch-admitted) rule.
  235 | 
  236 |     Returns a Violation dict if the rule fires, else None. Never raises: an
  237 |     internal predicate error degrades to an ``engineError`` soft violation
  238 |     (SPEC AC11) so one bad rule can't sink the whole run."""
  239 |     try:
  240 |         if not _eval_when(rule, record):
  241 |             return None
  242 |         kind = rule['predicate']
  243 |         fired = _PREDICATES[kind](rule, record, ctx)
  244 |         # 'duplicate' may return the STUB sentinel — never a real violation yet.
  245 |         if fired is _STUB:
  246 |             return None
```

```py
  310 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  311 | # Authority-link (^3) integrity — INTEGRATION_MAP edge 6.3, SPEC_service_authority
  312 | # §0/§4.3. Each heading field (700/701/710/606/607/…) may carry ``^3`` = the
  313 | # authority record number; ФЛК checks the link resolves against the authority
  314 | # base. A non-empty ``^3`` that the authority store can't find is a DANGLING link
  315 | # (DB_AUTHORITY §5.2) → severity-2 violation (преодолимо: the operator may be
  316 | # importing or the authority is pending review). Self-contained map (kept local
  317 | # so flk does not import authority): catalog field tag → authority db + kind.
  318 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  319 | AUTHORITY_LINK_FIELDS = {
  320 |     '700': ('athra', 'personal'),
  321 |     '701': ('athra', 'personal'),
  322 |     '702': ('athra', 'personal'),
  323 |     '600': ('athra', 'personal'),
  324 |     '710': ('athrc', 'corporate'),
  325 |     '711': ('athrc', 'corporate'),
  326 |     '601': ('athrc', 'corporate'),
  327 |     '606': ('athrs', 'subject'),
  328 |     '607': ('athrg', 'geographic'),
  329 | }
  330 | 
  331 | 
  332 | def _authref_broken(record, authority):
  333 |     """Return the list of ``(field, instance, ^3)`` whose ``^3`` does NOT resolve.
  334 | 
  335 |     ``authority`` exposes ``get(db, id) -> record|None`` (AuthorityStore). For each
  336 |     authority-linked field instance carrying a non-empty ``^3``, resolve it in the
  337 |     bound authority db; a None result == dangling link. A ``^3`` that isn't an
  338 |     integer-coercible id is also reported as broken (malformed reference)."""
  339 |     broken = []
  340 |     for tag, (db, _kind) in AUTHORITY_LINK_FIELDS.items():
  341 |         for i, inst in enumerate(_instances(record, tag)):
  342 |             ref = _inst_value(inst, '3')
  343 |             if not ref:
  344 |                 continue
  345 |             try:
  346 |                 rid = int(str(ref))
  347 |             except (TypeError, ValueError):
  348 |                 broken.append((tag, i, ref))
  349 |                 continue
  350 |             try:
  351 |                 hit = authority.get(db, rid)
  352 |             except Exception:                 # store can't answer -> don't fabricate
  353 |                 continue
  354 |             if hit is None:
  355 |                 broken.append((tag, i, str(ref)))
  356 |     return broken
```

```py
  359 | def _pred_authority_ref(rule, record, ctx):

  374 | _PREDICATES = {

  385 | def _make_violation(rule, record, ctx=None):

  388 |         'severity': rule['severity'],
  389 |         'message': _format_message(rule, record, ctx),

  391 |         'field': rule.get('field'),
  392 |         'subfield': rule.get('subfield'),

  394 |

  396 | def _engine_error(rule, detail):

  398 |         'ruleId': rule['id'],
  399 |         'severity': SEV_SOFT,            # never block on an engine failure
  400 |         'message': 'Правило %s не вычислено (%s)' % (rule['id'], detail),
  401 |         'path': rule.get('path', rule.get('field', '')),
  402 |         'field': rule.get('field'),
  403 |         'subfield': rule.get('subfield'),
  404 |         'engineError': True,
  405 |     }

  408 | _TEMPLATE = re.compile(r'\{\{\s*([0-9]+)(?:\^([a-zA-Z]))?\s*\}\}')

  411 | def _format_message(rule, record, ctx=None):
```

```py
  412 |     """Substitute ``{{NNN^x}}`` / ``{{NNN}}`` tokens from the record (thin local
  413 |     version of SPEC's A1.format; full templating is A1).
  414 | 
  415 |     Special token ``{{authref}}`` expands to the list of dangling ``^3`` links the
  416 |     authority-ref predicate stashed on ctx (edge 6.3), so the operator sees which
  417 |     references are broken (e.g. ``700#0=^3:999``)."""
  418 |     msg = rule.get('message', '')
  419 | 
  420 |     def repl(m):
  421 |         return value(record, m.group(1), m.group(2)) or ''
  422 |     msg = _TEMPLATE.sub(repl, msg)
  423 |     if '{{authref}}' in msg:
  424 |         broken = (ctx or {}).get('_authref_broken') or []
  425 |         detail = ', '.join('%s#%d=^3:%s' % (tag, i, ref) for tag, i, ref in broken)
  426 |         msg = msg.replace('{{authref}}', detail)
  427 |     return msg
```

```py
  559 | 
  560 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  561 | # Ruleset loading + per-tenant override overlay (SPEC §3).
  562 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  563 | def _clone(rule):
  564 |     r = dict(rule)
  565 |     if 'branch' in r and r['branch'] is not None:
  566 |         r['branch'] = dict(r['branch'])
  567 |     return r
  568 | 
  569 | 
  570 | def load_ruleset(tenant_overrides=None, allow_hardening=False):
  571 |     """Return the effective ruleset = canon ⊕ per-tenant deltas (SPEC §3.1).
  572 | 
  573 |     ``tenant_overrides`` maps ``ruleId -> {enabled?, severity?, message?}``. A
  574 |     delta may DISABLE a rule, SOFTEN it (1→2), or override its message. Hardening
  575 |     (2→1) is refused unless ``allow_hardening`` (SPEC §3.1 policy R4) — the canon
  576 |     severity is kept instead. ``None`` fields inherit the canon.
```

```py
  577 | 
  578 |     Returns a list of effective rule dicts (canon order preserved)."""
  579 |     overrides = tenant_overrides or {}
  580 |     out = []
  581 |     for canon in SYSTEM_RULES:
  582 |         eff = _clone(canon)
  583 |         ov = overrides.get(canon['id'])
  584 |         if ov:
  585 |             if ov.get('enabled') is not None:
  586 |                 eff['enabled'] = bool(ov['enabled'])
  587 |             if ov.get('severity') is not None:
  588 |                 new_sev = int(ov['severity'])
  589 |                 # softening (canon 1 -> 2) always allowed; hardening gated.
  590 |                 if new_sev >= eff['severity'] or allow_hardening:
  591 |                     eff['severity'] = new_sev
  592 |             if ov.get('message') is not None:
  593 |                 eff['message'] = ov['message']
  594 |         out.append(eff)
  595 |     return out
  596 | 
  597 | 
  598 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  599 | # The engine — evaluate a record against a ruleset for one phase.
  600 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  601 | def validate(record, ruleset=None, phase='save', field=None,
  602 |              store=None, current_mfn=None, dup_index=None,
  603 |              tenant_overrides=None, authority=None):
  604 |     """Validate ``record`` for ``phase`` and aggregate worst-severity (SPEC §2).
  605 | 
  606 |     Pipeline (SPEC §2.1–2.4):
  607 |       1. resolve the ruleset (canon ⊕ tenant overrides) if not supplied;
  608 |       2. filter by ``enabled`` and ``phase`` (and ``field`` for fieldExit);
  609 |       3. 920 first (selector), then by field, then by rule id (determinism);
  610 |       4. apply ``branch`` (920 record-type gating) and ``when``;
  611 |       5. evaluate the predicate -> Violation (engineError-isolated);
  612 |       6. aggregate: overallSeverity = worst, canSave = (overall != 1), sorted list.
  613 | 
  614 |     Returns ``{overallSeverity, canSave, violations:[...]}``."""
  615 |     if ruleset is None:
  616 |         ruleset = load_ruleset(tenant_overrides)
  617 |     record_type = value(record, '920') or ''
  618 |     ctx = {'store': store, 'current_mfn': current_mfn, 'dup_index': dup_index,
  619 |            'authority': authority}
  620 | 
  621 |     # SPEC §2.1: the `save` phase is a FULL run — it also includes the field-level
  622 |     # `!NNN` (fieldExit) rules, not only record-scope `save` rules. `fieldExit` on
  623 |     # its own runs just that field's rules (live per-field feedback).
  624 |     if phase == 'save':
  625 |         active = [r for r in ruleset if r.get('enabled', True)
  626 |                   and r.get('phase') in ('save', 'fieldExit')]
  627 |     else:
  628 |         active = [r for r in ruleset if r.get('enabled', True) and r.get('phase') == phase]
  629 |     if phase == 'fieldExit' and field is not None:
  630 |         active = [r for r in active if r.get('field') == field]
  631 | 
```

<!-- ─── страница 172 ─── -->

```py
  632 |     # Order: 920 selector first, then ascending field label, then rule id (SPEC §2.1).
  633 |     def _order_key(r):
  634 |         is_920 = 0 if r.get('field') == '920' else 1
  635 |         try:
  636 |             fld = int(r.get('field') or 0)
  637 |         except ValueError:
  638 |             fld = 0
  639 |         return (is_920, fld, r['id'])
  640 |     active.sort(key=_order_key)
  641 | 
  642 |     violations = []
  643 |     for rule in active:
  644 |         if not branch_applies(rule.get('branch'), record_type):
  645 |             continue
  646 |         v = evaluate_rule(rule, record, ctx)
  647 |         if v is not None:
  648 |             violations.append(v)
  649 | 
  650 |     overall = _worst_severity(violations)
  651 |     # Sort for the UI: severity 1 first, then 2; within, by path (SPEC §2.4).
  652 |     violations.sort(key=lambda v: (0 if v['severity'] == SEV_HARD else 1,
  653 |                                    v.get('path') or ''))
  654 |     return {
  655 |         'overallSeverity': overall,
  656 |         'canSave': overall != SEV_HARD,
  657 |         'violations': violations,
  658 |     }
  659 | 
  660 | 
  661 | def _worst_severity(violations):
  662 |     """worst() over the list: 1 if any hard, else 2 if any soft, else 0."""
  663 |     if any(v['severity'] == SEV_HARD for v in violations):
  664 |         return SEV_HARD
  665 |     if any(v['severity'] == SEV_SOFT for v in violations):
  666 |         return SEV_SOFT
  667 |     return SEV_PASS
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/authority.py`  · строк: 435

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Authority-files service — first shippable slice (gap A4, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Self-contained sqlite store + lookup + substitution pipeline for the six
    5 | ИРБИС authority bases (ATHRA/C/S/G/U/B), unified onto one polymorphic
    6 | ``authority_record`` table with JSON subfields — the model decided in
    7 | ``docs/design/specs/engines/SPEC_service_authority.md`` §1 and grounded on
    8 | ``docs/recon/deep/reference/databases/DB_AUTHORITY.md``.
    9 | 
   10 | What this module ships (SPEC §9 critical path, steps 1-3 — the search +
   11 | substitution core; plus the autoin hook of step 4, edge 6.2; merge/trees remain
   12 | later slices):
   13 | 
   14 |   * ``AuthorityStore`` — own sqlite tables ``authority_record`` (id, kind, db,
   15 |     data_json) + ``authority_term`` (authority_id, term) created on init.
   16 |     The term table is the projection that ports the FST dictionary (SPEC §1.3):
   17 |     every searchable string of a record is denormalised into one ``term`` row.
   18 |   * ``search(db, q)`` — prefix/substring lookup over ``authority_term`` (SPEC §2.1,
   19 |     AC-L1); empty store → empty list, never an error.
```

<!-- ─── страница 173 ─── -->

```py
   20 |   * ``get(db, id)`` — full record (SPEC §2.2, AC-L3).
   21 |   * ``substitute(catalog_field_tag, authority_record)`` — the fill-map step of the
   22 |     substitution pipeline (SPEC §3.1 step [4]/[5], §3.2 table): returns the subfield
   23 |     patch ``{'a':…,'b':…,'g':…,'3':authority_id}`` to write into the catalog field,
   24 |     always carrying the ``^3`` link = the authority record id (DB_AUTHORITY §5.2).
   25 |   * ``AuthorityStore.autoin(catalog_field_tag, subfields)`` — the autoin hook
   26 |     (SPEC §3.4, port of ``autoin.gbl``) for the REVERSE edge «catalog → ATHR*»
   27 |     (INTEGRATION_MAP 6.2): a catalog heading (700/710/606/…) saved WITHOUT ``^3``
   28 |     is folded to an alcode, linked to an existing authority record by that alcode
   29 |     or, failing that, materialised as a fresh ``needs_review`` record. Returns
   30 |     ``(authority_id, created)`` — the id to thread into ``^3``.
   31 | 
   32 | Design notes (paritet with ground truth):
   33 |   * ``db`` ∈ {athra, athrc, athrs, athrg, athru, athrb} ↔ ``kind`` ∈
   34 |     {personal, corporate, subject, geographic, udc_index, bbk_index}
   35 |     (SPEC §1.1 table). ``search``/``get`` are scoped by ``db`` so a term in one
   36 |     base never leaks into another (paritet RLS isolation, AC-L5 at the db axis).
   37 |   * Catalog subfield keys are lowercase ('a','b','g','f','s','c','h','9','3') —
   38 |     the SPEC §3.2 fill-map convention (FIELD_DICTIONARY writes them ^A/^B/… in
   39 |     upper, but the JSON patch the client applies in the DynamicField is keyed by
   40 |     the lowercased letter). ``^3`` is *always* the authority id, never copied
   41 |     from the authority's own subfields (DB_AUTHORITY §5.2: catalog ^3 = number of
   42 |     the authority record).
   43 | 
   44 | The store accepts ``:memory:`` or a temp path so tests run with no DB server,
   45 | matching the AccessStore pattern in ``access/store.py``.
   46 | """
   47 | import json
   48 | import sqlite3
   49 | import threading
   50 | 
   51 | 
   52 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   53 | # db (ИРБИС authority base) <-> kind (polymorphic discriminator).  SPEC §1.1.
   54 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   55 | DB_TO_KIND = {
   56 |     'athra': 'personal',
   57 |     'athrc': 'corporate',
   58 |     'athrs': 'subject',
   59 |     'athrg': 'geographic',
   60 |     'athru': 'udc_index',
   61 |     'athrb': 'bbk_index',
   62 | }
   63 | KIND_TO_DB = {v: k for k, v in DB_TO_KIND.items()}
   64 | 
   65 | 
   66 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   67 | # Fill-map (SPEC §3.2).  catalog_field_tag -> spec describing which authority
   68 | # heading_210 subfields flow into which catalog subfields, plus the binding db.
   69 | #
   70 | # Catalog-side keys are lowercase letters (and '3' for the ^3 link).  Each tuple
   71 | # is (catalog_key, authority_key): copy authority.heading_210[authority_key] into
   72 | # the patch under catalog_key when present.  '3' is appended automatically by
   73 | # substitute() and is intentionally NOT in these lists (it is the record id, not
   74 | # an authority subfield — DB_AUTHORITY §5.2).
```

```py
  116 | class AuthorityNotFound(Exception):
  117 |     """Raised by substitute() when handed a None / unknown authority record."""

  120 | class UnknownCatalogField(Exception):

  124 | SCHEMA_SQLITE = """
  125 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS authority_record (
  126 |   id        INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  127 |   kind      TEXT NOT NULL,
  128 |   db        TEXT NOT NULL,
  129 |   data_json TEXT NOT NULL
```

```py
  130 | );
  131 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS authority_record_db_idx ON authority_record(db);
  132 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS authority_term (
  133 |   authority_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES authority_record(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  134 |   term         TEXT NOT NULL,

  141 | 
  142 | def _norm(s):
  143 |     """Normalisation for the search projection (SPEC §1.3 term_norm): lower-case,
  144 |     collapsed whitespace.  Diacritic folding is out of scope for this slice."""
  145 |     return ' '.join(str(s).lower().split())
  146 | 
  147 | 
  148 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  149 | # Алкод свёртки заголовка (SPEC §3.4 / §4.1 слой 1 — детерминированный
  150 | # дублетный ключ).  Минимальный порт `normalizer.fold(subfields) → alcode`:
  151 | # конкатенация значимых подполей заголовка в нормализованную строку.  Это ключ,
  152 | # по которому autoin (§3.4) находит уже существующую авторитетную запись либо
  153 | # заводит новую — точное совпадение алкода == точный дубль.
  154 | #
  155 | # `pairs` берётся из FILL_MAP[catalog_tag] (каталог-подполе ⇆ авторитет-подполе),
  156 | # поэтому свёртка считается по тем же подполям, что и подстановка — заголовок,
  157 | # собранный из БО, сворачивается так же, как заголовок из авторитета.
  158 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  159 | def fold_heading(heading, auth_keys):
  160 |     """Свернуть подполя заголовка (по ключам авторитета ``auth_keys``) в алкод.
  161 | 
  162 |     ``heading`` — словарь подполей (значения авторитет-подполей: 'a','b','g',…);
  163 |     ``auth_keys`` — упорядоченный список авторитет-подполей, участвующих в свёртке.
  164 |     Возвращает нормализованную строку-ключ ('' если ни одно значимое подполе не
  165 |     заполнено).  Порядок ключей фиксирован, поэтому ключ детерминирован."""
  166 |     parts = []
  167 |     for k in auth_keys:
  168 |         v = heading.get(k)
  169 |         if v:
  170 |             parts.append(_norm(v))
  171 |     return ' | '.join(p for p in parts if p)
  172 | 
  173 | 
  174 | def _auth_fold_keys_for_kind(kind):
  175 |     """Авторитет-подполя свёртки для данного ``kind``: берём ``pairs`` первого
  176 |     каталог-поля FILL_MAP с этим kind (например personal → 700 → a/b/g/f/9).
  177 |     Так свёртка записи-авторитета и свёртка заголовка из БО считаются по одному
  178 |     набору подполей.  Неизвестный kind → пустой список (алкод == '')."""
  179 |     for spec in FILL_MAP.values():
  180 |         if spec.get('kind') == kind:
  181 |             return [auth_key for _ck, auth_key in spec['pairs']]
  182 |     return []
  183 | 
  184 | 
```

```py
  185 | class AuthorityStore:
  186 |     """Self-contained sqlite store for authority records + the term projection.
  187 | 
  188 |     Pass ``:memory:`` or a temp path; schema is created on init (paritet
  189 |     AccessStore).  One connection per thread (sqlite objects aren't shareable
  190 |     across threads).
  191 |     """
  192 | 
  193 |     def __init__(self, db_path=':memory:'):
  194 |         self.db_path = db_path
  195 |         self._local = threading.local()
  196 |         self.ensure_schema()
  197 | 
  198 |     def _conn(self):
  199 |         c = getattr(self._local, 'conn', None)
  200 |         if c is None:
  201 |             c = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)
  202 |             c.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
  203 |             c.execute('PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON')
  204 |             self._local.conn = c
  205 |         return c
  206 | 
  207 |     def ensure_schema(self):
  208 |         c = self._conn()
  209 |         c.executescript(SCHEMA_SQLITE)
  210 |         c.commit()
  211 | 
  212 |     # ---- write ----------------------------------------------------------- #
  213 |     def add_record(self, db, heading_210, terms=None, **extra):
  214 |         """Insert one authority record and project its search terms.
  215 | 
  216 |         ``db`` is one of DB_TO_KIND keys (kind is derived).  ``heading_210`` is the
  217 |         dict of accepted-heading subfields ({'a':…,'b':…,…}).  ``terms`` is an
  218 |         optional explicit list of searchable strings (ports the FST projection,
  219 |         SPEC §1.3); when omitted, the heading values are projected as terms so a
  220 |         record is always findable by its own subfield text.  ``extra`` is merged
  221 |         into the stored record (e.g. worklist, see_410, classmarks).
  222 | 
  223 |         Returns the new record id, which is the ``^3`` link target.
  224 |         """
  225 |         db = db.lower()
  226 |         if db not in DB_TO_KIND:
  227 |             raise ValueError('unknown authority db %r (expected one of %s)'
  228 |                              % (db, ', '.join(sorted(DB_TO_KIND))))
  229 |         kind = DB_TO_KIND[db]
  230 |         record = dict(extra)
  231 |         record['kind'] = kind
  232 |         record['db'] = db
  233 |         record['heading_210'] = dict(heading_210)
  234 |         c = self._conn()
  235 |         cur = c.execute(
  236 |             'INSERT INTO authority_record(kind, db, data_json) VALUES(?,?,?)',
  237 |             (kind, db, json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False)))
  238 |         rec_id = cur.lastrowid
  239 |         # write the id back into the stored JSON so get() echoes its own id
```

```py
  243 |         # ---- term projection (FST port, SPEC §1.3) ----

  246 |         seen = set()

  259 |     # ---- read ------------------------------------------------------------ #
  260 |     def get(self, db, rec_id):
  261 |         """Full authority record (dict) for ``rec_id`` within ``db``, or None.
  262 | 
  263 |         Scoped by ``db`` so a personal-author id can't be fetched as a subject
  264 |         (paritet base isolation).  SPEC §2.2 / AC-L3.
  265 |         """
  266 |         r = self._conn().execute(
  267 |             'SELECT data_json FROM authority_record WHERE id=? AND db=?',
  268 |             (rec_id, db.lower())).fetchone()
  269 |         return json.loads(r['data_json']) if r else None
  270 | 
  271 |     def search(self, db, q, mode='prefix', limit=20):
  272 |         """Lookup over the term projection within ``db``.  SPEC §2.1, AC-L1.
  273 | 
  274 |         ``mode='prefix'`` matches term_norm by right-truncation (paritet
  275 |         словарного поиска ``A=ТОЛС…``); ``mode='substring'`` matches anywhere.
  276 |         Empty store / no hit → ``[]`` (never an error — AC-L1).  Each hit is the
  277 |         full record dict with an added ``'matched'`` term for UI display, and a
  278 |         ready ``'fill_hint'`` echo of the heading (SPEC §2.1 ``fill`` block,
  279 |         AC-L4 — client doesn't reconstruct subfields).
  280 |         """
  281 |         qn = _norm(q)
  282 |         if not qn:
  283 |             return []
  284 |         if mode == 'substring':
  285 |             pattern = '%' + qn.replace('%', r'\%').replace('_', r'\_') + '%'
  286 |         else:  # prefix (default)
  287 |             pattern = qn.replace('%', r'\%').replace('_', r'\_') + '%'
  288 |         rows = self._conn().execute(
  289 |             "SELECT DISTINCT t.authority_id AS aid, r.data_json AS data_json "
  290 |             "FROM authority_term t JOIN authority_record r ON r.id = t.authority_id "
  291 |             "WHERE r.db=? AND t.term_norm LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\' "
  292 |             "ORDER BY t.authority_id LIMIT ?",
  293 |             (db.lower(), pattern, limit)).fetchall()
  294 |         hits = []
```

```py
  307 |     # ---- autoin (SPEC §3.4, порт autoin.gbl — обратное ребро 6.2) --------- #
  308 |     def find_by_alcode(self, db, alcode):
  309 |         """Найти id принятой авторитетной записи по алкоду свёртки в ``db``.
  310 | 
  311 |         Сканирует записи базы и сравнивает их собственный алкод (свёртка
  312 |         heading_210 по подполям FILL_MAP) с ``alcode``.  Возвращает id первого
  313 |         совпадения или None.  Это слой-1 дедупликации (SPEC §4.1): точное
  314 |         совпадение алкода == уже существующая запись."""
  315 |         if not alcode:
  316 |             return None
  317 |         db = db.lower()
  318 |         kind = DB_TO_KIND.get(db)
  319 |         # авторитет-подполя свёртки берём по первому каталог-полю этого kind.
  320 |         auth_keys = _auth_fold_keys_for_kind(kind)
  321 |         rows = self._conn().execute(
  322 |             'SELECT id, data_json FROM authority_record WHERE db=?',
  323 |             (db,)).fetchall()
  324 |         for r in rows:
  325 |             rec = json.loads(r['data_json'])
  326 |             if fold_heading(rec.get('heading_210', {}), auth_keys) == alcode:
  327 |                 return r['id']
  328 |         return None
  329 | 
  330 |     def autoin(self, catalog_field_tag, subfields, *, status='needs_review',
  331 |                worklist=None):
  332 |         """Авто-создать/привязать авторитетную запись по заголовку из БО.
  333 | 
  334 |         Порт `autoin.gbl` для **обратного** ребра «каталог → пополнение ATHR*»
  335 |         (INTEGRATION_MAP 6.2, SPEC §3.4): когда в БО сохраняется поле-заголовок
  336 |         (700/710/606/…) БЕЗ ``^3``, мы по его подполям
  337 | 
  338 |           1) считаем алкод свёртки (``fold_heading`` по подполям FILL_MAP);
  339 |           2) ищем существующую принятую запись с тем же алкодом →
  340 |              привязываемся к ней (без новой записи, паритет AC-S5/T9);
  341 |           3) иначе заводим новую запись (``status='needs_review'``, паритет
  342 |              autoin — «требует ревизии каталогизатором») и берём её id.
  343 | 
  344 |         Возвращает ``(authority_id, created)``: ``authority_id`` — номер для
  345 |         протяжки в ``^3``; ``created`` — True если создана новая запись, False
  346 |         если привязка к существующей.  Поднимает ``UnknownCatalogField`` для
  347 |         каталог-поля без fill-map и ``ValueError`` если из подполей нечего
  348 |         свернуть (пустой заголовок — нечего пополнять)."""
  349 |         tag = str(catalog_field_tag)
```

```py
  382 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  383 | # Substitution pipeline — fill-map step (SPEC §3.1 [4]/[5], §3.2).
  384 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  385 | def substitute(catalog_field_tag, authority_record):
  386 |     """Build the subfield patch to write into a catalog field from a chosen
  387 |     authority record (SPEC §3.1 steps map+link, §3.2 fill-map; AC-L4/AC-S1..S3).
  388 | 
  389 |     Returns a dict ``{catalog_subfield: value, …, '3': authority_id}`` —
  390 |     e.g. for a person in 700:
  391 |         {'a': 'Толстой', 'b': 'Л. Н.', 'g': 'Лев Николаевич', '3': '42'}
  392 | 
  393 |     The ``^3`` link is *always* added (= the authority record id) for fields that
  394 |     carry it, and is NEVER taken from the authority's own subfields
  395 |     (DB_AUTHORITY §5.2).  Index fields 675/621 have ``link=False`` → no ^3.
  396 | 
  397 |     Only fill-map subfields present (and truthy) in the authority's heading are
  398 |     written; absent subfields are simply omitted so the operator/ФЛК can fill
  399 |     role ^9 / ^4 etc. without the pipeline overwriting them (SPEC §3.2 note).
  400 | 
  401 |     Raises:
  402 |       AuthorityNotFound   — authority_record is None (missing authority, AC graceful).
  403 |       UnknownCatalogField — catalog_field_tag has no fill-map entry.
  404 |     """
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/pft.py`  · строк: 1594

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """PFT formatting engine — the A1 keystone (epic #188, SPEC_engine_pft.md).
    3 | 
    4 | This is the self-contained *expression core* of the ИРБИС64 PFT formatting
    5 | language that the ФЛК (A2, ``access/flk.py``) and глобальная корректировка (A3,
    6 | ``.gbl``) engines delegate to. It is the in-process module contract formalized in
    7 | SPEC_api_contracts §3.1 / mismatch M4:
    8 | 
    9 |     pft.eval(fmt, record, ctx)       -> str          # single-line result
   10 |     pft.eval_lines(fmt, record, ctx) -> list[str]    # multi-line (repeat groups)
   11 | 
   12 | Scope (first shippable slice — SPEC §1.3 Phase 1 + a Phase-2 / Phase-3 subset)
   13 | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   14 | A *hybrid interpreter*: the format string is tokenized → parsed to a small AST →
   15 | interpreted against a record. Output is a plain string (the IR-document /
   16 | HTML/PDF rendering of SPEC §1.1 is a later phase; here the IR collapses to text).
   17 | 
   18 | Record shape (the I1 field/subfield draft — same as ``access/flk.py``)::
   19 | 
   20 |     {
   21 |       "200": [{"a": "Заглавие", "f": "Автор"}],   # repeating field = list of instances
   22 |       "910": [{"a": "0", "b": "INV1"}, {"b": "INV2"}],
   23 |       "101": "rus",                                # bare-string field
   24 |       "10":  {"a": "5-7654-0001-X"}                # single instance (dict)
```

<!-- ─── страница 181 ─── -->

```py
   25 |     }
   26 | 
   27 | A field may be a bare string, a ``{subfield: value}`` dict, or a list of either.
   28 | 
   29 | Implemented PFT constructs
   30 | --------------------------
   31 |   * Field refs: ``v200``, ``v200^a``, ``v200^*`` (first subfield), fragment
   32 |     ``v200^a*off.len`` / ``v1*3.3`` (SPEC §2).
   33 |   * ``mfn`` / ``mfn(d)`` — record number from ``ctx['mfn']`` (SPEC §2).
   34 |   * Literals: conditional ``"…"`` (once if the *adjacent* field is present),
   35 |     repeatable ``|…|`` (per instance, incl. ``|x|+`` prefix / ``+|x|`` suffix),
   36 |     unconditional ``'…'`` (always) (SPEC §5).
   37 |   * Dummy output ``d<tag>`` / ``n<tag>`` (literal by field presence/absence, §6).
   38 |   * ``IF … THEN … ELSE … FI`` with nesting; conditions ``= <> < <= > >= :``
   39 |     (contains) and ``NOT / AND / OR``; predicates ``p(...)`` / ``a(...)`` (§7,§10).
   40 |   * Repeat groups ``(… v910 …)`` — one pass per instance of the repeating field
   41 |     inside (SPEC §10); ``eval_lines`` surfaces them as separate lines.
   42 |   * Concatenation by ``,`` and ``+`` (SPEC §7).
   43 |   * ``f(expr[,width[,dec]])`` number→string; ``val(...)`` text→number;
   44 |     ``s(...)`` concatenation/implicit-OR (SPEC §8).
   45 |   * ``&unifor('…')`` / ``&uf('…')`` — the UNIFOR registry (see ``UNIFOR``).
   46 | 
   47 | UNIFOR codes implemented (vs degraded)
   48 | --------------------------------------
   49 | Implemented (first char of the argument):
   50 |   ``C`` ISBN/ISSN checksum control (0 ok / 1 error) — **the code ФЛК A2 reuses**;
   51 |   ``3`` date/time (``3`` ГГГГММДД, ``30`` year, ``31`` month, ``32`` day, ``33``
   52 |   YY, ``34``/``35`` no-lead-zero, ``39`` time, today's date from ``ctx['now']``);
   53 |   ``A``/``P`` Nth field occurrence (``Av910^a#2``); ``9`` strip double-quotes;
   54 |   ``Q`` lowercase; ``X`` strip ``<…>``; ``E`` first N words; ``!`` / ``+F``
   55 |   post-edit (collapse double separators / drop RTF); ``6`` nested template format
   56 |   (``6<name>`` resolved from ``ctx['modules']``, ``%N`` params).
   57 | Degraded to empty string (carried, never crash — SPEC §2.1 risk, §15 "Format
   58 | error 99" parity): every other code (``D``,``7``,``J``,``l``-index lookups,
   59 | ``+1``/``+7`` globals beyond ctx, ``T``,``+I``,``$`` …). Unknown ⇒ ``''``.
   60 | 
   61 | Graceful degradation
   62 | ---------------------
   63 | The engine NEVER raises on malformed/unsupported input: an unparsable construct
   64 | is skipped (emits ``''``), so a partially-understood real ``.pft`` still yields
   65 | the parts we understand rather than a 500. ``strict=True`` opts into raising
   66 | ``PftError`` (used by the format editor / preview path).
   67 | """
   68 | from __future__ import annotations
   69 | 
   70 | import datetime as _dt
   71 | import re
   72 | 
   73 | __all__ = [
   74 |     'eval', 'eval_lines', 'PftError', 'UNIFOR', 'register_unifor',
   75 |     'isbn_issn_ok',
   76 | ]
   77 | 
   78 | 
   79 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
```

<!-- ─── страница 182 ─── -->

```py
   80 | # Errors. In non-strict mode (the default, what A2/A3 use) we never raise.
   81 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   82 | class PftError(Exception):
   83 |     """A PFT format/parse error. Carries an ИРБИС-style numeric ``code``
   84 |     (SPEC §15 / PFT_LANGUAGE §15): 8 IF-without-THEN, 19/20 unbalanced parens,
   85 |     53 IF-without-FI, 99 unknown command."""
   86 | 
   87 |     def __init__(self, message, code=99):
   88 |         super().__init__(message)
   89 |         self.code = code
   90 | 
   91 | 
   92 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   93 | # Record-draft accessors. A field can be a bare string, a {subfield:val} dict,
   94 | # or a list of either (repeating). Mirrors access/flk.py so A2 and A1 agree.
   95 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   96 | def _instances(record, tag):
   97 |     raw = record.get(tag) if record else None
   98 |     if raw is None:
   99 |         return []
  100 |     if isinstance(raw, list):
  101 |         return raw
  102 |     return [raw]
  103 | 
  104 | 
  105 | def _inst_value(inst, subfield):
  106 |     """Read ``subfield`` from one instance. subfield=None => whole field value."""
  107 |     if isinstance(inst, dict):
  108 |         if subfield is None or subfield == '':
  109 |             # whole-field value: the bare '' key, else join subfields in order
  110 |             if inst.get(''):
  111 |                 return inst['']
  112 |             if inst.get('value'):
  113 |                 return inst['value']
  114 |             return ''.join(str(v) for v in inst.values())
  115 |         return (inst.get(subfield) or inst.get(subfield.lower())
  116 |                 or inst.get(subfield.upper()) or '')
  117 |     # bare string instance: only the whole-field value exists
  118 |     return inst if (subfield is None or subfield == '') else ''
  119 | 
  120 | 
  121 | def _first_subfield(inst):
  122 |     """``v200^*`` — value of the first subfield of the first instance."""
  123 |     if isinstance(inst, dict):
  124 |         for v in inst.values():
  125 |             if v:
  126 |                 return v
  127 |         return ''
  128 |     return inst or ''
  129 | 
  130 | 
  131 | def field_value(record, tag, subfield=None):
  132 |     """First non-empty instance's subfield value (or '')."""
  133 |     for inst in _instances(record, tag):
  134 |         v = _inst_value(inst, subfield)
```

```py
  141 | def field_values(record, tag, subfield=None):

  151 | def present(record, tag, subfield=None):
  152 |     """``p(...)`` — at least one non-empty instance of the field/subfield."""

  155 | 
  156 | def _fragment(value, off, length):
  157 |     """``v…*off.len`` — substring (0-based offset, given length)."""
  158 |     if value is None:
  159 |         return ''
  160 |     s = str(value)
  161 |     if off is None:
  162 |         return s
  163 |     if off >= len(s):
  164 |         return ''
  165 |     return s[off:off + length] if length is not None else s[off:]
  166 | 
  167 | 
  168 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  169 | # Check digits (ISBN-10/13 + ISSN) — UNIFOR 'C', reused by ФЛК A2 (SPEC §2.2).
  170 | # Self-contained: A1 owns the algorithm; A2 may delegate via &unifor('C'…).
  171 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  172 | def _clean_ident(raw):
  173 |     s = (raw or '').upper().replace('-', '').replace(' ', '')
  174 |     return ''.join(c for c in s if c.isdigit() or c == 'X')
  175 | 
  176 | 
  177 | def isbn_issn_ok(raw):
  178 |     """True iff ``raw`` is a structurally valid ISBN-10, ISBN-13 or ISSN
  179 |     (correct check digit). Empty / wrong length → False."""
  180 |     s = _clean_ident(raw)
  181 |     if len(s) == 10:
  182 |         total = 0
  183 |         for i, c in enumerate(s):
  184 |             if c == 'X':
  185 |                 if i != 9:
  186 |                     return False
  187 |                 d = 10
  188 |             elif c.isdigit():
  189 |                 d = ord(c) - 48
```

```py
  191 |                 return False

  194 |     if len(s) == 13:
  195 |         if not s.isdigit():
  196 |             return False
  197 |         total = sum((1 if i % 2 == 0 else 3) * (ord(c) - 48)
  198 |                     for i, c in enumerate(s))
  199 |         return total % 10 == 0
  200 |     if len(s) == 8:                      # ISSN

  211 |             total += (8 - i) * d
  212 |         return total % 11 == 0

  215 | 
  216 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  217 | # UNIFOR registry. Each handler: fn(arg:str, value:str, ctx, record) -> str.
  218 | # ``arg`` is the code-stripped remainder; ``value`` is the already-evaluated
  219 | # inline value (the part after the code literal, if the call embeds a vNNN).
  220 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  221 | UNIFOR = {}
  222 | 
  223 | 
  224 | def register_unifor(code, fn):
  225 |     """Register/override a UNIFOR handler keyed by its leading code char(s)."""
  226 |     UNIFOR[code] = fn
  227 | 
  228 | 
  229 | _MONTHS_NOM = ['', 'январь', 'февраль', 'март', 'апрель', 'май', 'июнь',
  230 |                'июль', 'август', 'сентябрь', 'октябрь', 'ноябрь', 'декабрь']
  231 | # Genitive (родительный падеж) — '36MM' vs '37MM' (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1 code 3).
  232 | _MONTHS_GEN = ['', 'января', 'февраля', 'марта', 'апреля', 'мая', 'июня',
  233 |                'июля', 'августа', 'сентября', 'октября', 'ноября', 'декабря']
  234 | _MONTHS_ENG = ['', 'January', 'February', 'March', 'April', 'May', 'June',
  235 |                'July', 'August', 'September', 'October', 'November', 'December']
  236 | 
  237 | 
  238 | def _now(ctx):
  239 |     """The 'today' used by date codes — overridable via ctx['now'] for tests."""
  240 |     if ctx and ctx.get('now') is not None:
  241 |         return ctx['now']
  242 |     return _dt.datetime.now()
  243 | 
  244 |
```

```py
  245 | def _parse_yyyymmdd(s):

  258 | def _uf_date(arg, value, ctx, record):
  259 |     """UNIFOR ``3`` — date/time (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1 / decompiled case ``0x33``).
  260 | 
  261 |     Today's date is ``ctx['now']`` (else ``datetime.now()``). Sub-codes
  262 |     (the ``3`` is already stripped by the dispatcher):
  263 | 
  264 |       * (empty) ГГГГММДД · ``0`` year · ``1`` month(zero-pad) · ``2`` day(pad)
  265 |         · ``3`` ГГ (YY) · ``4`` month no-lead-zero · ``5`` day no-lead-zero
  266 |         · ``9`` HHMMSS — current-clock fields.
  267 |       * ``6MM`` month name nominative · ``7MM`` genitive · ``8MM`` English
  268 |         (``MM`` = the 2-digit month, e.g. ``&unifor('36',&unifor('31'))``).
  269 |       * ``A`` day-of-year of the supplied/inline ``ГГГГММДД`` (decompiled 3A).
  270 |       * ``B<date>,<±days>`` shift a date by N days → ГГГГММДД (decompiled 3B).
  271 |       * ``C<date1>,<date2>`` whole-day difference ``date1 - date2`` (3C; ``0``
  272 |         if either side unparsable, matching the C default).
  273 |     """
  274 |     now = _now(ctx)
  275 |     sub = arg  # arg already had the leading '3' stripped by the dispatcher
  276 |     if sub == '' or sub is None:
  277 |         return now.strftime('%Y%m%d')
  278 |     if sub == '0':
  279 |         return now.strftime('%Y')
  280 |     if sub == '1':
  281 |         return now.strftime('%m')
  282 |     if sub == '2':
  283 |         return now.strftime('%d')
  284 |     if sub == '3':
  285 |         return now.strftime('%y')
  286 |     if sub == '4':
  287 |         return str(now.month)
  288 |     if sub == '5':
  289 |         return str(now.day)
  290 |     if sub == '9':
  291 |         return now.strftime('%H%M%S')
  292 |     # month-name codes 6MM/7MM/8MM — the MM is taken from the trailing two
  293 |     # digits (or, if absent, from the inline value, else today's month).
  294 |     if sub[0] in ('6', '7', '8'):
  295 |         table = {'6': _MONTHS_NOM, '7': _MONTHS_GEN, '8': _MONTHS_ENG}[sub[0]]
  296 |         mm = sub[1:3] if len(sub) >= 3 and sub[1:3].isdigit() else ''
  297 |         if not mm:
  298 |             mm = (value or '')[:2]
  299 |         try:
```

```py
  300 |             month = int(mm) if mm else now.month

  304 |     # The date payload for A/B/C may be inline in the spec (e.g.
  305 |     # &unifor('3C20260621,20260601')) or in the evaluated value
  306 |     # (&unifor('3C'v210^d)); prefer the in-spec tail, fall back to the value.

  314 |     # 3B<date>,<±days> — shift a date by N days → ГГГГММДД.
  315 |     if sub[0] == 'B':
  316 |         base_s, _, off_s = payload.partition(',')
  317 |         base = _parse_yyyymmdd(base_s) or today
  318 |         try:
  319 |             days = int(off_s) if off_s.strip() else 0
  320 |         except ValueError:
  321 |             days = 0
  322 |         return (base + _dt.timedelta(days=days)).strftime('%Y%m%d')
  323 |     # 3C<date1>,<date2> — whole-day difference date1-date2 (0 if unparsable).
  324 |     if sub[0] == 'C':
  325 |         a_s, _, b_s = payload.partition(',')
  326 |         da, db = _parse_yyyymmdd(a_s), _parse_yyyymmdd(b_s)
  327 |         if da is None or db is None:
  328 |             return '0'
  329 |         return str((da - db).days)
  330 |     return now.strftime('%Y%m%d')

  333 | def _uf_checksum(arg, value, ctx, record):
  334 |     """UNIFOR ``C`` — ISSN/ISBN control: '0' valid, '1' error (SPEC §9.1).
  335 |     The value to check is the inline ``value`` (e.g. ``&unifor('C'v10^a)``)."""

  337 |     return '0' if isbn_issn_ok(target) else '1'
  338 | 
  339 | 
  340 | def _uf_occurrence(arg, value, ctx, record):
  341 |     """UNIFOR ``A``/``P`` — specified field occurrence: ``Av<tag>^<sub>#<occ>``
  342 |     (SPEC §9.1, e.g. ``&unifor('Av920#1')``). 1-based occurrence."""
  343 |     m = re.match(r'v(\d+)(?:\^(.))?(?:\*(\d+)(?:\.(\d+))?)?(?:#(\d+))?$', arg, re.I)
  344 |     if not m:
  345 |         return ''
  346 |     tag, sub, off, length, occ = m.groups()
  347 |     insts = _instances(record, tag)
  348 |     idx = (int(occ) - 1) if occ else 0
  349 |     if idx < 0 or idx >= len(insts):
  350 |         return ''
  351 |     v = _first_subfield(insts[idx]) if sub == '*' else _inst_value(insts[idx], sub)
  352 |     if off is not None:
  353 |         v = _fragment(v, int(off), int(length) if length is not None else None)
  354 |     return v or ''
```

```py
  416 | def _uf_substr_marker(arg, value, ctx, record):
  417 |     """UNIFOR ``G`` — substring relative to a marker char: ``GNA<string>``
  418 |     (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1 / decompiled case ``0x33``-sibling).
  419 | 
  420 |     ``N`` = 0 → take the part *up to* (excluding) the marker; ``N`` = 1 → the
  421 |     part *from* (including) the marker. ``A`` is the marker character, with two
  422 |     wildcards: ``#`` = the first digit, ``$`` = the first (latin/cyrillic)
  423 |     letter. Marker not found → the whole string (mode 0) / empty (mode 1)."""
  424 |     m = re.match(r'([01])(.)(.*)$', arg, re.S)
  425 |     if not m:
  426 |         return value or arg or ''
  427 |     mode, marker, tail = m.group(1), m.group(2), m.group(3)
  428 |     src = value if value else tail
  429 |     src = src or ''
  430 |     if marker == '#':
  431 |         pos = next((i for i, c in enumerate(src) if c.isdigit()), -1)
  432 |     elif marker == '$':
  433 |         pos = next((i for i, c in enumerate(src) if c.isalpha()), -1)
  434 |     else:
  435 |         pos = src.find(marker)
  436 |     if pos < 0:
  437 |         return src if mode == '0' else ''
  438 |     return src[:pos] if mode == '0' else src[pos:]
  439 | 
  440 | 
  441 | # Cyrillic→Latin transliteration tables (UNIFOR ``T``). Table 0 is a practical
  442 | # GOST-7.79-ish System B mapping; table 1 a simplified ASCII variant. Both are
  443 | # applied longest-key-first so digraphs (``щ``→``shh``) win over single letters.
  444 | _TRANSLIT_0 = {
  445 |     'а': 'a', 'б': 'b', 'в': 'v', 'г': 'g', 'д': 'd', 'е': 'e', 'ё': 'yo',
  446 |     'ж': 'zh', 'з': 'z', 'и': 'i', 'й': 'j', 'к': 'k', 'л': 'l', 'м': 'm',
  447 |     'н': 'n', 'о': 'o', 'п': 'p', 'р': 'r', 'с': 's', 'т': 't', 'у': 'u',
  448 |     'ф': 'f', 'х': 'x', 'ц': 'cz', 'ч': 'ch', 'ш': 'sh', 'щ': 'shh',
  449 |     'ъ': '"', 'ы': 'y', 'ь': "'", 'э': 'e', 'ю': 'yu', 'я': 'ya',
  450 | }
  451 | _TRANSLIT_1 = dict(_TRANSLIT_0, **{
  452 |     'й': 'i', 'х': 'kh', 'ц': 'ts', 'ъ': '', 'ь': '', 'э': 'eh',
  453 | })
  454 | 
  455 |

  458 |     for ch in src:

  460 |         rep = table.get(low)
  461 |         if rep is None:
  462 |             out.append(ch)
  463 |         elif ch == low:
  464 |             out.append(rep)
```

```py
  470 | def _uf_translit(arg, value, ctx, record):
  471 |     """UNIFOR ``T`` — transliterate Cyrillic to Latin: ``TN<string>`` where
  472 |     ``N`` selects the table (``0`` default, ``1`` simplified) (PFT_LANGUAGE
  473 |     §9.1). Non-Cyrillic characters pass through unchanged."""

  478 |     return _translit(src, table)

  481 | # Insertion-point markers ``%…%`` that the term-ending code ``L`` removes.
  482 | _ENDMARK_RE = re.compile(r'%([^%]*)%')
  483 | 
  484 | 
  485 | def _uf_term_ending(arg, value, ctx, record):
  486 |     """UNIFOR ``L`` — term ending: ``L<term>`` (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1 / decompiled
  487 |     case ``0x4c``). The dictionary/morphology lookup needs the inverted file,
  488 |     which this slice does not carry, so we degrade to the documented *marker
  489 |     pass*: the real code strips the ``%…%`` insertion-point markers from the
  490 |     computed ending and concatenates the segments. With no index we apply that
  491 |     same marker-stripping to the supplied term so a template using ``L`` still
  492 |     renders clean text rather than echoing the ``%`` markers."""
  493 |     src = value if value else (arg or '')
  494 |     return _ENDMARK_RE.sub(r'\1', src or '')
  495 | 
  496 | 
  497 | def _uf_full_record(arg, value, ctx, record):
  498 |     """UNIFOR ``0`` — the whole document (format ALL): every field flattened to
  499 |     ``<tag>#<value>`` lines (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1 / decompiled case ``0x30``).
  500 | 
  501 |     A plain readable dump of the record; repeating instances are each emitted
  502 |     on their own line. Useful for debug/diagnostic templates."""
  503 |     if not record:
  504 |         return ''
  505 |     lines = []
  506 |     for tag in record:
  507 |         for inst in _instances(record, tag):
  508 |             lines.append('%s#%s' % (tag, _inst_value(inst, None)))
  509 |     return '\n'.join(lines)
  510 |

  512 | def _uf_record_status(arg, value, ctx, record):

  514 |     deleted, ``0`` if it is (PFT_LANGUAGE §9.1 / decompiled case ``0x2b``→
  515 |     ``0x36`` via ``IrbisIsDeleted``). The flag is read from ``ctx['deleted']``
  516 |     (the A1↔store contract); absent ⇒ treated as live (``1``)."""
  517 |     deleted = bool((ctx or {}).get('deleted'))
  518 |     return '0' if deleted else '1'
  519 |
```

```py
  520 | 
  521 | def _uf_urlcode(arg, value, ctx, record):
  522 |     """UNIFOR ``+3E`` / ``+3D`` — URL-encode / -decode a string (PFT_LANGUAGE
  523 |     §9.1). The leading ``E``/``D`` selects the direction; payload is the inline
  524 |     value (or the spec tail)."""
  525 |     direction = arg[:1].upper()
  526 |     src = value if value else arg[1:]
  527 |     src = src or ''
  528 |     import urllib.parse as _url
  529 |     if direction == 'D':
  530 |         return _url.unquote(src)
  531 |     # default to encode (E); quote keeps unreserved chars, encodes the rest.
  532 |     return _url.quote(src, safe='')
  533 | 
  534 | 
  535 | def _uf_postedit_seps(arg, value, ctx, record):
  536 |     """UNIFOR ``!`` — post-edit: collapse double subfield separators / spaces
  537 |     (SPEC §9.1 — a whole-output pass; here a no-op marker returning '')."""
  538 |     return ''

  541 | def _uf_postedit_rtf(arg, value, ctx, record):

  543 |     return ''

  546 | def _uf_module(arg, value, ctx, record):
  547 |     """UNIFOR ``6`` — nested/template format ``6<name>`` or ``6<tmpl>#<p1>,...``
  548 |     (SPEC §9.1/§9.2). Resolves the module body from ``ctx['modules']`` (a
  549 |     ``{name: pft_body}`` map), substitutes ``%N`` params, and recursively
  550 |     evaluates. Unknown module → '' (carried, not a crash)."""
  551 |     name = arg
  552 |     params = []
  553 |     if '#' in arg:
  554 |         name, raw = arg.split('#', 1)
  555 |         params = raw.split(',')
  556 |     modules = (ctx or {}).get('modules') or {}
  557 |     body = modules.get(name)
  558 |     if body is None:
  559 |         return ''
  560 |     # %1..%9 parameter substitution (SPEC §9.2 template formats).
  561 |     for i, p in enumerate(params, start=1):
  562 |         body = body.replace('%%%d' % i, p)
  563 |     return eval(body, record, ctx)       # noqa: recursion is intentional
  564 | 
  565 | 
  566 | # Default registry (longest code key first so '+F' beats 'F', etc.).
  567 | register_unifor('3', _uf_date)
  568 | register_unifor('C', _uf_checksum)
  569 | register_unifor('A', _uf_occurrence)
  570 | register_unifor('P', _uf_occurrence)
  571 | register_unifor('9', _uf_strip_quotes)
  572 | register_unifor('Q', _uf_lower)
  573 | register_unifor('X', _uf_strip_angle)
  574 | register_unifor('E', _uf_first_words)
```

```py
  575 | register_unifor('F', _uf_cut_after_words)
  576 | register_unifor('G', _uf_substr_marker)
  577 | register_unifor('T', _uf_translit)
  578 | register_unifor('L', _uf_term_ending)
  579 | register_unifor('0', _uf_full_record)

  586 | 
  587 | def _dispatch_unifor(spec, value, ctx, record):
  588 |     """Route a parsed UNIFOR call to its handler. ``spec`` is the leading-literal
  589 |     argument (code + remainder); ``value`` is the evaluated inline value, if any.
  590 |     Unknown code → '' (SPEC §15: diagnosable, never a silent crash)."""
  591 |     if spec is None:
  592 |         spec = ''
  593 |     # multi-char codes first
  594 |     for code in ('+F', '+I', '+1', '+7', '+3', '+9', '+5', '+4', '+6', '+0'):
  595 |         if spec.startswith(code):
  596 |             fn = UNIFOR.get(code)
  597 |             return fn(spec[len(code):], value, ctx, record) if fn else ''
  598 |     if not spec:
  599 |         return ''
  600 |     code = spec[0]
  601 |     fn = UNIFOR.get(code)
  602 |     if fn is None:
  603 |         return ''                         # degrade gracefully (Format error 99)
  604 |     return fn(spec[1:], value, ctx, record)
  605 | 
  606 | 
  607 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  608 | # Tokenizer. PFT is whitespace-insensitive between commands; literals carry their
  609 | # own delimiters. We emit a flat token stream, then a recursive-descent parser
  610 | # builds the AST. Comments ``/* … EOL`` are stripped first.
  611 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  612 | _COMMENT = re.compile(r'/\*[^\n]*')
  613 | 
  614 | # token kinds
  615 | T_FIELD, T_DUMMY, T_LIT, T_NUM, T_IDENT, T_PUNCT, T_UNIFOR = (
  616 |     'FIELD', 'DUMMY', 'LIT', 'NUM', 'IDENT', 'PUNCT', 'UNIFOR')
  617 | 
  618 | _KEYWORDS = {'if', 'then', 'else', 'fi', 'and', 'or', 'not', 'val', 'f', 's',
  619 |              'p', 'a', 'ref', 'mfn', 'rsum', 'rmin', 'rmax', 'ravr', 'l'}
  620 | 
  621 | 
  622 | class _Tok:
  623 |     __slots__ = ('kind', 'value', 'extra')
  624 | 
  625 |     def __init__(self, kind, value, extra=None):
  626 |         self.kind = kind
  627 |         self.value = value
  628 |         self.extra = extra
  629 | 
```

<!-- ─── страница 192 ─── -->

```py
  630 |     def __repr__(self):
  631 |         return '<%s %r>' % (self.kind, self.value)
  632 | 
  633 | 
  634 | def _tokenize(src):
  635 |     src = _COMMENT.sub('', src)
  636 |     toks = []
  637 |     i, n = 0, len(src)
  638 |     while i < n:
  639 |         c = src[i]
  640 |         if c in ' \t\r\n':
  641 |             i += 1
  642 |             continue
  643 |         # literals
  644 |         if c == '"':
  645 |             j = src.find('"', i + 1)
  646 |             if j < 0:
  647 |                 j = n
  648 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_LIT, src[i + 1:j], 'cond'))
  649 |             i = j + 1
  650 |             continue
  651 |         if c == "'":
  652 |             j = src.find("'", i + 1)
  653 |             if j < 0:
  654 |                 j = n
  655 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_LIT, src[i + 1:j], 'uncond'))
  656 |             i = j + 1
  657 |             continue
  658 |         if c == '|':
  659 |             j = src.find('|', i + 1)
  660 |             if j < 0:
  661 |                 j = n
  662 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_LIT, src[i + 1:j], 'rep'))
  663 |             i = j + 1
  664 |             continue
  665 |         # UNIFOR call: &name(arg) or &uf('...') — keep the raw call, parse later
  666 |         if c == '&':
  667 |             j, depth = i + 1, 0
  668 |             # read the name up to '('
  669 |             while j < n and src[j] != '(':
  670 |                 j += 1
  671 |             # now scan balanced parens
  672 |             k = j
  673 |             while k < n:
  674 |                 if src[k] == '(':
  675 |                     depth += 1
  676 |                 elif src[k] == ')':
  677 |                     depth -= 1
  678 |                     if depth == 0:
  679 |                         k += 1
  680 |                         break
  681 |                 k += 1
  682 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_UNIFOR, src[i:k]))
  683 |             i = k
  684 |             continue
```

<!-- ─── страница 193 ─── -->

```py
  685 |         # field reference  v<tag>[^sub|*][*off.len]
  686 |         if c in 'vV':
  687 |             m = re.match(r'[vV](\d+)(\^[\*a-zA-Z0-9])?(\*\d+(?:\.\d+)?)?',
  688 |                          src[i:])
  689 |             if m and m.group(1):
  690 |                 toks.append(_Tok(T_FIELD, m.group(0)))
  691 |                 i += m.end()
  692 |                 continue
  693 |         # dummy output d<tag> / n<tag>
  694 |         if c in 'dDnN':
  695 |             m = re.match(r'[dDnN](\d+)(\^[a-zA-Z0-9])?', src[i:])
  696 |             if m:
  697 |                 toks.append(_Tok(T_DUMMY, m.group(0)))
  698 |                 i += m.end()
  699 |                 continue
  700 |         # number
  701 |         if c.isdigit():
  702 |             m = re.match(r'\d+(?:\.\d+)?', src[i:])
  703 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_NUM, m.group(0)))
  704 |             i += m.end()
  705 |             continue
  706 |         # identifier / keyword
  707 |         if c.isalpha():
  708 |             m = re.match(r'[A-Za-z]+', src[i:])
  709 |             word = m.group(0)
  710 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_IDENT, word))
  711 |             i += m.end()
  712 |             continue
  713 |         # comparison operators (two-char first)
  714 |         two = src[i:i + 2]
  715 |         if two in ('<>', '<=', '>='):
  716 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_PUNCT, two))
  717 |             i += 2
  718 |             continue
  719 |         if c in '(),+=<>:/#%':
  720 |             toks.append(_Tok(T_PUNCT, c))
  721 |             i += 1
  722 |             continue
  723 |         # mode commands Mmc, placement Xn/Cn, '*' etc — skipped (no-op in text IR)
  724 |         i += 1
  725 |     return toks
  726 | 
  727 | 
  728 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  729 | # AST nodes. Each ``render(record, ctx, state)`` returns a string. ``state`` is
  730 | # the per-render mutable workspace (current repeat-instance index, output flags).
  731 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  732 | class _Node:
  733 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  734 |         return ''
  735 | 
  736 | 
  737 | class _Seq(_Node):
  738 |     """A concatenation of nodes (',' / '+' / juxtaposition)."""
  739 | 
```

```py
  740 |     def __init__(self, parts):
  741 |         self.parts = parts
  742 | 
  743 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  744 |         return ''.join(p.render(record, ctx, state) for p in self.parts)

  747 | class _Literal(_Node):
  748 |     def __init__(self, text, kind):
  749 |         self.text = text
  750 |         self.kind = kind            # 'cond' | 'rep' | 'uncond'
  751 | 
  752 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  753 |         if self.kind == 'uncond':
  754 |             return self.text
  755 |         # cond/rep literals are gated by adjacent-field presence; the parser
  756 |         # attaches them to the field, so a standalone one falls back to: emit if
  757 |         # the current group instance exists (rep) or once (cond) — approximated
  758 |         # by the state's "last field emitted" flag.
  759 |         if state.get('emit_literal', True):
  760 |             return self.text
  761 |         return ''
  762 | 
  763 | 
  764 | class _Field(_Node):
  765 |     def __init__(self, tag, subfield, first_sub, off, length,
  766 |                  prefix=None, suffix=None):
  767 |         self.tag = tag
  768 |         self.subfield = subfield
  769 |         self.first_sub = first_sub
  770 |         self.off = off
  771 |         self.length = length
  772 |         self.prefix = prefix          # list[_Literal] before the field
  773 |         self.suffix = suffix          # list[_Literal] after the field
  774 | 
  775 |     def _one(self, inst, record, ctx, state):
  776 |         if self.first_sub:
  777 |             v = _first_subfield(inst)
  778 |         else:
  779 |             v = _inst_value(inst, self.subfield)
  780 |         if self.off is not None:
  781 |             v = _fragment(v, self.off, self.length)
  782 |         return v or ''
  783 | 
  784 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  785 |         insts = _instances(record, self.tag)
  786 |         # inside a repeat group, only the current instance is visible
  787 |         gi = state.get('group_index')
  788 |         if gi is not None and state.get('group_tag') == self.tag:
  789 |             insts = [insts[gi]] if 0 <= gi < len(insts) else []
  790 |         # only non-empty occurrences are emitted; literals key off emitted order
  791 |         emitted = [self._one(x, record, ctx, state) for x in insts]
  792 |         emitted = [v for v in emitted if v]
  793 |         present_any = bool(emitted)
  794 |         last = len(emitted) - 1
```

```py
  795 |         out = []
  796 |         cond_done = False                    # conditional literals emit once
  797 |         for k, v in enumerate(emitted):
  798 |             piece = ''
  799 |             for lit in (self.prefix or []):
  800 |                 if lit.kind == 'rep_plus':       # |x|+ : not before the first
  801 |                     if k > 0:
  802 |                         piece += lit.text
  803 |                 elif lit.kind == 'cond':         # "x" prefix: once, with the field
  804 |                     if not cond_done:
  805 |                         piece += lit.text
  806 |                 else:                            # 'x' uncond / |x| rep
  807 |                     piece += lit.text
  808 |             piece += v
  809 |             for lit in (self.suffix or []):
  810 |                 if lit.kind == 'plus_rep':       # +|x| : not after the last
  811 |                     if k < last:
  812 |                         piece += lit.text
  813 |                 elif lit.kind == 'cond':         # "x" suffix: once
  814 |                     if not cond_done:
  815 |                         piece += lit.text
  816 |                 else:                            # 'x' / |x| rep
  817 |                     piece += lit.text
  818 |             cond_done = True
  819 |             out.append(piece)
  820 |         # a conditional prefix literal must still print once even if the field has
  821 |         # exactly one (already handled); absent field => prefix/suffix suppressed.
  822 |         state['last_present'] = present_any
  823 |         return ''.join(out)

  826 | class _Dummy(_Node):
  827 |     """``d<tag>`` / ``n<tag>`` — emit attached conditional literals by presence."""
  828 | 
  829 |     def __init__(self, negate, tag, subfield, literals):
  830 |         self.negate = negate
  831 |         self.tag = tag
  832 |         self.subfield = subfield
  833 |         self.literals = literals     # the preceding conditional literals
  834 | 
  835 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  836 |         has = present(record, self.tag, self.subfield)
  837 |         fire = (not has) if self.negate else has
  838 |         if not fire:
  839 |             return ''
  840 |         return ''.join(l.text for l in self.literals)
```

```py
  855 | class _Unifor(_Node):
  856 |     def __init__(self, spec_node, value_node):
  857 |         self.spec_node = spec_node       # _Node producing the code+arg literal
  858 |         self.value_node = value_node     # _Node producing the inline value (opt)
  859 | 
  860 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  861 |         spec = self.spec_node.render(record, ctx, state) if self.spec_node else ''
  862 |         value = self.value_node.render(record, ctx, state) if self.value_node else ''
  863 |         try:
  864 |             return _dispatch_unifor(spec, value, ctx, record)
  865 |         except Exception:                # noqa: BLE001 — degrade, never crash
  866 |             return ''
  867 |

  869 | class _Func(_Node):
  870 |     """val / f / s / rsum / rmin / rmax / ravr — numeric / string functions."""
  871 | 
  872 |     def __init__(self, name, args):
  873 |         self.name = name
  874 |         self.args = args                 # list[_Node]
  875 | 
  876 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  877 |         name = self.name
  878 |         if name == 'val':
  879 |             # val() takes the FIRST numeric value of its formatted argument. For a
  880 |             # bare repeating field we read the first instance (so val(v999)==first
  881 |             # occurrence), else the whole formatted string (SPEC §8.1).
  882 |             arg = _unwrap_field(self.args[0]) if self.args else None
  883 |             if isinstance(arg, _Field):
  884 |                 txt = field_value(record, arg.tag, arg.subfield)
  885 |             else:
  886 |                 txt = self.args[0].render(record, ctx, state) if self.args else ''
  887 |             return _fmt_num(_to_num(txt))
  888 |         if name == 's':
  889 |             return ''.join(a.render(record, ctx, state) for a in self.args)
  890 |         if name == 'f':
  891 |             val = _to_num(self.args[0].render(record, ctx, state)) if self.args else 0
  892 |             width = int(_to_num(self.args[1].render(record, ctx, state))) \
  893 |                 if len(self.args) > 1 else 0
  894 |             dec = int(_to_num(self.args[2].render(record, ctx, state))) \
  895 |                 if len(self.args) > 2 else 0
  896 |             s = ('%.*f' % (dec, val)) if dec else str(int(val))
  897 |             return s.rjust(width) if width else s
  898 |         if name in ('rsum', 'rmin', 'rmax', 'ravr'):
  899 |             nums = []
  900 |             for a in self.args:
  901 |                 # a repeat-aware numeric scan over all instances it references
  902 |                 for piece in _iter_numeric(a, record, ctx, state):
  903 |                     nums.append(piece)
  904 |             if not nums:
```

```py
  915 | 
  916 | def _unwrap_field(node):
  917 |     """If ``node`` is a _Field (possibly wrapped in a single-element _Seq), return
  918 |     that _Field; else return the node unchanged."""
  919 |     if isinstance(node, _Field):
  920 |         return node
  921 |     if isinstance(node, _Seq) and len(node.parts) == 1:
  922 |         return _unwrap_field(node.parts[0])
  923 |     return node
  924 | 
  925 | 
  926 | def _iter_numeric(node, record, ctx, state):
  927 |     """Yield numeric values of a node across all repeat instances of any single
  928 |     field it references (rsum/rmin/... support). Falls back to one scalar."""
  929 |     inner = _unwrap_field(node)
  930 |     if isinstance(inner, _Field):
  931 |         for inst in _instances(record, inner.tag):
  932 |             v = inner._one(inst, record, ctx, state)
  933 |             if v:
  934 |                 yield _to_num(v)
  935 |         return
  936 |     txt = node.render(record, ctx, state)
  937 |     if txt:
  938 |         yield _to_num(txt)
  939 | 
  940 | 
  941 | class _Pred(_Node):
  942 |     """p(...) / a(...) used only inside IF conditions — returns '1' or ''."""
  943 | 
  944 |     def __init__(self, negate, field_node):
  945 |         self.negate = negate
  946 |         self.field_node = field_node
  947 | 
  948 |     def truth(self, record, ctx, state):
  949 |         if isinstance(self.field_node, _Field):
  950 |             has = present(record, self.field_node.tag, self.field_node.subfield)
  951 |         else:
  952 |             has = bool(self.field_node.render(record, ctx, state))
  953 |         return (not has) if self.negate else has
  954 | 
  955 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  956 |         return '1' if self.truth(record, ctx, state) else ''
  957 |
```

```py
  960 |     def __init__(self, left, op, right):
  961 |         self.left = left
  962 |         self.op = op
  963 |         self.right = right
  964 | 
  965 |     def truth(self, record, ctx, state):
  966 |         lv = self.left.render(record, ctx, state)
  967 |         rv = self.right.render(record, ctx, state)
  968 |         op = self.op
  969 |         if op == '=':
  970 |             return lv == rv
  971 |         if op == '<>':
  972 |             return lv != rv
  973 |         if op == ':':
  974 |             return rv.lower() in lv.lower()
  975 |         # numeric/lexicographic for < <= > >=
  976 |         ln, rn = _to_num(lv), _to_num(rv)
  977 |         if op == '<':
  978 |             return ln < rn
  979 |         if op == '<=':
  980 |             return ln <= rn
  981 |         if op == '>':
  982 |             return ln > rn
  983 |         if op == '>=':
  984 |             return ln >= rn
  985 |         return False
  986 | 
  987 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
  988 |         return '1' if self.truth(record, ctx, state) else ''
  989 |

  991 | class _BoolOp(_Node):
  992 |     def __init__(self, op, parts):
  993 |         self.op = op                    # 'and' | 'or'
  994 |         self.parts = parts
  995 | 
  996 |     def truth(self, record, ctx, state):
  997 |         vals = [_truth(p, record, ctx, state) for p in self.parts]
  998 |         return all(vals) if self.op == 'and' else any(vals)
  999 | 
 1000 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
 1001 |         return '1' if self.truth(record, ctx, state) else ''
 1002 |

 1004 | class _NotOp(_Node):

 1009 |         return not _truth(self.part, record, ctx, state)
 1010 | 
 1011 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
 1012 |         return '1' if self.truth(record, ctx, state) else ''
```

```py
 1022 | class _If(_Node):
 1023 |     def __init__(self, cond, then_node, else_node):
 1024 |         self.cond = cond
 1025 |         self.then_node = then_node
 1026 |         self.else_node = else_node
 1027 |

 1034 | class _Group(_Node):
 1035 |     """Repeat group ``(…)`` — evaluate the body once per instance of the (single)
 1036 |     repeating field referenced inside (SPEC §10). Emits joined; eval_lines splits
 1037 |     on the group boundary so multi-line group output is preserved."""
 1038 | 
 1039 |     def __init__(self, body, tags):
 1040 |         self.body = body
 1041 |         self.tags = tags                # field tags referenced in the group
 1042 | 
 1043 |     def _max_instances(self, record):
 1044 |         return max((len(_instances(record, t)) for t in self.tags), default=1)
 1045 | 
 1046 |     def render(self, record, ctx, state):
 1047 |         n = self._max_instances(record)
 1048 |         if n <= 1:
 1049 |             saved = state.get('group_index'), state.get('group_tag')
 1050 |             state['group_index'] = None
 1051 |             out = self.body.render(record, ctx, state)
 1052 |             state['group_index'], state['group_tag'] = saved
 1053 |             return out
 1054 |         lead = self.tags[0] if self.tags else None
 1055 |         out = []
 1056 |         saved_i, saved_t = state.get('group_index'), state.get('group_tag')
 1057 |         for k in range(n):
 1058 |             state['group_index'] = k
 1059 |             state['group_tag'] = lead
 1060 |             out.append(self.body.render(record, ctx, state))
 1061 |         state['group_index'], state['group_tag'] = saved_i, saved_t
 1062 |         return ''.join(out)
 1063 | 
 1064 |     def render_lines(self, record, ctx, state):
 1065 |         n = self._max_instances(record)
 1066 |         lead = self.tags[0] if self.tags else None
 1067 |         lines = []
 1068 |         saved_i, saved_t = state.get('group_index'), state.get('group_tag')
 1069 |         for k in range(max(n, 1)):
```

```py
 1071 |             state['group_tag'] = lead
 1072 |             lines.append(self.body.render(record, ctx, state))
 1073 |         state['group_index'], state['group_tag'] = saved_i, saved_t
 1074 |         return lines

 1076 | 
 1077 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 1078 | # numeric helpers
 1079 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 1080 | _NUM_RE = re.compile(r'-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?')
 1081 | 
 1082 | 
 1083 | def _to_num(text):
 1084 |     """``val()`` semantics — first number in the text, else 0."""
 1085 |     if isinstance(text, (int, float)):
 1086 |         return text
 1087 |     m = _NUM_RE.search(str(text or ''))
 1088 |     if not m:
 1089 |         return 0
 1090 |     s = m.group(0)
 1091 |     return float(s) if '.' in s else int(s)
 1092 | 
 1093 | 
 1094 | def _fmt_num(n):
 1095 |     if isinstance(n, float) and n.is_integer():
 1096 |         return str(int(n))
 1097 |     return str(n)
 1098 | 
 1099 | 
 1100 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 1101 | # Parser — recursive descent over the token stream.
 1102 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
 1103 | class _Parser:
 1104 |     def __init__(self, toks, strict=False):
 1105 |         self.toks = toks
 1106 |         self.pos = 0
 1107 |         self.strict = strict
 1108 | 
 1109 |     def _peek(self, k=0):
 1110 |         j = self.pos + k
 1111 |         return self.toks[j] if j < len(self.toks) else None
 1112 | 
 1113 |     def _next(self):
 1114 |         t = self._peek()
 1115 |         self.pos += 1
 1116 |         return t
 1117 | 
 1118 |     def _at_keyword(self, word):
 1119 |         t = self._peek()
 1120 |         return t is not None and t.kind == T_IDENT and t.value.lower() == word
 1121 | 
 1122 |     # ---- top: a sequence of terms separated by ',' / '+' / juxtaposition ---- #
 1123 |     def parse_sequence(self, stoppers=()):
 1124 |         parts = []
```

```py
 1126 |         while True:
 1127 |             t = self._peek()
 1128 |             if t is None:

 1131 |                 break

 1149 |     def parse_term(self, tags):
 1150 |         t = self._peek()
 1151 |         if t is None:
 1152 |             return None
 1153 |         # IF … FI
 1154 |         if t.kind == T_IDENT and t.value.lower() == 'if':
 1155 |             return self.parse_if(tags)
 1156 |         # repeat group
 1157 |         if t.kind == T_PUNCT and t.value == '(':
 1158 |             return self.parse_group(tags)
 1159 |         # field, with optional prefix/suffix literals
 1160 |         if t.kind == T_FIELD:
 1161 |             return self.parse_field(tags, prefix=None)
 1162 |         # leading conditional/repeat literal — may be a field prefix or dummy lit
 1163 |         if t.kind == T_LIT:
 1164 |             return self.parse_literal_led(tags)
 1165 |         if t.kind == T_DUMMY:
 1166 |             return self.parse_dummy(tags, leading=[])
 1167 |         if t.kind == T_UNIFOR:
 1168 |             self._next()
 1169 |             return self.parse_unifor(t.value)
 1170 |         if t.kind == T_IDENT:
 1171 |             return self.parse_ident(tags)
 1172 |         if t.kind == T_NUM:
 1173 |             self._next()
 1174 |             return _Literal(t.value, 'uncond')
 1175 |         if t.kind == T_PUNCT and t.value in ('/', '#', '%', '=', '<', '>', ':'):
 1176 |             self._next()                  # placement / stray operator -> no-op
 1177 |             return _Seq([])               # empty node (already advanced)
 1178 |         return None
 1179 |
```
