```py
  103 |     the originating row id).
  104 |     """
  105 | 
  106 |     __slots__ = ('id', 'event', 'recipient', 'subject', 'body', 'payload', 'tenant')
  107 | 
  108 |     def __init__(self, id, event, recipient, subject, body, payload, tenant='public'):
  109 |         self.id = id
  110 |         self.event = event
  111 |         self.recipient = recipient
  112 |         self.subject = subject
  113 |         self.body = body
  114 |         self.payload = payload
  115 |         self.tenant = tenant
  116 | 
  117 |     def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debug aid only
  118 |         return ('Notification(id=%r, event=%r, recipient=%r)'
  119 |                 % (self.id, self.event, self.recipient))
  120 | 
  121 | 
  122 | class DeliveryResult:
  123 |     """The outcome a channel returns from :meth:`Channel.deliver`.
  124 | 
  125 |     * ``ok``        — was the notice accepted by the surface/provider?
  126 |     * ``provider_id`` — opaque id the surface assigns (message-id), for the log.
  127 |     * ``error``     — failure detail when ``ok`` is False (recorded in the journal).
  128 | 
  129 |     A channel never *raises* to signal a miss on the dispatch path — it returns
  130 |     ``DeliveryResult.fail(...)`` so the dispatcher records the attempt and walks
  131 |     the fallback chain. (A raised exception is still caught defensively and turned
  132 |     into a failed result, so a buggy channel can't abort dispatch.)
  133 |     """
  134 | 
  135 |     __slots__ = ('ok', 'provider_id', 'error')
  136 | 
  137 |     def __init__(self, ok, provider_id=None, error=None):
  138 |         self.ok = ok
  139 |         self.provider_id = provider_id
  140 |         self.error = error
  141 | 
  142 |     @classmethod
  143 |     def sent(cls, provider_id=None):
  144 |         return cls(True, provider_id=provider_id)
  145 | 
  146 |     @classmethod
  147 |     def fail(cls, error):
  148 |         return cls(False, error=str(error))
  149 | 
  150 |     def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debug aid only
  151 |         return ('DeliveryResult(ok=%r, provider_id=%r, error=%r)'
  152 |                 % (self.ok, self.provider_id, self.error))
  153 | 
  154 | 
  155 | class Channel:
  156 |     """A delivery surface (email / sms / push / in-app / …).
  157 | 
```

<!-- ─── страница 237 ─── -->

```py
  158 |     Two contracts coexist for back-compat:
  159 | 
  160 |       * :meth:`send(recipient, subject, body, payload)` — the original
  161 |         first-success API used by :meth:`NotificationQueue.process_once`; returns a
  162 |         provider message-id on success and raises :class:`SendError` on failure.
  163 |       * :meth:`deliver(notification) -> DeliveryResult` — the dispatch contract
  164 |         (SPEC §1.1): takes the whole :class:`Notification` and returns a
  165 |         :class:`DeliveryResult` instead of raising. The base class implements
  166 |         ``deliver`` on top of ``send`` so existing channels (which only define
  167 |         ``send``) work unchanged under the new dispatcher; a channel that needs the
  168 |         full notice (in-app inbox) overrides ``deliver`` directly.
  169 |     """
  170 |     name = 'abstract'
  171 | 
  172 |     def send(self, recipient, subject, body, payload):  # pragma: no cover
  173 |         raise NotImplementedError
  174 | 
  175 |     def deliver(self, notification):
  176 |         """Default adapter: drive the legacy ``send`` and wrap the outcome.
  177 | 
  178 |         Lets every pre-existing :class:`Channel` (which implements only ``send``)
  179 |         participate in :meth:`NotificationQueue.dispatch` without change.
  180 |         """
  181 |         try:
  182 |             provider_id = self.send(notification.recipient, notification.subject,
  183 |                                     notification.body, notification.payload)
  184 |             return DeliveryResult.sent(provider_id=provider_id)
  185 |         except SendError as e:
  186 |             return DeliveryResult.fail(e)
  187 |         except Exception as e:  # defensive: any channel bug is a miss, not a crash
  188 |             return DeliveryResult.fail('%s: %s' % (type(e).__name__, e))
  189 | 
  190 | 
  191 | class MemoryChannel(Channel):
  192 |     """In-memory recorder for tests. Captures every send; can be forced to fail.
  193 | 
  194 |     ``fail`` -> always raises (exercise the fallback chain).
  195 |     ``fail_times`` -> raises for the first N attempts then succeeds (exercise the
  196 |     retry/attempts counter).
  197 |     """
  198 | 
  199 |     def __init__(self, name='memory', fail=False, fail_times=0):
  200 |         self.name = name
  201 |         self.fail = fail
  202 |         self.fail_times = fail_times
  203 |         self.sent = []          # list of dicts actually delivered
  204 |         self.attempts = 0       # total send() invocations (incl. failures)
  205 | 
  206 |     def send(self, recipient, subject, body, payload):
  207 |         self.attempts += 1
  208 |         if self.fail:
  209 |             raise SendError('%s: forced failure' % self.name)
  210 |         if self.fail_times > 0:
  211 |             self.fail_times -= 1
  212 |             raise SendError('%s: transient failure' % self.name)
```

```py
  216 |         return '%s-%d' % (self.name, len(self.sent))

  219 | class EmailChannel(Channel):
  220 |     """SMTP stub — NO real network send in this slice.
  221 | 
  222 |     ``available=False`` makes it raise (simulating an unconfigured gateway), which
  223 |     is how the fallback chain is exercised in prod-shaped wiring. When available it
  224 |     records to ``outbox`` exactly like a real adapter would hand off to a relay.
  225 |     """
  226 |     name = 'email'
  227 | 
  228 |     def __init__(self, available=True):
  229 |         self.available = available
  230 |         self.outbox = []
  231 | 
  232 |     def send(self, recipient, subject, body, payload):
  233 |         if not self.available:
  234 |             raise SendError('email: gateway not configured')
  235 |         self.outbox.append({'to': recipient, 'subject': subject, 'body': body})
  236 |         return 'email-stub-%d' % len(self.outbox)
  237 | 
  238 | 
  239 | class SmsChannel(Channel):
  240 |     """SMS gateway stub — NO real send. Mirrors :class:`EmailChannel`."""

  242 | 
  243 |     def __init__(self, available=True):
  244 |         self.available = available
  245 |         self.outbox = []
  246 | 
  247 |     def send(self, recipient, subject, body, payload):
  248 |         if not self.available:
  249 |             raise SendError('sms: gateway not configured')
  250 |         self.outbox.append({'to': recipient, 'text': body})
  251 |         return 'sms-stub-%d' % len(self.outbox)

  253 | 
  254 | class InAppChannel(Channel):
  255 |     """In-app inbox — the terminal, always-available fallback (SPEC §1.3).
  256 | 
  257 |     Unlike email/SMS it needs no external contact or consent, so it is the last
  258 |     link of every fallback chain and can never be "unavailable". It overrides
  259 |     :meth:`deliver` directly (it wants the whole :class:`Notification`, not just a
  260 |     body) and persists the rendered card straight onto the queue row by stamping
  261 |     ``read_at=NULL`` semantics — the notice is *already* a queue row, so "delivery
  262 |     to the inbox" is simply marking the row as inbox-visible (status ``sent`` via
  263 |     this channel) and the existing :meth:`NotificationQueue.inbox` view renders it.
  264 | 
  265 |     The reader/staff inbox surface (#222) reads the same rows; this channel is what
  266 |     makes a notice land there as a guaranteed delivery when every external channel
  267 |     has missed. It records what it accepted in ``delivered`` for tests.
```

```py
  274 |     def deliver(self, notification):
  275 |         # In-app is durable + local: accepting the notice is infallible. The row
  276 |         # itself is the inbox entry; the dispatcher marks it 'sent' via 'inapp' and
  277 |         # inbox()/unread_count() surface it to the reader or staff.

  279 |                                'event': notification.event, 'subject': notification.subject,
  280 |                                'body': notification.body})

  285 |         self.delivered.append({'recipient': recipient, 'subject': subject, 'body': body})
  286 |         return 'inapp-%d' % len(self.delivered)

  292 | class EventCatalog:
  293 |     """Maps reader-account events to their template + default channel chain.

  295 |     Seeded with the SPEC event set: the reader-facing hold/loan/fine notices

  297 |     account_blocked / renewal_confirmed / fine_paid / lost_confirmed) plus the
  298 |     staff-facing ``staff_alert`` (write-off candidate). The set must cover every
  299 |     event ``CirculationEngine._emit`` raises, or that intent falls through
  300 |     silently (no template → never rendered/sent). ``register`` lets a tenant
  301 |     add/override an entry. Each entry is ``{'template': {'subject','body'},
  302 |     'default_channels': [<name>, ...]}`` — channel *names*, resolved to live
  303 |     :class:`Channel` objects at send time.

  322 |         },
```

```py
  394 |     def __init__(self, entries=None):
  395 |         # deep-ish copy so a register() never mutates the class-level DEFAULTS
  396 |         src = entries if entries is not None else self.DEFAULTS
  397 |         self._entries = {ev: dict(spec) for ev, spec in src.items()}
  398 | 
  399 |     def register(self, event, template, default_channels):
  400 |         self._entries[event] = {'template': dict(template),
  401 |                                  'default_channels': list(default_channels)}
  402 | 
  403 |     def has(self, event):
  404 |         return event in self._entries
  405 | 
  406 |     def template(self, event):
  407 |         return self._entries[event]['template']
  408 | 
  409 |     def default_channels(self, event):
  410 |         return list(self._entries[event]['default_channels'])
  411 | 
  412 |     def events(self):
  413 |         return sorted(self._entries)

  416 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  417 | # Reader preferences — channel order per event + opt-out.
  418 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  419 | class Preferences:
  420 |     """Per-reader channel preferences and opt-outs.
  421 | 
  422 |     * ``channels`` — optional ``{event: [channel_name, ...]}`` overriding the
  423 |       catalog default for that event (and the global default below).
  424 |     * ``default_channels`` — optional reader-wide channel order applied to events
  425 |       with no per-event entry.
  426 |     * ``opted_out`` — a set of events the reader refuses; ``'*'`` opts out of
  427 |       everything. ``is_opted_out(event)`` honours both.
  428 | 
  429 |     ``resolve(event, catalog)`` returns the effective channel-name chain:
  430 |     per-event pref → reader default → catalog default.
  431 |     """
  432 | 
```

<!-- ─── страница 242 ─── -->

```py
  433 |     def __init__(self, channels=None, default_channels=None, opted_out=None):
  434 |         self.channels = dict(channels or {})
  435 |         self.default_channels = list(default_channels) if default_channels else None
  436 |         self.opted_out = set(opted_out or ())
  437 | 
  438 |     def is_opted_out(self, event):
  439 |         return '*' in self.opted_out or event in self.opted_out
  440 | 
  441 |     def resolve(self, event, catalog):
  442 |         if event in self.channels:
  443 |             return list(self.channels[event])
  444 |         if self.default_channels:
  445 |             return list(self.default_channels)
  446 |         return catalog.default_channels(event)
  447 | 
  448 | 
  449 | # Convenience: a reader who takes everything on the catalog defaults.
  450 | DEFAULT_PREFERENCES = Preferences()

  453 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  454 | # Queue — durable sqlite store with idempotent enqueue + claim/process.
  455 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  456 | SCHEMA_SQLITE = """
  457 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification (
  458 |   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  459 |   tenant TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'public',
  460 |   event TEXT NOT NULL,
  461 |   recipient TEXT NOT NULL,
  462 |   channel TEXT,                 -- channel that finally delivered (NULL until sent)
  463 |   status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
  464 |          CHECK (status IN ('pending','sent','failed','suppressed')),
  465 |   dedup_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  466 |   attempts INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  467 |   payload_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  468 |   channels_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',  -- resolved channel-name chain
  469 |   last_error TEXT,
  470 |   next_attempt_at REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  471 |   read_at REAL,                 -- reader inbox: NULL=unread, epoch=when marked read (#222)
  472 |   created_at REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')),
  473 |   updated_at REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')),
  474 |   UNIQUE (dedup_key)
  475 | );
  476 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS notification_claim_idx
  477 |   ON notification(status, next_attempt_at);
  478 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS notification_inbox_idx
  479 |   ON notification(recipient, read_at);
  480 | 
  481 | -- Delivery journal (SPEC §5.4 `delivery_attempt`): one row per channel attempt of
  482 | -- a notification, for "did it arrive?" forensics and per-channel idempotency. The
  483 | -- pair (notification_id, channel) is UNIQUE so a retry never logs/sends a second
  484 | -- delivery on a channel that already accepted — channel-level idempotency on top of
  485 | -- the event-level dedup on notification(dedup_key).
  486 | CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS delivery_attempt (
  487 |   id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
```

<!-- ─── страница 243 ─── -->

```py
  488 |   notification_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  489 |   channel TEXT NOT NULL,
  490 |   status TEXT NOT NULL                          -- 'sent' | 'failed'
  491 |          CHECK (status IN ('sent','failed')),
  492 |   provider_id TEXT,
  493 |   error TEXT,
  494 |   tries INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,             -- how many times this channel was hit
  495 |   created_at REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')),
  496 |   updated_at REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s','now')),
  497 |   UNIQUE (notification_id, channel)
  498 | );
  499 | CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS delivery_attempt_notif_idx
  500 |   ON delivery_attempt(notification_id);
  501 | """
  502 | 
  503 | # attempts -> seconds of advisory backoff before the row is reclaimable.
  504 | _BACKOFF = [0, 60, 300, 900, 3600]
  505 | MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5
  506 | 
  507 | 
  508 | def backoff_seconds(attempts):
  509 |     """Advisory backoff for the Nth attempt (capped at the last bucket)."""
  510 |     if attempts < 0:
  511 |         attempts = 0
  512 |     return _BACKOFF[min(attempts, len(_BACKOFF) - 1)]
  513 | 
  514 | 
  515 | def make_dedup_key(tenant, event, recipient, payload):
  516 |     """Deterministic idempotency key.
  517 | 
  518 |     A caller may pass an explicit ``dedup_key`` in the payload (e.g. a circulation
  519 |     transaction id) — that wins. Otherwise we derive a stable key from the logical
  520 |     identity of the event so the same hold/loan can't notify twice. ``ref`` (a
  521 |     domain id such as the hold/loan mfn) is the canonical disambiguator.
  522 |     """
  523 |     if payload and payload.get('dedup_key'):
  524 |         return str(payload['dedup_key'])
  525 |     ref = '' if not payload else str(payload.get('ref', ''))
  526 |     return '|'.join([tenant, event, str(recipient), ref])
  527 | 
  528 | 
  529 | class NotificationQueue:
  530 |     """sqlite-backed notification work queue (idempotent enqueue, claim/send).
  531 | 
  532 |     ``enqueue`` applies preferences/opt-out and inserts a single ``pending`` row
  533 |     keyed by ``dedup_key`` (a duplicate enqueue is a no-op, returning the existing
  534 |     row's id). ``process_once`` claims due ``pending`` rows and runs each through
  535 |     its resolved channel chain.
  536 |     """
  537 | 
  538 |     def __init__(self, db_path=':memory:', catalog=None, tenant='public'):
  539 |         self.db_path = db_path
  540 |         self.catalog = catalog or EventCatalog()
  541 |         self.tenant = tenant
  542 |         self._local = threading.local()
```

<!-- ─── страница 244 ─── -->

```py
  543 |         self.ensure_schema()
  544 | 
  545 |     def _conn(self):
  546 |         c = getattr(self._local, 'conn', None)
  547 |         if c is None:
  548 |             c = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)
  549 |             c.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
  550 |             self._local.conn = c
  551 |         return c
  552 | 
  553 |     def ensure_schema(self):
  554 |         c = self._conn()
  555 |         c.executescript(SCHEMA_SQLITE)
  556 |         # Lazy migration: a persistent queue created before the inbox feature (#222)
  557 |         # lacks read_at — add it so the inbox can mark notices read. Idempotent.
  558 |         cols = {r[1] for r in c.execute('PRAGMA table_info(notification)').fetchall()}
  559 |         if 'read_at' not in cols:
  560 |             c.execute('ALTER TABLE notification ADD COLUMN read_at REAL')
  561 |         c.execute('CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS notification_inbox_idx '
  562 |                   'ON notification(recipient, read_at)')
  563 |         c.commit()
  564 | 
  565 |     # ---- enqueue (idempotent) -------------------------------------------- #
  566 |     def enqueue(self, event, recipient, payload=None, prefs=None, tenant=None):
  567 |         """Queue one notification. Idempotent by ``dedup_key``; opt-out aware.
  568 | 
  569 |         Returns ``{'id', 'status', 'dedup_key', 'deduped'}``:
  570 |           * opted-out  -> a ``suppressed`` row (recorded, never sent),
  571 |           * duplicate  -> the existing row, ``deduped=True``, no new insert,
  572 |           * otherwise  -> a fresh ``pending`` row.
  573 |         """
  574 |         if not self.catalog.has(event):
  575 |             raise KeyError('unknown event type: %r' % event)
  576 |         payload = payload or {}
  577 |         prefs = prefs or DEFAULT_PREFERENCES
  578 |         tenant = tenant or self.tenant
  579 |         dedup_key = make_dedup_key(tenant, event, recipient, payload)
  580 |         c = self._conn()
  581 | 
  582 |         existing = c.execute(
  583 |             'SELECT id, status FROM notification WHERE dedup_key=?',
```

```py
  605 |     # ---- claim + send ----------------------------------------------------- #
  606 |     def _claim_due(self, now, limit):
  607 |         c = self._conn()
  608 |         return c.execute(
  609 |             "SELECT * FROM notification WHERE status='pending' "
  610 |             'AND next_attempt_at<=? ORDER BY id LIMIT ?',
  611 |             (now, limit)).fetchall()
  612 | 
  613 |     # ---- delivery journal (SPEC §5.4 delivery_attempt) -------------------- #
  614 |     def _channel_already_sent(self, notif_id, channel):
  615 |         """True if this channel already accepted this notice (idempotency guard).
  616 | 
  617 |         A retry must never re-send on a channel that already succeeded — the
  618 |         UNIQUE(notification_id, channel) journal row with status='sent' is the
  619 |         record of that. Honoured before every ``deliver`` attempt.
  620 |         """
  621 |         r = self._conn().execute(
  622 |             "SELECT 1 FROM delivery_attempt WHERE notification_id=? AND channel=? "
  623 |             "AND status='sent'", (notif_id, channel)).fetchone()
  624 |         return r is not None
  625 | 
  626 |     def _record_attempt(self, notif_id, channel, status, provider_id, error, now):
  627 |         """Upsert a delivery_attempt row (one per notification+channel).
  628 | 
  629 |         First hit on a channel inserts; a later retry on the same channel bumps
  630 |         ``tries`` and overwrites the latest outcome — so the journal carries the
  631 |         attempt count without ever duplicating the (notification, channel) pair.
  632 |         """
  633 |         c = self._conn()
  634 |         existing = c.execute(
  635 |             'SELECT id, tries FROM delivery_attempt WHERE notification_id=? '
  636 |             'AND channel=?', (notif_id, channel)).fetchone()
  637 |         if existing is None:
  638 |             c.execute(
  639 |                 'INSERT INTO delivery_attempt(notification_id,channel,status,'
  640 |                 'provider_id,error,tries,created_at,updated_at) VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,1,?,?)',
  641 |                 (notif_id, channel, status, provider_id, error, now, now))
  642 |         else:
  643 |             c.execute(
  644 |                 'UPDATE delivery_attempt SET status=?, provider_id=?, error=?, '
  645 |                 'tries=?, updated_at=? WHERE id=?',
  646 |                 (status, provider_id, error, existing['tries'] + 1, now, existing['id']))
  647 | 
  648 |     def delivery_log(self, notif_id):
  649 |         """The per-channel delivery journal for one notification (oldest first)."""
  650 |         return [dict(r) for r in self._conn().execute(
  651 |             'SELECT * FROM delivery_attempt WHERE notification_id=? ORDER BY id',
  652 |             (notif_id,)).fetchall()]
```

```py
  653 | 
  654 |     def _build_notification(self, row):
  655 |         """Render a claimed row into the immutable :class:`Notification` handed
  656 |         to channels (subject/body rendered via the event template)."""
  657 |         payload = json.loads(row['payload_json'])
  658 |         tmpl = self.catalog.template(row['event'])
  659 |         return Notification(
  660 |             id=row['id'], event=row['event'], recipient=row['recipient'],
  661 |             subject=render(tmpl.get('subject'), payload),
  662 |             body=render(tmpl.get('body'), payload),
  663 |             payload=payload, tenant=row['tenant'])
  664 | 
  665 |     def process_once(self, channels, now=None, limit=100):
  666 |         """Process up to ``limit`` due pending rows once. Returns a summary dict.
  667 | 
  668 |         ``channels`` is ``{name: Channel}``. For each claimed row we render the
  669 |         notice and walk its resolved channel chain via :meth:`Channel.deliver`,
  670 |         stopping at the first success (records the delivering channel + marks
  671 |         ``sent``). Every attempt — hit or miss — is written to the delivery journal
  672 |         (``delivery_attempt``); a channel that already accepted this notice is
  673 |         skipped (channel-level idempotency). If the whole chain misses we bump
  674 |         ``attempts``: under :data:`MAX_ATTEMPTS` the row goes back to ``pending``
  675 |         with an advisory backoff; at the cap it is marked ``failed``.
  676 |         """
  677 |         now = time.time() if now is None else now
  678 |         c = self._conn()
  679 |         summary = {'processed': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'retried': 0}
  680 | 
  681 |         for row in self._claim_due(now, limit):
  682 |             summary['processed'] += 1
  683 |             chain = json.loads(row['channels_json'])
  684 |             notice = self._build_notification(row)
  685 | 
  686 |             delivered_via = None
  687 |             last_error = None
  688 |             for cname in chain:
  689 |                 ch = channels.get(cname)
  690 |                 if ch is None:
  691 |                     last_error = 'no channel %r wired' % cname
  692 |                     continue
  693 |                 # Channel-level idempotency: never re-send where we already did.
  694 |                 if self._channel_already_sent(row['id'], cname):
  695 |                     delivered_via = cname
  696 |                     break
  697 |                 result = ch.deliver(notice)
  698 |                 if result.ok:
  699 |                     self._record_attempt(row['id'], cname, 'sent',
  700 |                                          result.provider_id, None, now)
  701 |                     delivered_via = cname
  702 |                     break
  703 |                 last_error = result.error or 'delivery failed'
  704 |                 self._record_attempt(row['id'], cname, 'failed',
  705 |                                      None, last_error, now)
  706 | 
  707 |             attempts = row['attempts'] + 1
```

```py
  709 |                 c.execute(
  710 |                     "UPDATE notification SET status='sent', channel=?, "
  711 |                     "attempts=?, last_error=NULL, updated_at=? WHERE id=?",
  712 |                     (delivered_via, attempts, now, row['id']))
  713 |                 summary['sent'] += 1
  714 |             elif attempts >= MAX_ATTEMPTS:
  715 |                 c.execute(
  716 |                     "UPDATE notification SET status='failed', attempts=?, "
  717 |                     'last_error=?, updated_at=? WHERE id=?',
  718 |                     (attempts, last_error, now, row['id']))
  719 |                 summary['failed'] += 1
  720 |             else:
  721 |                 nxt = now + backoff_seconds(attempts)
  722 |                 c.execute(
  723 |                     "UPDATE notification SET status='pending', attempts=?, "
  724 |                     'last_error=?, next_attempt_at=?, updated_at=? WHERE id=?',
  725 |                     (attempts, last_error, nxt, now, row['id']))
  726 |                 summary['retried'] += 1
  727 |         c.commit()

  729 | 
  730 |     def dispatch(self, channels, now=None, limit=100, max_passes=MAX_ATTEMPTS):
  731 |         """Drive the queue's dispatch: deliver every due notice, retrying within
  732 |         backoff up to its attempt cap. The high-level "диспетч очереди".
  733 | 
  734 |         Where :meth:`process_once` is a *single* claim-and-send pass,
  735 |         ``dispatch`` repeats passes until the queue settles — no due pending row
  736 |         remains deliverable in this call. Each pass advances the clock to the
  737 |         earliest ``next_attempt_at`` so a transiently-failing notice is retried
  738 |         within the same dispatch rather than waiting for an external scheduler;
  739 |         ``max_passes`` bounds the loop (defaults to :data:`MAX_ATTEMPTS`, the
  740 |         per-notice retry cap, so an always-failing notice walks to ``failed`` and
  741 |         stops). An empty / fully-settled queue is a clean no-op.
  742 | 
  743 |         Idempotent: a second ``dispatch`` over an already-settled queue delivers
  744 |         nothing (no pending rows) and never re-sends (channel-journal guard).
  745 |         Returns an aggregate ``{passes, processed, sent, failed, retried}``.
  746 |         """
  747 |         clock = time.time() if now is None else now
  748 |         agg = {'passes': 0, 'processed': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'retried': 0}
  749 |         for _ in range(max(1, max_passes)):
  750 |             summary = self.process_once(channels, now=clock, limit=limit)
  751 |             agg['passes'] += 1
  752 |             for k in ('processed', 'sent', 'failed', 'retried'):
  753 |                 agg[k] += summary[k]
  754 |             if summary['processed'] == 0:
  755 |                 break                       # nothing was due -> settled, stop
  756 |             # Advance to the soonest re-claimable pending row; if none remain
  757 |             # pending the next pass claims nothing and we exit on processed==0.
  758 |             nxt = self._conn().execute(
  759 |                 "SELECT MIN(next_attempt_at) AS t FROM notification "
  760 |                 "WHERE status='pending'").fetchone()['t']
  761 |             if nxt is None:
  762 |                 break                       # no pending rows left -> done
```

```py
  764 |         return agg

  786 |     # ---- reader inbox (#222) — reader-addressable view of dispatched notices ----
  787 |     # A reader sees the notices circulation enqueued FOR THEM (recipient == ticket),
  788 |     # rendered to a {subject, body} card via the event catalog. Suppressed notices
  789 |     # (opted-out, never meant for delivery) are excluded from the inbox view.
  790 |     def _inbox_card(self, row, tenant):
  791 |         """Render one stored notification row into an inbox card (id/ts/event/...)."""
  792 |         payload = json.loads(row['payload_json'])
  793 |         subject, body = '', ''
  794 |         if self.catalog.has(row['event']):
  795 |             tmpl = self.catalog.template(row['event'])
  796 |             subject = render(tmpl.get('subject'), payload)
  797 |             body = render(tmpl.get('body'), payload)
  798 |         return {'id': row['id'], 'ts': row['created_at'], 'event': row['event'],
  799 |                 'subject': subject, 'body': body, 'read': row['read_at'] is not None}
  800 | 
  801 |     def inbox(self, recipient, unread_only=False, tenant=None):
  802 |         """Dispatched notices addressed to ``recipient`` (newest first), as cards."""
  803 |         tenant = tenant or self.tenant
  804 |         sql = ("SELECT * FROM notification WHERE recipient=? AND tenant=? "
  805 |                "AND status!='suppressed'")
  806 |         params = [recipient, tenant]
  807 |         if unread_only:
  808 |             sql += ' AND read_at IS NULL'
  809 |         sql += ' ORDER BY id DESC'
  810 |         rows = self._conn().execute(sql, params).fetchall()
  811 |         return [self._inbox_card(r, tenant) for r in rows]
  812 |

  815 |         return self._conn().execute(
  816 |             "SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM notification WHERE recipient=? AND tenant=? "
  817 |             "AND status!='suppressed' AND read_at IS NULL",
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Licensing / entitlements (issue #101, I1): which MODULES a tenant is licensed for.
    3 | 
    4 | Entitlements answer "is this tenant LICENSED for module X?" — a separate axis from
    5 | grants. Grants are *can-do* (this account may write IBIS); entitlements are
    6 | *is-licensed* (this tenant has the cataloging module turned on at all). A request
    7 | is refused if EITHER fails: a valid grant on a disabled module is still refused
    8 | (403/404). Billing (initiative I7) will later consume this table; it is kept
    9 | independent of billing here.
   10 | 
   11 | Storage: ``control.tenant_module(tenant_id, module, enabled, PRIMARY KEY(tenant_id,
   12 | module))`` in the PostgreSQL ``control`` schema (the cross-tenant catalog, next to
   13 | ``control.tenant``). DDL is idempotent (``schema_control.sql``).
   14 | 
   15 | Dev / single-tenant fallback: the sqlite dev box and the non-tenant ``public``
   16 | path have no ``control`` schema. There, entitlement checks default to *enabled*
   17 | (``is_module_enabled`` returns True) so the guest flow and sqlite dev keep working
   18 | with zero config — licensing only bites once a tenant is provisioned on PG and a
   19 | module is explicitly disabled.
   20 | """
   21 | import os
   22 | 
   23 | # Functional modules of the product (surfaces composed by grants). The seed turns
   24 | # these ON by default for a freshly provisioned tenant; an operator disables one to
   25 | # revoke its license. Keep this list aligned with the product's functional modules.
   26 | DEFAULT_MODULES = (
   27 |     'opac',          # читательский портал / поиск-каталог
   28 |     'cataloging',    # каталогизация (запись/редактирование)
   29 |     'circulation',   # книговыдача
   30 |     'acquisition',   # комплектование (АРМ Комплектатор)
   31 |     'bookprovision', # книгообеспеченность (ВУЗ — связка + Кко)
   32 |     'reader',        # читательский кабинет / заказы
   33 |     'admin',         # администрирование тенанта
   34 |     'analytics',     # отчёты/статистика
   35 | )
   36 | 
```

```py
   38 | def default_pg_dsn():
   39 |     """DSN used by entitlement helpers — same env knob the Access store reads."""
   40 |     return os.environ.get(
   41 |         'ACCESS_PG_DSN', 'postgresql://postgres:pg@127.0.0.1:5433/irbis_access')
   42 | 
   43 | 
   44 | def _admin_conn(dsn):
   45 |     """Short-lived autocommit connection for control-plane reads/writes.
   46 | 
   47 |     Lazy psycopg import so this module imports on a Python without psycopg
   48 |     (the sqlite dev path never calls anything that needs it).
   49 |     """
   50 |     import psycopg
   51 |     from psycopg.rows import dict_row
   52 |     return psycopg.connect(dsn, row_factory=dict_row, autocommit=True)

   55 | def _tenant_id(conn, tenant):
   56 |     """Resolve a tenant slug to its control.tenant id, or None if unknown."""
   57 |     r = conn.execute('SELECT id FROM control.tenant WHERE slug=%s', (tenant,)).fetchone()
   58 |     return r['id'] if r else None
   59 | 
   60 | 
   61 | def seed_modules(tenant, dsn=None, modules=DEFAULT_MODULES, enabled=True):
   62 |     """Idempotently insert the default module set for ``tenant`` as enabled.
   63 | 
   64 |     Run as part of provisioning. Existing rows are left untouched (so an operator
   65 |     who disabled a module is not re-enabled by a re-provision). Returns the list
   66 |     of modules seeded. No-op-safe to call repeatedly.
   67 |     """
   68 |     dsn = dsn or default_pg_dsn()
   69 |     conn = _admin_conn(dsn)
   70 |     try:
   71 |         tid = _tenant_id(conn, tenant)
   72 |         if tid is None:
   73 |             raise ValueError('unknown tenant %r (provision it first)' % tenant)
   74 |         for m in modules:
   75 |             conn.execute(
   76 |                 'INSERT INTO control.tenant_module(tenant_id, module, enabled) '
   77 |                 'VALUES(%s,%s,%s) ON CONFLICT(tenant_id, module) DO NOTHING',
   78 |                 (tid, m, bool(enabled)))
   79 |         return list(modules)
   80 |     finally:
   81 |         conn.close()
   82 | 
   83 | 
   84 | def set_module(tenant, module, enabled, dsn=None):
   85 |     """Enable/disable one module for ``tenant`` (upsert). Returns the new state."""
   86 |     dsn = dsn or default_pg_dsn()
   87 |     conn = _admin_conn(dsn)
   88 |     try:
   89 |         tid = _tenant_id(conn, tenant)
   90 |         if tid is None:
   91 |             raise ValueError('unknown tenant %r' % tenant)
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/billing.py`  · строк: 285

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Billing skeleton (D5 / issue #209, epic #223 — MVP Phase 2).
    3 | 
    4 | A *skeleton*: tariff plans → the set of functional modules they license + a few
    5 | usage limits. NO payment gateway, NO invoicing/VAT, NO metering pipeline — those
    6 | are later D5 work. What this provides is the part the MVP needs:
    7 | 
    8 |   * a small, declarative ``PLANS`` table (``free`` / ``standard`` / ``pro``);
    9 |   * ``plan_modules(plan)`` / ``plan_limits(plan)`` — what a plan unlocks;
   10 |   * ``set_tenant_plan(store, tenant, plan)`` — record the tenant's plan AND apply
   11 |     its module entitlements (turn the plan's modules ON, the rest OFF) via the
   12 |     existing per-tenant entitlement gate (``access.entitlements``, issue #101);
   13 |   * ``get_tenant_plan`` / ``tenant_usage`` / ``check_limit`` — read the plan, count
   14 |     current usage, and a soft-enforcement helper for a record-create style check.
   15 | 
   16 | **Where the plan is stored.** The plan name is a control-plane fact, so it lives
   17 | next to the tenant in ``control.tenant.plan`` on PostgreSQL (added idempotently by
   18 | ``_ensure_plan_column`` — a plain ``ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`` so an
   19 | already-provisioned DB upgrades with no migration step). Entitlements themselves
   20 | stay in ``control.tenant_module`` exactly as before — billing only *drives* them.
   21 | 
   22 | **Dev / single-tenant degradation.** The sqlite dev box and the non-tenant
   23 | ``public`` path have no control plane. There ``set_tenant_plan`` is a no-op that
   24 | still validates the plan name and reports it; ``get_tenant_plan`` returns the
   25 | ``DEFAULT_PLAN``; entitlements stay fail-open (everything enabled) exactly as
   26 | ``access.entitlements`` already behaves with no rows. So billing never locks dev
   27 | out and is *off by default until a plan is explicitly set on a provisioned PG
   28 | tenant* — matching the "best-effort, off by default if no plan set" contract.
   29 | """
   30 | import os
   31 | 
   32 | from . import entitlements
   33 | 
   34 | # All modules a tenant could be licensed for — the catalogue billing draws from.
   35 | # Kept in lock-step with ``entitlements.DEFAULT_MODULES`` (the product's functional
```

```py
   36 | # module set); a plan selects a subset of these to enable.

   42 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   43 | # Plan catalogue. Each plan declares the modules it licenses and a few usage
   44 | # limits. ``limits`` keys are resources ``check_limit`` understands:
   45 | #   max_records   — bibliographic records the tenant may hold (catalog size)
   46 | #   max_readers   — registered readers
   47 | #   max_storage_mb— attached-file / cover storage budget, MB
   48 | # A limit of ``UNLIMITED`` (None) means no ceiling. A resource absent from a
   49 | # plan's limits is treated as unlimited too.
   50 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   51 | PLANS = {
   52 |     'free': {
   53 |         'title': 'Free',
   54 |         # A try-it tier: read/search + reader portal only; no staff write desks.
   55 |         'modules': ('opac', 'reader'),
   56 |         'limits': {'max_records': 1000, 'max_readers': 200, 'max_storage_mb': 100},
   57 |     },
   58 |     'standard': {
   59 |         'title': 'Standard',
   60 |         # A working small/medium library: catalog + circulation + reader portal +
   61 |         # admin + analytics. No acquisition / book-provision (those are pro).
   62 |         'modules': ('opac', 'cataloging', 'circulation', 'reader', 'admin', 'analytics'),
   63 |         'limits': {'max_records': 100000, 'max_readers': 20000, 'max_storage_mb': 10240},
   64 |     },
   65 |     'pro': {
   66 |         'title': 'Pro',
   67 |         # Everything: all functional modules, no ceilings.
   68 |         'modules': ALL_MODULES,
   69 |         'limits': {'max_records': UNLIMITED, 'max_readers': UNLIMITED,
   70 |                    'max_storage_mb': UNLIMITED},
   71 |     },
   72 | }
   73 | 
   74 | # The plan a freshly provisioned tenant gets, and the dev/``public`` answer.
   75 | DEFAULT_PLAN = os.environ.get('DEFAULT_PLAN', 'standard')
   76 | 
   77 | # Resources ``check_limit`` knows how to enforce.
   78 | LIMIT_RESOURCES = ('max_records', 'max_readers', 'max_storage_mb')
   79 | 
   80 | 
   81 | class BillingError(Exception):
   82 |     """Raised on an unknown plan or an over-limit when enforcing hard."""
   83 | 
   84 | 
   85 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   86 | # Pure plan lookups (no DB) — usable from anywhere, including sqlite dev.
   87 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   88 | def is_plan(plan):
   89 |     return plan in PLANS
   90 | 
```

```py
   91 | 
   92 | def _require_plan(plan):
   93 |     if plan not in PLANS:
   94 |         raise BillingError('unknown plan %r (known: %s)'
   95 |                            % (plan, ', '.join(sorted(PLANS))))
   96 |     return plan
   97 | 
   98 | 
   99 | def plan_modules(plan):
  100 |     """The functional modules a plan licenses (sorted), or raise BillingError."""
  101 |     return sorted(PLANS[_require_plan(plan)]['modules'])
  102 | 
  103 | 
  104 | def plan_limits(plan):
  105 |     """The usage limits for a plan as ``{resource: int|None}`` for every known
  106 |     resource (missing → UNLIMITED), or raise BillingError."""
  107 |     _require_plan(plan)
  108 |     declared = PLANS[plan]['limits']
  109 |     return {r: declared.get(r, UNLIMITED) for r in LIMIT_RESOURCES}
  110 | 
  111 | 
  112 | def plan_limit(plan, resource):
  113 |     """One limit value (int) or ``UNLIMITED`` (None). Raises on an unknown plan."""
  114 |     return plan_limits(plan).get(resource, UNLIMITED)
  115 | 
  116 | 
  117 | def plans_catalog():
  118 |     """The full catalogue for an admin UI: [{plan,title,modules,limits}]."""
  119 |     return [{'plan': name, 'title': PLANS[name]['title'],
  120 |              'modules': sorted(PLANS[name]['modules']), 'limits': plan_limits(name)}
  121 |             for name in sorted(PLANS)]
  122 | 
  123 | 
  124 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  125 | # Plan storage on the control plane (PostgreSQL). The sqlite dev / public path
  126 | # has no control plane → these degrade to DEFAULT_PLAN / no-op.
  127 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  128 | def _on_control_plane(store):
  129 |     """True iff billing should target a real PostgreSQL control plane.
  130 | 
  131 |     Real PG iff a ``PgAccessStore`` is in play OR the env selects the postgres
  132 |     backend. The sqlite dev ``AccessStore`` (single-tenant) → dev no-op path, even
  133 |     for a non-'public' slug (sqlite has no per-tenant schemas / control plane).
  134 |     """
  135 |     try:
  136 |         from .pgstore import PgAccessStore
  137 |         if isinstance(store, PgAccessStore):
  138 |             return True
  139 |     except Exception:
  140 |         pass
  141 |     return os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', 'sqlite').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg')
  142 | 
  143 | 
  144 | def _ensure_plan_column(dsn):
  145 |     """Add ``control.tenant.plan`` if missing (idempotent, no migration step).
```

<!-- ─── страница 255 ─── -->

```py
  146 | 
  147 |     The control schema/table are created by ``ensure_control_schema``; we only add
  148 |     the billing column here so billing owns its own storage extension without
  149 |     editing the shared control DDL. Safe to call repeatedly. The DEFAULT is inlined
  150 |     (a validated PLANS key, not user input) because PG can't infer a parameter's
  151 |     type inside DDL.
  152 |     """
  153 |     from .pgstore import _admin_conn, ensure_control_schema
  154 |     ensure_control_schema(dsn)
  155 |     default = DEFAULT_PLAN if DEFAULT_PLAN in PLANS else 'standard'
  156 |     conn = _admin_conn(dsn)
  157 |     try:
  158 |         conn.execute(
  159 |             "ALTER TABLE control.tenant ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS plan TEXT "
  160 |             "NOT NULL DEFAULT '%s'" % default)
  161 |     finally:
  162 |         conn.close()
  163 | 
  164 | 
  165 | def get_tenant_plan(tenant, dsn=None):
  166 |     """The tenant's stored plan, or ``DEFAULT_PLAN`` for dev/public/unreachable.
  167 | 
  168 |     Fail-soft like ``entitlements``: the ``public`` path, a control plane that is
  169 |     down, or a tenant with no row all answer ``DEFAULT_PLAN`` so dev keeps working.
  170 |     """
  171 |     if entitlements._is_public(tenant) or not _on_control_plane(None):
  172 |         # public, or sqlite dev (env backend not postgres) → default plan, no probe.
  173 |         return DEFAULT_PLAN
  174 |     dsn = dsn or entitlements.default_pg_dsn()
  175 |     try:
  176 |         _ensure_plan_column(dsn)
  177 |         from .pgstore import _admin_conn
  178 |         conn = _admin_conn(dsn)
  179 |     except Exception:
  180 |         return DEFAULT_PLAN
  181 |     try:
  182 |         r = conn.execute('SELECT plan FROM control.tenant WHERE slug=%s',
  183 |                          (tenant,)).fetchone()
  184 |         if not r or not r.get('plan'):
  185 |             return DEFAULT_PLAN
  186 |         plan = r['plan']
  187 |         return plan if plan in PLANS else DEFAULT_PLAN
  188 |     except Exception:
  189 |         return DEFAULT_PLAN
  190 |     finally:
  191 |         conn.close()
  192 | 
  193 | 
  194 | def set_tenant_plan(store, tenant, plan, dsn=None):
  195 |     """Assign ``plan`` to ``tenant`` and apply its module entitlements.
  196 | 
  197 |     Steps:
  198 |       1. validate the plan name (raise BillingError on unknown);
  199 |       2. record the plan on ``control.tenant.plan`` (PG only);
  200 |       3. drive ``control.tenant_module`` so exactly the plan's modules are enabled
```

<!-- ─── страница 256 ─── -->

```py
  201 |          and every other known module is disabled — the plan becomes the single
  202 |          source of truth for what the tenant can reach.
  203 | 
  204 |     Dev / ``public`` (no control plane): a no-op that still validates + returns the
  205 |     plan (entitlements stay fail-open). ``store`` is accepted for symmetry with the
  206 |     other provisioning helpers and future per-tenant bookkeeping; the entitlement
  207 |     writes go through the control plane by slug.
  208 |     """
  209 |     _require_plan(plan)
  210 |     if entitlements._is_public(tenant) or not _on_control_plane(store):
  211 |         # dev / single-tenant: validate + report, but nothing to persist or gate.
  212 |         return {'tenant': tenant, 'plan': plan, 'modules': plan_modules(plan),
  213 |                 'applied': False}
  214 |     dsn = dsn or entitlements.default_pg_dsn()
  215 |     _ensure_plan_column(dsn)
  216 |     from .pgstore import _admin_conn
  217 |     conn = _admin_conn(dsn)
  218 |     try:
  219 |         conn.execute('UPDATE control.tenant SET plan=%s WHERE slug=%s', (plan, tenant))
  220 |     finally:
  221 |         conn.close()
  222 |     # Apply entitlements: enable the plan's modules, disable the rest. set_module
  223 |     # upserts, so this converges regardless of prior state (re-plan safe).
  224 |     wanted = set(plan_modules(plan))
  225 |     for module in ALL_MODULES:
  226 |         entitlements.set_module(tenant, module, module in wanted, dsn)
  227 |     return {'tenant': tenant, 'plan': plan, 'modules': sorted(wanted), 'applied': True}
  228 | 
  229 | 
  230 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  231 | # Usage + soft enforcement.
  232 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  233 | def tenant_usage(store, tenant=None):
  234 |     """Best-effort current usage counters for a tenant ``store``.
  235 | 
  236 |     Counts what the Access store can see locally — registered staff accounts and
  237 |     the dictionary footprint — plus, when wired, catalog record / reader counts.
  238 |     A resource we can't count is simply omitted (callers treat absence as 0). Never
  239 |     raises: a store predating a table just yields a smaller dict.
  240 |     """
  241 |     usage = {}
  242 |     # Records: prefer a catalog handle on the store if present (max_records source).
  243 |     counter = getattr(store, 'record_count', None)
  244 |     if callable(counter):
  245 |         try:
  246 |             usage['max_records'] = int(counter())
  247 |         except Exception:
  248 |             pass
  249 |     # Readers: staff store doesn't hold readers, but a wired counter may.
  250 |     rcounter = getattr(store, 'reader_count', None)
  251 |     if callable(rcounter):
  252 |         try:
  253 |             usage['max_readers'] = int(rcounter())
  254 |         except Exception:
  255 |             pass
```

<!-- ─── страница 257 ─── -->

```py
  256 |     return usage
  257 | 
  258 | 
  259 | def check_limit(store, tenant, resource, current, plan=None, hard=False, dsn=None):
  260 |     """Soft-enforce a usage limit. Returns True iff ``current`` is within budget.
  261 | 
  262 |     ``current`` is the count *if this operation proceeds* (so a record-create passes
  263 |     ``record_count + 1``). Resolves the tenant's plan (unless ``plan`` is given),
  264 |     looks up that resource's ceiling, and compares.
  265 | 
  266 |     Best-effort + off by default:
  267 |       * an UNLIMITED ceiling (pro plan, or a resource a plan doesn't cap) → True;
  268 |       * the dev/``public`` path uses ``DEFAULT_PLAN`` like everything else;
  269 |       * an unknown ``resource`` → True (we don't enforce what we don't model).
  270 | 
  271 |     ``hard=True`` raises ``BillingError`` instead of returning False when over —
  272 |     for a call site that wants to abort rather than branch.
  273 |     """
  274 |     if resource not in LIMIT_RESOURCES:
  275 |         return True
  276 |     plan = plan or get_tenant_plan(tenant, dsn)
  277 |     ceiling = plan_limit(plan, resource)
  278 |     if ceiling is UNLIMITED:
  279 |         return True
  280 |     ok = int(current) <= int(ceiling)
  281 |     if not ok and hard:
  282 |         raise BillingError(
  283 |             'plan %r limit exceeded for %s: %s > %s'
  284 |             % (plan, resource, current, ceiling))
  285 |     return ok
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/provision.py`  · строк: 254

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Tenant provisioning (issue #187/#207, epic #223 — MVP Phase 2).
    3 | 
    4 | "A new library registers → gets a tenant → catalogues → lends." This is the
    5 | *one command* that stands a tenant up end-to-end, on top of the schema-per-tenant
    6 | machinery already in ``access.pgstore`` (issue #100):
    7 | 
    8 |   1. **schema** — ``CREATE SCHEMA t_<slug>`` + the Access DDL into it (done by
    9 |      ``PgAccessStore(tenant_schema=…)`` construction; recorded in
   10 |      ``control.tenant``);
   11 |   2. **vocabularies** — ``seed_vocabularies()`` populates the system dictionaries
   12 |      and creates the institution ones empty (gap A5, #188);
   13 |   3. **admin account** — the tenant's first staff account, carrying the seeded
   14 |      ``administrator`` role (so the library can immediately log in and run every
   15 |      desk);
   16 |   4. **plan + entitlements** — ``billing.set_tenant_plan`` records the plan and
   17 |      turns exactly that plan's modules ON (issue #101 / #209).
   18 | 
   19 | Everything is **idempotent**: re-provisioning the same slug re-converges (no dup
   20 | schema, no dup account, plan re-applied) and never destroys tenant data.
   21 | 
   22 | **sqlite / single-tenant dev degradation.** There is no control plane on the
   23 | sqlite dev box, and only the one ``DEFAULT_TENANT`` ('public') store. Provisioning
   24 | there still does the useful, real work it can — seed the roles + vocabularies into
   25 | that single store and create the admin account against it — and reports the plan
```

```py
   26 | without a control plane to persist it on. So the same call works in dev (you get a

   28 | isolated PG schema + persisted plan/entitlements).
   29 | 
   30 | CLI::
   31 | 
   32 |     python -m access.provision <slug> [--name NAME] [--admin LOGIN]
   33 |                                        [--password PW] [--plan PLAN] [--kind KIND]
   34 |     python -m access.provision --list

   36 | """
   37 | import os
   38 | import sys
   39 | 
   40 | from . import billing
   41 | 
   42 | # Default library kind for ``control.tenant.kind`` (NOT NULL). 'публичная' = a
   43 | # public library; an operator can pass --kind for school/vuz/departmental.
   44 | DEFAULT_KIND = 'публичная'
   45 | # Dev-default admin password, env-overridable (no secret in code; access.db is
   46 | # gitignored, PG tenant pw should be set explicitly in prod).
   47 | DEFAULT_ADMIN_PASSWORD = os.environ.get('ADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD', 'changeme')
   48 | # The role the tenant's first account gets — the seeded role that holds every
   49 | # staff grant (see access/seed.py ROLE_GRANTS['administrator']).
   50 | ADMIN_ROLE = 'administrator'
   51 | 
   52 | 
   53 | def _is_pg_dsn(handle):
   54 |     """True if ``handle`` looks like a PostgreSQL DSN string (vs a store object)."""
   55 |     return isinstance(handle, str) and handle.startswith(('postgres://', 'postgresql://'))
   56 | 
   57 | 
   58 | def _on_postgres(store):
   59 |     """Decide whether this provisioning targets a real PG control plane.
   60 | 
   61 |     Real PG iff a ``PgAccessStore`` (or a DSN string) is in play, or the env
   62 |     selects the postgres backend. The sqlite dev ``AccessStore`` → single-tenant.
   63 |     """
   64 |     if _is_pg_dsn(store):
   65 |         return True
   66 |     try:
   67 |         from .pgstore import PgAccessStore
   68 |         if isinstance(store, PgAccessStore):
   69 |             return True
   70 |     except Exception:
   71 |         pass
   72 |     return os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', 'sqlite').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg')
   73 | 
   74 | 
   75 | def _seed_admin_account(store, login, password, full_name=''):
   76 |     """Create (idempotent) ``login`` and give it the ``administrator`` role.
   77 | 
   78 |     Reuses the same primitives the dev seed uses (``create_account`` hashes the
   79 |     password pbkdf2-sha256; ``add_role``/``assign_role`` wire the role). Safe to
   80 |     re-run: ``create_account`` is INSERT-OR-IGNORE, role assignment is idempotent.
```

```py
   81 |     Returns the account row.
   82 |     """
   83 |     acc = store.create_account(login, password, full_name or login)
   84 |     rid = store.add_role(ADMIN_ROLE)
   85 |     store.assign_role(acc['id'], rid)

   87 | 
   88 | 
   89 | def provision_tenant(store, slug, name, admin_login,
   90 |                      admin_password=None, plan='standard', kind=DEFAULT_KIND,
   91 |                      dsn=None):
   92 |     """Stand up tenant ``<slug>`` end-to-end. Idempotent. Returns a report dict.
   93 | 
   94 |     ``store`` is the control-plane handle: on sqlite dev pass the single
   95 |     ``AccessStore`` (the 'public' store); on PG pass the public ``PgAccessStore``,
   96 |     a DSN string, or ``None`` (the env DSN is used). The tenant-scoped store the
   97 |     rest of the steps operate on is built here.
   98 | 
   99 |     Steps: schema → vocabularies → admin account (administrator role) → plan +
  100 |     entitlements. On the sqlite dev path the single store IS the tenant store.
  101 |     """
  102 |     admin_password = admin_password or DEFAULT_ADMIN_PASSWORD
  103 |     billing._require_plan(plan)               # fail fast on a bad plan name
  104 |     # Validate the slug on BOTH paths (PG validates downstream too, but the sqlite
  105 |     # dev path must reject a bad slug just as firmly so the contract is uniform).
  106 |     from .pgstore import _validate_slug
  107 |     _validate_slug(slug)
  108 | 
  109 |     if _on_postgres(store):
  110 |         # ---- real multi-tenant PG: isolated schema + control row + seeds ----
  111 |         from . import pgstore, seed, seed_vocab, entitlements
  112 |         if dsn is None:
  113 |             dsn = store if _is_pg_dsn(store) else getattr(store, 'dsn', None)
  114 |         dsn = dsn or pgstore.default_pg_dsn()
  115 |         # 1+2: schema + control row + DDL; seed roles/accounts + default modules +
  116 |         # vocabularies (pgstore.provision_tenant does roles/seed/entitlements/vocab).
  117 |         tstore = pgstore.provision_tenant(slug, name, kind, dsn=dsn, do_seed=True)
  118 |         # 3: the tenant's named admin account (beyond the demo admin the seed makes).
  119 |         admin = _seed_admin_account(tstore, admin_login, admin_password, name)
  120 |         # 4: plan → record it + drive entitlements to exactly the plan's modules.
  121 |         plan_info = billing.set_tenant_plan(tstore, slug, plan, dsn=dsn)
  122 |         on_pg = True
  123 |     else:
  124 |         # ---- sqlite / single-tenant dev: seed the single store, no control plane ----
  125 |         from . import seed, seed_vocab
  126 |         tstore = store
  127 |         seed.seed(tstore)                                 # roles + demo accounts (idempotent)
  128 |         try:
  129 |             seed_vocab.seed_vocabularies(tstore, from_catalog=False)
  130 |         except Exception:
  131 |             pass
  132 |         admin = _seed_admin_account(tstore, admin_login, admin_password, name)
  133 |         plan_info = billing.set_tenant_plan(tstore, slug, plan)   # no-op persist; validates
  134 |         on_pg = False
  135 |
```

```py
  147 | def deprovision_tenant(slug, dsn=None):
  148 |     """Drop tenant ``<slug>`` (PG: ``DROP SCHEMA t_<slug> CASCADE`` + control row).
  149 | 
  150 |     Delegates to ``pgstore.deprovision_tenant`` (idempotent). A no-op-safe call on
  151 |     a clean DB. On sqlite dev there is nothing per-tenant to drop, so this is a
  152 |     documented no-op there (the single store is shared).
  153 |     """
  154 |     from . import pgstore
  155 |     try:
  156 |         pgstore.deprovision_tenant(slug, dsn=dsn)
  157 |         return {'slug': slug, 'deprovisioned': True}
  158 |     except Exception as e:
  159 |         # psycopg missing / PG down → nothing to do on the dev box.
  160 |         return {'slug': slug, 'deprovisioned': False, 'reason': str(e).splitlines()[0]}
  161 | 
  162 | 
  163 | def list_tenants(dsn=None):
  164 |     """All provisioned tenants from the control catalog, with their plan.
  165 | 
  166 |     Reuses ``pgstore.list_tenants`` and joins the billing plan
  167 |     (``billing.get_tenant_plan``). Returns [] cleanly when there is no control
  168 |     plane (sqlite dev / PG down).
  169 |     """
  170 |     from . import pgstore
  171 |     try:
  172 |         rows = pgstore.list_tenants(dsn=dsn)
  173 |     except Exception:
  174 |         return []
  175 |     out = []
  176 |     for r in rows:
  177 |         item = {'slug': r['slug'], 'name': r['name'], 'kind': r.get('kind')}
  178 |         try:
  179 |             item['plan'] = billing.get_tenant_plan(r['slug'], dsn=dsn)
  180 |         except Exception:
  181 |             item['plan'] = billing.DEFAULT_PLAN
  182 |         out.append(item)
  183 |     return out
  184 |
```

```py
  211 | def main(argv=None):
  212 |     import argparse
  213 |     p = argparse.ArgumentParser(
  214 |         prog='python -m access.provision',
  215 |         description='Provision / deprovision / list IRBIS-web tenants.')
  216 |     p.add_argument('slug', nargs='?', help='tenant slug (^[a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,40}$)')
  217 |     p.add_argument('--name', help='human-readable library name (default: slug)')
  218 |     p.add_argument('--admin', default='admin', help='admin account login (default: admin)')
  219 |     p.add_argument('--password', help='admin password (default: $ADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD or changeme)')
  220 |     p.add_argument('--plan', default='standard',
  221 |                    choices=sorted(billing.PLANS), help='billing plan (default: standard)')
  222 |     p.add_argument('--kind', default=DEFAULT_KIND, help='library kind (default: %s)' % DEFAULT_KIND)
  223 |     p.add_argument('--dsn', help='PostgreSQL DSN (default: $ACCESS_PG_DSN)')
  224 |     p.add_argument('--list', action='store_true', help='list provisioned tenants and exit')
  225 |     p.add_argument('--deprovision', metavar='SLUG', help='drop a tenant and exit')
  226 |     args = p.parse_args(argv)
  227 | 
  228 |     if args.list:
  229 |         rows = list_tenants(dsn=args.dsn)
  230 |         if not rows:
  231 |             print('(no tenants — sqlite dev or empty control plane)')
  232 |         for r in rows:
  233 |             print('%-20s %-8s %-12s %s' % (r['slug'], r.get('plan'), r.get('kind'), r['name']))
  234 |         return 0
  235 | 
  236 |     if args.deprovision:
  237 |         rep = deprovision_tenant(args.deprovision, dsn=args.dsn)
  238 |         print('deprovisioned %r: %s' % (rep['slug'], rep['deprovisioned'])
  239 |               + ('' if rep['deprovisioned'] else ' (%s)' % rep.get('reason')))
  240 |         return 0
  241 | 
  242 |     if not args.slug:
  243 |         p.error('a tenant slug is required (or use --list / --deprovision)')
  244 | 
  245 |     store = _build_store(args.dsn)
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Access store — sqlite3 for dev (runs with no DB server); schema mirrors the
    3 | PostgreSQL DDL in schema_postgres.sql (ADR-004: sqlite dev / Postgres prod).
    4 | 
    5 | Holds staff accounts, grants, roles and the audit log. Readers are NOT stored
    6 | here (they authenticate against RDR); only staff + their grants live in this store.
    7 | """
```

```py
  181 | class AccessStore:
  182 |     def __init__(self, db_path):
  183 |         self.db_path = db_path
  184 |         self._local = threading.local()
  185 |         self.ensure_schema()
  186 | 
  187 |     def _conn(self):
  188 |         c = getattr(self._local, 'conn', None)
  189 |         if c is None:
  190 |             c = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)
  191 |             c.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
  192 |             c.execute('PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON')
  193 |             self._local.conn = c
  194 |         return c
  195 | 
  196 |     def ensure_schema(self):
  197 |         c = self._conn()
  198 |         c.executescript(SCHEMA_SQLITE)
  199 |         c.commit()
  200 | 
  201 |     # ---- password hashing (pbkdf2-sha256, stdlib) ----
  202 |     @staticmethod
  203 |     def hash_password(pw, iterations=200000):
  204 |         salt = os.urandom(16)
  205 |         dk = hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac('sha256', pw.encode('utf-8'), salt, iterations)
  206 |         return 'pbkdf2$%d$%s$%s' % (iterations, salt.hex(), dk.hex())
  207 | 
  208 |     @staticmethod
  209 |     def verify_password(pw, stored):
  210 |         try:
  211 |             _algo, it, salt_hex, hash_hex = stored.split('$')
```

```py
  293 |         Idempotent and atomic: clears the account's account_role rows then assigns

  296 |         c = self._conn()

  298 |         for name in role_names:

  300 |             c.execute('INSERT OR IGNORE INTO account_role(account_id,role_id) VALUES(?,?)',

  302 |         c.commit()
  303 |         return self.account_roles(account_id)
```

```py
  338 |     def upsert_vocabulary(self, name, title, kind, field_hint, seed_version):

  348 |     def upsert_vocabulary_value(self, vocab, code, label, sort, origin='seed'):

  350 | 
  351 |         Never downgrades origin: a value the library marked 'custom'/'imported' keeps
  352 |         that origin on a re-seed so reseed merge logic (SPEC §3.3) can protect it.

  354 |         c = self._conn()
  355 |         c.execute(
  356 |             'INSERT INTO vocabulary_value(vocab,code,label,sort,origin) VALUES(?,?,?,?,?) '
  357 |             'ON CONFLICT(vocab,code) DO UPDATE SET label=excluded.label, sort=excluded.sort',
  358 |             (vocab, code, label, sort, origin))
  359 |         c.commit()
```

```py
  449 |     def shelf_create(self, ticket, list_id, name, system=0):
  450 |         c = self._conn()
  451 |         c.execute('INSERT OR IGNORE INTO reader_shelf(id,ticket,name,system) '
  452 |                   'VALUES(?,?,?,?)', (list_id, ticket, name, 1 if system else 0))
  453 |         c.commit()
  454 |         return self.shelf_get(ticket, list_id)
  455 | 
  456 |     def shelf_next_custom_id(self, ticket):
  457 |         """Next free custom-list id 's<n>' for this reader (avoids system ids)."""
  458 |         n = self._conn().execute(
  459 |             "SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM reader_shelf WHERE ticket=? AND system=0",
  460 |             (ticket,)).fetchone()['n']
  461 |         existing = {r['id'] for r in self.shelf_lists(ticket)}
  462 |         i = n + 1
  463 |         while ('s%d' % i) in existing:
  464 |             i += 1
  465 |         return 's%d' % i
  466 | 
  467 |     def shelf_items(self, ticket, list_id):
  468 |         return [dict(r) for r in self._conn().execute(
  469 |             'SELECT db,mfn,title FROM reader_shelf_item WHERE ticket=? AND list_id=? '
  470 |             'ORDER BY added_at, db, mfn', (ticket, list_id)).fetchall()]
  471 | 
  472 |     def shelf_add_item(self, ticket, list_id, db, mfn, title):
  473 |         """Add (db,mfn) to a list, deduped per list (PK upserts title)."""
  474 |         c = self._conn()
  475 |         c.execute(
  476 |             'INSERT INTO reader_shelf_item(ticket,list_id,db,mfn,title) VALUES(?,?,?,?,?) '
  477 |             'ON CONFLICT(ticket,list_id,db,mfn) DO UPDATE SET title=excluded.title',
  478 |             (ticket, list_id, db, mfn, title))
  479 |         c.commit()
  480 | 
  481 |     def shelf_remove_item(self, ticket, list_id, db, mfn):
  482 |         c = self._conn()
  483 |         c.execute('DELETE FROM reader_shelf_item WHERE ticket=? AND list_id=? '
  484 |                   'AND db=? AND mfn=?', (ticket, list_id, db, mfn))
  485 |         c.commit()
  486 |
```

```py
  489 |     def _decrypt_review(row):
  490 |         """Return the review dict with ``reader_name`` decrypted (V1).

  492 |         The column is stored as ciphertext at rest (crypto.encrypt); decryption is

  494 |         if row is None:

  501 |     def review_upsert(self, ticket, db, mfn, rating, text, reader_name, ts):
  502 |         """Insert/replace the reader's review of (db,mfn) (one per reader+item).
  503 | 
  504 |         ``reader_name`` is the reader's resolved display name (ПДн); it is
  505 |         ENCRYPTED at rest (V1) so a raw DB dump never reveals it. The returned row
  506 |         is decrypted for the caller (transparent)."""
  507 |         c = self._conn()
  508 |         enc_name = crypto.encrypt(reader_name)
  509 |         c.execute(
  510 |             'INSERT INTO reader_review(ticket,db,mfn,rating,text,reader_name,ts) '
  511 |             'VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?) ON CONFLICT(ticket,db,mfn) DO UPDATE SET '
  512 |             'rating=excluded.rating, text=excluded.text, '
  513 |             'reader_name=excluded.reader_name, ts=excluded.ts',
  514 |             (ticket, db, mfn, rating, text, enc_name, ts))
  515 |         c.commit()
  516 |         r = c.execute('SELECT * FROM reader_review WHERE ticket=? AND db=? AND mfn=?',
  517 |                       (ticket, db, mfn)).fetchone()
  518 |         return self._decrypt_review(r)

  525 |             'SELECT * FROM reader_review WHERE db=? AND mfn=? ORDER BY ts DESC, id DESC',
  526 |             (db, mfn)).fetchall()]

  532 |             (ticket, db, mfn)).fetchone()
```

```py
  598 |     # ---- consent (V9 / R9) — append-only; latest row by ts is effective ----
  599 |     def consent_record(self, ticket, given, version, ts):
  600 |         """Append a consent state (granted/withdrawn) for the reader. Never an
  601 |         UPDATE — the prior state is kept as history (SPEC_compliance_152fz §2.5).
  602 |         Returns the effective consent after the write."""
  603 |         c = self._conn()
  604 |         c.execute('INSERT INTO reader_consent(ticket,given,version,ts) VALUES(?,?,?,?)',
  605 |                   (ticket, 1 if given else 0, int(version), ts))
  606 |         c.commit()
  607 |         return self.consent_current(ticket)
  608 | 
  609 |     def consent_current(self, ticket):
  610 |         """The reader's effective consent (latest row by ts), or None if never set.
  611 | 
  612 |         Returns ``{'given': bool, 'ts': float, 'version': int}``."""
  613 |         r = self._conn().execute(
  614 |             'SELECT given,version,ts FROM reader_consent WHERE ticket=? '
  615 |             'ORDER BY ts DESC, id DESC LIMIT 1', (ticket,)).fetchone()
  616 |         if not r:
  617 |             return None
  618 |         return {'given': bool(r['given']), 'ts': r['ts'], 'version': r['version']}
  619 | 
  620 |     # ---- right to erasure (V9 / R9) — delete OUR stored reader data only ----
  621 |     # Deletes the reader's own rows across every table OUR store holds for a
  622 |     # ticket. Does NOT touch the live ИРБИС RDR (that is the system of record);
  623 |     # only the portal-side state we persisted. Reader-scoped: a ticket can only
  624 |     # erase its own rows (the route guards the ticket == session).
  625 |     def erase_reader(self, ticket):
  626 |         """Delete all of ``ticket``'s portal data; return a per-table count dict."""
  627 |         c = self._conn()
  628 |         counts = {}
  629 |         for key, sql in (
  630 |                 ('reviews', 'DELETE FROM reader_review WHERE ticket=?'),
  631 |                 ('holds', 'DELETE FROM reader_hold WHERE ticket=?'),
  632 |                 ('shelves', 'DELETE FROM reader_shelf WHERE ticket=?'),
  633 |                 ('shelfItems', 'DELETE FROM reader_shelf_item WHERE ticket=?'),
  634 |                 ('history', 'DELETE FROM reader_history WHERE ticket=?'),
  635 |                 ('savedSearches', 'DELETE FROM saved_search WHERE ticket=?'),
  636 |                 ('consent', 'DELETE FROM reader_consent WHERE ticket=?')):
  637 |             cur = c.execute(sql, (ticket,))
  638 |             counts[key] = cur.rowcount if cur.rowcount is not None and cur.rowcount >= 0 else 0
  639 |         c.commit()
  640 |         return counts
  641 |

  648 |         SPEC_compliance_152fz §3.5). Each entry carries actor, subject ticket (in
  649 |         detail), ts, action."""
  650 |         return [dict(r) for r in self._conn().execute(
  651 |             "SELECT * FROM audit_log WHERE function='pdn.read' "
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/access/pgstore.py`  · строк: 700

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """PostgreSQL-backed Access store (prod target, ADR-004). Mirrors the sqlite
    3 | ``AccessStore`` public surface exactly — same methods, same return shapes — so
    4 | ``core.py`` and the Access test suite run unchanged against either backend
    5 | (switch via env ``ACCESS_BACKEND=sqlite|postgres``).
    6 | 
    7 | Holds staff accounts, grants, roles and the audit log (readers live in RDR, not
    8 | here). DDL is the shared ``schema_postgres.sql``. Password hashing (pbkdf2-sha256)
    9 | is reused verbatim from ``AccessStore`` so hashes are interchangeable across
   10 | backends.
   11 | 
   12 | Driver: psycopg 3 (``pip install "psycopg[binary]"``). psycopg is imported
   13 | lazily so this module still imports on a Python without psycopg installed
   14 | (e.g. 3.14) when the sqlite backend is selected — only constructing
   15 | ``PgAccessStore`` requires the driver.
   16 | """
   17 | import os
   18 | import re
   19 | import threading
   20 | 
   21 | from .store import AccessStore   # reuse pbkdf2 hash/verify (interchangeable hashes)
   22 | from . import crypto             # ПДн field encryption (V1): pgcrypto key + token fallback
   23 | 
   24 | # DDL lives next to this module as the shared production schema.
   25 | _HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
   26 | _SCHEMA_SQL_PATH = os.path.join(_HERE, 'schema_postgres.sql')      # Access DDL (per tenant)
   27 | _CONTROL_SQL_PATH = os.path.join(_HERE, 'schema_control.sql')      # control.tenant catalog
   28 | 
   29 | # A slug is the tenant key and becomes part of a SQL identifier (t_<slug>). It is
   30 | # NEVER interpolated into SQL without passing this gate, so schema names can't be
   31 | # used for injection. Lowercase letters/digits/underscore, must start with a letter.
   32 | _SLUG_RE = re.compile(r'^[a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,40}$')
   33 | 
   34 | 
   35 | def _validate_slug(slug):
   36 |     if not isinstance(slug, str) or not _SLUG_RE.match(slug):
   37 |         raise ValueError(
   38 |             'invalid tenant slug %r (need ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,40}$)' % (slug,))
   39 |     return slug
   40 | 
   41 | 
   42 | def schema_for(slug):
   43 |     """Schema name that holds tenant <slug>'s Access data: ``t_<slug>``."""
   44 |     return 't_' + _validate_slug(slug)
   45 | 
   46 | 
   47 | class PgAccessStore:
   48 |     """Same public API as ``access.store.AccessStore`` on PostgreSQL/psycopg3.
   49 | 
   50 |     A dedicated database/schema is expected (default DSN points at
   51 |     ``irbis_access``) so the Access tables never collide with own-server
   52 |     catalog data.
   53 | 
   54 |     **Multi-tenant (issue #100):** pass ``tenant_schema='t_<slug>'`` to scope the
```

<!-- ─── страница 275 ─── -->

```py
   55 |     SAME store class to one tenant. Every connection checkout then runs
   56 |     ``SET search_path = <schema>, public`` so all unqualified table access reads/
   57 |     writes that tenant's schema only — data is fully separated by schema, with no
   58 |     leakage when a pooled connection is reused (the path is set on every checkout).
   59 |     ``tenant_schema=None`` (the default) keeps the back-compat single-``public``
   60 |     behaviour unchanged. Prefer ``make_tenant_store(dsn, slug)`` to construct one.
   61 |     """
   62 | 
   63 |     def __init__(self, dsn, tenant_schema=None):
   64 |         self.dsn = dsn
   65 |         self.db_path = dsn          # parity with AccessStore.db_path (a few callers introspect it)
   66 |         self.tenant_schema = tenant_schema   # 't_<slug>' for a tenant, or None for public
   67 |         self._local = threading.local()
   68 |         self.ensure_schema()
   69 | 
   70 |     # ---- connection (thread-local, autocommit like the sqlite store's per-op commit) ----
   71 |     def _conn(self):
   72 |         import psycopg
   73 |         from psycopg.rows import dict_row
   74 |         conn = getattr(self._local, 'conn', None)
   75 |         if conn is None or conn.closed:
   76 |             conn = psycopg.connect(self.dsn, row_factory=dict_row, autocommit=True)
   77 |             self._apply_search_path(conn)
   78 |             self._local.conn = conn
   79 |         return conn
   80 | 
   81 |     def _apply_search_path(self, conn):
   82 |         """Pin this connection to the tenant schema (then ``public``).
   83 | 
   84 |         Called on every checkout of a fresh connection, so a connection reused
   85 |         across tenants never leaks: the search_path is (re)set deterministically.
   86 |         The schema identifier is built only from a validated slug (see
   87 |         ``schema_for``), so it is safe to embed without quoting injection risk.
   88 |         Run only when this store is tenant-scoped; the public store keeps PG's
   89 |         default search_path untouched (back-compat).
   90 |         """
   91 |         if self.tenant_schema:
   92 |             from psycopg import sql
   93 |             conn.execute(
   94 |                 sql.SQL('SET search_path = {}, public').format(
   95 |                     sql.Identifier(self.tenant_schema)))
   96 | 
   97 |     def ensure_schema(self):
   98 |         """Create the Access tables. For a tenant store, create the schema first
   99 |         and apply the (unqualified) Access DDL *into* it via ``search_path``."""
  100 |         conn = self._conn()   # also applies search_path = t_<slug>, public
  101 |         # pgcrypto: enable best-effort BEFORE the DDL (V1 ПДн encryption). It lives
  102 |         # in the public/extension schema, so it is shared across tenant schemas. A
  103 |         # role without CREATE EXTENSION privilege must not abort schema creation —
  104 |         # we record availability and degrade to the Python-side cipher token.
  105 |         self._pgcrypto = self._ensure_pgcrypto(conn)
  106 |         if self.tenant_schema:
  107 |             from psycopg import sql
  108 |             conn.execute(sql.SQL('CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS {}').format(
  109 |                 sql.Identifier(self.tenant_schema)))
```

```py
  110 |             # search_path is already pinned (PG resolves it lazily at query time),
  111 |             # so the unqualified DDL below creates tables inside t_<slug>.
  112 |         with open(_SCHEMA_SQL_PATH, encoding='utf-8') as f:
  113 |             ddl = f.read()
  114 |         conn.execute(ddl)
  115 | 
  116 |     @staticmethod
  117 |     def _ensure_pgcrypto(conn):
  118 |         """Best-effort ``CREATE EXTENSION pgcrypto`` + verify pgp_sym_* are callable.
  119 | 
  120 |         Returns True iff in-database encryption is available. On a role without the
  121 |         privilege (or pgcrypto absent) returns False so review writes fall back to
  122 |         the Python-side cipher token — still ciphertext at rest, just not pgcrypto."""
  123 |         try:
  124 |             conn.execute('CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto')
  125 |         except Exception:
  126 |             pass
  127 |         try:
  128 |             conn.execute("SELECT pgp_sym_decrypt(pgp_sym_encrypt('x', 'k'), 'k')").fetchone()
  129 |             return True
  130 |         except Exception:
  131 |             return False
  132 | 
  133 |     # ---- password hashing — reuse the sqlite store's pbkdf2 (hashes interchangeable) ----
  134 |     @staticmethod
  135 |     def hash_password(pw, iterations=200000):
  136 |         return AccessStore.hash_password(pw, iterations)
  137 | 
  138 |     @staticmethod
  139 |     def verify_password(pw, stored):
  140 |         return AccessStore.verify_password(pw, stored)
  141 |

  146 |             ' ON CONFLICT(login) DO NOTHING',

  148 |         return self.get_account(login)

  152 |             'SELECT * FROM staff_account WHERE login=%s', (login,)).fetchone()

  158 |             return a
  159 |         return None
```

```py
  237 |             out.append({'name': ro['name'], 'grants': grants})
  238 |         return out

  251 |     # ---- vocabularies (seeding engine, gap A5 #188) — same surface as AccessStore ----

  264 |             'ON CONFLICT(vocab,code) DO UPDATE SET label=EXCLUDED.label, sort=EXCLUDED.sort',
  265 |             (vocab, code, label, sort, origin))
```
