```py
   58 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   59 | # Fixtures — реальный каталог (in-memory) с записью, несущей 903 шифр + 910 экз.
   60 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   61 | def _seeded_access_store():
   62 |     st = AccessStore(':memory:')
   63 |     seed_vocab.seed_vocabularies(st, from_catalog=False)
   64 |     return st
   65 | 
   66 | 
   67 | def _book(shifr='AB/Ч-15', inv='INV-777', status=EXEMPLAR_FREE):
   68 |     """Валидная книга: поле 903 (шифр документа) + один экз. 910 по инв.№ ``inv``."""
   69 |     return {
   70 |         '920': 'PAZK',
   71 |         '200': [{'a': 'Основы каталогизации', 'f': 'Иванова И.И.'}],
   72 |         '700': [{'a': 'Петров П.П.'}],
   73 |         '101': 'rus',
   74 |         '903': shifr,
   75 |         '910': [{'a': status, 'b': inv}],
   76 |         '907': [{'a': 'Сидорова С.С.'}],
   77 |     }
   78 | 
   79 | 
   80 | def _engine_with_catalog(shifr='AB/Ч-15', inv='INV-777', status=EXEMPLAR_FREE):
   81 |     """Движок книговыдачи с подключённым каталогом и одной записью в ЭК."""
   82 |     cat = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=_seeded_access_store())
   83 |     cat.save('IBIS', _book(shifr, inv, status))
   84 |     eng = CirculationEngine(store=CirculationStore(':memory:'),
   85 |                             policy=default_policy(), catalog=cat, catalog_db='IBIS')
   86 |     return cat, eng
   87 | 
   88 | 
   89 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
```

```py
  146 | 
  147 |     # прямое: код брони → наш enum (DB_CIRCULATION §5 «БРОНИРОВАНИЕ»).
  148 |     expected_in = {'0': 'queued', '1': 'ready', '2': 'fulfilled',

  155 |     check('таблица RESERVSTATUS_TO_HOLD совпадает', RESERVSTATUS_TO_HOLD == expected_in)
  156 | 
  157 |     # неизвестный/пустой код → None (нечего отображать).

  159 |     check('None код → None', eng.resolve_hold_status_in(None) is None)

  161 |     # обратное: наш enum → код брони.

  169 |
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """HTTP-route tests for the CIRCULATION desk + CATALOGING worklist (MVP Phase 1,
    3 | #183 cataloger / #185 circulation, epic #223).
    4 | 
    5 | The CirculationEngine is unit-tested standalone (``test_circulation.py``) and its
    6 | catalog 910^A flip is covered by ``test_integration.py``; THIS suite proves the
    7 | staff workstation surface is reachable over HTTP — every assertion goes through
    8 | the real ``core.Api.route()`` dispatcher (the same path server.py / app_aiohttp.py
    9 | use), with a constructed ``Api`` (the constructor does NOT connect to ИРБИС).
   10 | 
   11 | Covered (the task contract, all through route()):
   12 |   * CATALOGING #183 — GET /api/worklist/{db} returns the editor field menu
   13 |     (curated WORKLIST_IBIS for IBIS/PAZK, generic fallback otherwise);
   14 |   * CIRCULATION #185 — a STAFF session can issue → the reader formulary
   15 |     (/api/circ/reader, field 40) shows the loan → return clears it; renew updates
```

```py
   79 | def _api(with_copy=True, fixed_now=None):

   97 |         notifications=api.notifications,
   98 |         catalog=api.catalog, catalog_db=api.cfg.db_default)
   99 |     if fixed_now is not None:
  100 |         api._CIRC_NOW = lambda: fixed_now
  101 |     return api, _core

  104 | def _staff(api, login='circ'):
  105 |     tok, _sess = api._new_session('staff', login, STAFF_GRANTS, tenant='public')

  107 |

  112 | 
  113 |

  116 |                               tenant='public', rdr_mfn=1)
  117 |     return {'authorization': 'Bearer ' + tok}
```

```py
  291 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  292 | # 3. Auth — guest AND reader get 403 on every staff circ/worklist route.
  293 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  294 | STAFF_ROUTES = [
  295 |     ('GET', '/api/worklist/IBIS', None),
  296 |     ('GET', '/api/circ/reader', None),       # ?ticket= via query
  297 |     ('POST', '/api/circ/issue', {'ticket': '111', 'db': 'IBIS', 'item': INV}),
  298 |     ('POST', '/api/circ/return', {'ticket': '111', 'db': 'IBIS', 'item': INV}),
  299 |     ('POST', '/api/circ/renew', {'ticket': '111', 'db': 'IBIS', 'item': INV}),
  300 |     ('GET', '/api/circ/fines', None),        # ?ticket= via query

  303 | 
  304 | def auth_checks():
  305 |     print('-- auth: guest + reader 403 on every staff circ/worklist route')
  306 |     api, _core = _api()
  307 | 
  308 |     for label, headers in (('guest', _guest_headers(api)),

  313 |             check('%s denied on %s %s (403)' % (label, m, path), st == 403)
  314 | 
  315 |     # No session at all -> 401/403, never 200.

  318 |         check('no session on %s %s -> 401/403' % (m, path), st in (401, 403))

  323 |     check('staff admitted to /api/worklist/IBIS (200)', st == 200)
  324 |     st, _ = api.route('GET', '/api/circ/reader', {'ticket': ['111']}, None, H)

  339 |     check('issue allowed when circulation module licensed', st == 200)
  340 | 
  341 |     orig = _core.entitlements.is_module_enabled
  342 |     # Disable the circulation module for this tenant (cataloging stays enabled).
  343 |     _core.entitlements.is_module_enabled = (
  344 |         lambda tenant, module, dsn=None: module != 'circulation')
  345 |     try:
```

```py
  346 |         st, p = api.route('GET', '/api/circ/reader', {'ticket': ['333']}, None, H)

  350 |         st, _ = api.route('POST', '/api/circ/return', {},

  358 | 
  359 |     # Restored: issue works again once the module is licensed.
  360 |     st, _ = api.route('POST', '/api/circ/issue', {},
  361 |                       {'ticket': '444', 'db': 'IBIS', 'item': INV}, H)
  362 |     check('issue allowed again after module re-enabled', st == 200)
  363 | 
  364 | 
  365 | def main():
  366 |     worklist_route_checks()
  367 |     circulation_route_checks()
  368 |     circ_fines_route_checks()
  369 |     circ_formular_blocks_checks()
  370 |     auth_checks()
  371 |     entitlement_gate_checks()
  372 |     print('\n%d passed, %d failed' % (PASS[0], FAIL[0]))
  373 |     sys.exit(1 if FAIL[0] else 0)
  374 | 
  375 | 
  376 | if __name__ == '__main__':
  377 |     main()
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_circulation.py`  · строк: 444

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Circulation engine tests (gap E2, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Scenario-based tests of the circulation rules + own sqlite store, per
    5 | SPEC_business_circulation.md. Everything runs against an in-memory store with an
    6 | *injected* ``today`` (epoch seconds) so the rules — fines, lost grace, queue —
    7 | are deterministic and need no clock.
    8 | 
    9 | Standalone-runnable in the house style of ``test_seeding.py``::
   10 | 
   11 |     py -3.12 tests/test_circulation.py   ->  ok ...  +  "N passed, M failed"  + exit code
   12 | 
   13 | Covered (mirrors the §11 test matrix + the task scenarios):
   14 |   * checkout respects ``max_books`` (limit_exceeded);
   15 |   * a hard debtor is blocked from checkout (reader_has_debt) but an authorised
   16 |     ``staff_override`` lends over the debt;
   17 |   * renew is blocked when a hold exists (holds_block_renewal=block), allowed with
   18 |     an authorised override; ``threshold`` / ``allow`` modes;
   19 |   * renew cap (max_prolong) enforced — never override-able;
   20 |   * fine accrues correctly after grace (numeric example) + cap + fine-free;
   21 |   * hold queue gives correct FIFO position;
   22 |   * return triggers hold-ready;
   23 |   * lost candidate → confirm → replacement charge;
```

```py
   24 |   * tenant policy isolation.
   25 | """
   26 | import os
   27 | import sys
   28 | 
   29 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   30 | from access import circulation as circ
   31 | from access.circulation import (
   32 |     CirculationStore, CirculationEngine, default_policy,
   33 |     ALLOW, DENY, REQUIRE_OVERRIDE, SECONDS_PER_DAY,
   34 | )
   35 | 
   36 | PASS = [0]
   37 | FAIL = [0]
   38 | 
   39 | # A fixed reference "today" so every scenario is deterministic.
   40 | DAY = SECONDS_PER_DAY
   41 | T0 = 1_700_000_000  # arbitrary epoch anchor
   42 | 
   43 | 
   44 | def check(name, cond):
   45 |     if cond:
   46 |         PASS[0] += 1
   47 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   48 |     else:
   49 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   50 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   51 | 
   52 | 
   53 | def fresh(policy=None, category='В01', reader='R1'):
   54 |     """A fresh engine over an in-memory store with one seeded reader."""
   55 |     store = CirculationStore(':memory:')
   56 |     eng = CirculationEngine(store=store, policy=policy or default_policy())
   57 |     store.add_reader(reader, category=category)
   58 |     return store, eng
   59 | 
   60 | 
   61 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   62 | # 1. Store + policy basics.
   63 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   64 | def store_and_policy_checks():
   65 |     print('-- store schema + policy defaults')
   66 |     store = CirculationStore(':memory:')
   67 |     store.add_reader('R1', category='В01')
   68 |     check('reader round-trips', store.get_reader('R1')['category'] == 'В01')
   69 | 
   70 |     loan = store.add_loan('R1', 'BK-1', T0 + 20 * DAY, T0)
   71 |     check('loan created on-hand', loan['returned'] == 0 and loan['renewals'] == 0)
   72 |     check('count_on_hand counts open loans', store.count_on_hand('R1') == 1)
   73 | 
   74 |     p = default_policy('tenantA')
   75 |     check('policy tenant id', p['tenant_id'] == 'tenantA')
   76 |     check('policy max_prolong default 5', p['limits']['В01']['max_prolong'] == 5)
   77 |     check('policy holds_block_renewal=block', p['renewal']['holds_block_renewal'] == 'block')
   78 |     check('policy lost_after_days=60', p['lost']['lost_after_days'] == 60)
```

```py
  100 |     check('6th checkout requires override (limit)', d6.decision == REQUIRE_OVERRIDE

  108 |                        override_grant=False).decision == DENY)

  111 | def checkout_debtor_checks():

  127 |

  132 |
```

```py
  229 | def fine_checks():

  240 |     check('accrue result reports days', res and res[0]['days'] == 9)
  241 | 
  242 |     # idempotent: re-running with the same today does not double the amount
  243 |     eng.accrue_fines(T0)
```

```py
  398 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  399 | # 7. Tenant policy isolation (AC7) + GUEST category.
  400 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  401 | def isolation_checks():
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_compliance.py`  · строк: 441

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """152-ФЗ / ФСТЭК compliance BACKEND tests — audit findings V1 / V5 / V9
    3 | (GitHub #199, epic #223; AUDIT_REPORT_IRBIS64_compliance.md; SPEC_compliance_152fz;
    4 | SPEC_pki_keys ADR-006). Exercises the three critical gaps closed in this slice:
    5 | 
    6 |   * V1 — field-level encryption of stored ПДн at rest. The reader display name in
    7 |     ``reader_review`` is ciphertext at rest (a raw dump never reveals it) and is
    8 |     decrypted only in the handler. The AES-GCM (AEAD) leg is SKIPPED-WITH-NOTE when
    9 |     the ``cryptography`` lib is absent (CI has no it) — the always-available dev
   10 |     fallback / pgcrypto leg still proves ciphertext-at-rest, so CI never fails for
   11 |     want of a cipher backend.
   12 |   * V5 — ПДн-access journal. A staff formulary read (GET /api/circ/reader) writes a
   13 |     ``pdn.read`` audit entry {actor, subject=ticket, action}; GET /api/admin/pdn-
   14 |     access returns it newest-first (super-admin only — 403 for regular staff /
   15 |     reader / guest). A reader reading their OWN data is NOT journaled.
   16 |   * V9 — consent + right-to-erasure. GET/POST /api/reader/consent records consent
   17 |     (append-only history); POST /api/reader/erase {confirm:true} deletes the authed
   18 |     reader's OWN reviews/holds/shelves/history/saved-searches/consent from OUR store
   19 |     (never the live ИРБИС RDR), returns per-table counts, audits, and a reader can
```

```py
  137 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  138 | # V1 — field-level ПДн encryption at rest (crypto round-trip + ciphertext at rest).
  139 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  140 | def crypto_checks():
  141 |     print('-- V1: crypto round-trip + ciphertext at rest (backend=%s, aead=%s)'
  142 |           % (crypto.backend(), crypto.aead_available()))
  143 | 
  144 |     # The always-available leg (dev fallback OR pgcrypto-key path): round-trip +
  145 |     # ciphertext (plaintext not a substring) + transparency for non-tokens.
  146 |     name = READER_NAME
  147 |     tok = crypto.encrypt(name)
  148 |     check('encrypt yields a pdn token', crypto.is_token(tok))
  149 |     check('round-trip decrypt == plaintext', crypto.decrypt(tok) == name)
  150 |     check('ciphertext at rest (name not in token)', name not in tok)
  151 |     check('empty/None pass through encrypt', crypto.encrypt('') == '' and crypto.encrypt(None) is None)
  152 |     check('legacy plaintext passes through decrypt', crypto.decrypt('Иванов И.') == 'Иванов И.')
  153 |     check('encrypt is idempotent (no double-wrap)', crypto.encrypt(tok) == tok)
  154 | 
  155 |     # Key ROTATION: a token written under one key id still reads after the active
  156 |     # id changes; new writes use the new id.
  157 |     old_id, old_k = os.environ.get('PDN_KEY_ID'), os.environ.get('PDN_KEY')
  158 |     try:
  159 |         os.environ['PDN_KEY_ID'] = 'a'
  160 |         os.environ['PDN_KEY_a'] = 'secret-a'
  161 |         ta = crypto.encrypt('РотацияA')
  162 |         os.environ['PDN_KEY_ID'] = 'b'
  163 |         os.environ['PDN_KEY_b'] = 'secret-b'
  164 |         tb = crypto.encrypt('РотацияB')
  165 |         check('rotated: old-id ciphertext still decrypts', crypto.decrypt(ta) == 'РотацияA')
  166 |         check('rotated: new-id ciphertext decrypts', crypto.decrypt(tb) == 'РотацияB')
  167 |         check('tokens carry distinct key ids', ta.split(':')[3] == 'a' and tb.split(':')[3] == 'b')
  168 |     finally:
  169 |         for k in ('PDN_KEY_a', 'PDN_KEY_b'):
  170 |             os.environ.pop(k, None)
  171 |         if old_id is None:
  172 |             os.environ.pop('PDN_KEY_ID', None)
  173 |         else:
  174 |             os.environ['PDN_KEY_ID'] = old_id
  175 |         if old_k is not None:
  176 |             os.environ['PDN_KEY'] = old_k
  177 | 
  178 |     # The AES-GCM (AEAD) leg — SKIP-WITH-NOTE when `cryptography` is absent.
  179 |     if crypto.aead_available():
  180 |         check('AEAD backend is AES-GCM', crypto.backend() == 'gcm')
  181 |         t = crypto.encrypt(name)
  182 |         check('AES-GCM token tagged gcm', t.split(':')[2] == 'gcm')
  183 |         check('AES-GCM round-trip', crypto.decrypt(t) == name)
  184 |     else:
```

```py
  276 | def consent_checks():
  277 |     print('-- V9: consent get/set (append-only history)')
  278 |     api, _core = _api()
  279 |     H = _reader_h(api, '111')
  280 | 
  281 |     # Unset → given:false.
  282 |     st, p = api.route('GET', '/api/reader/consent', {}, None, H)
  283 |     check('consent get (unset) 200 given:false', st == 200 and p['data']['given'] is False)
  284 |

  288 |     st, p = api.route('GET', '/api/reader/consent', {}, None, H)
  289 |     check('consent now reads granted', p['data']['given'] is True)
  290 |     check('consent carries version + ts', 'version' in p['data'] and p['data'].get('ts'))
  291 | 
  292 |     # Withdraw → latest row wins; prior granted row is kept (append-only).
  293 |     api.route('POST', '/api/reader/consent', {}, {'given': False}, H)
  294 |     st, p = api.route('GET', '/api/reader/consent', {}, None, H)
```

```py
  365 | 
  366 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  367 | # Postgres parity — pgcrypto encryption + consent + erasure on real PG.
  368 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  369 | def pg_parity_checks():
  370 |     # Gate on the PG intent. NB: a sibling suite (test_platform/test_admin_routes)
  371 |     # mutates os.environ['ACCESS_BACKEND'] to 'sqlite' before this module runs in
  372 |     # the test_access.py runner, so we must NOT rely on ACCESS_BACKEND alone — we
  373 |     # also honour an explicit ACCESS_PG_DSN (set by the CI postgres job) and
  374 |     # ACCESS_TEST_PG so the pgcrypto leg still runs there.
  375 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
  376 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1' \
  377 |         or bool(os.environ.get('ACCESS_PG_DSN'))
  378 |     if not want:
  379 |         return
  380 |     try:
  381 |         from access.pgstore import PgAccessStore, default_pg_dsn
  382 |         st = PgAccessStore(os.environ.get('ACCESS_PG_DSN', default_pg_dsn()))
  383 |         # Truncate audit_log too so the pdn.read journal assertion is isolation-safe
  384 |         # (this suite's own pdn.read entries go to an in-memory store, not PG).
  385 |         st._conn().execute('TRUNCATE reader_review, reader_hold, reader_shelf, '
  386 |                            'reader_shelf_item, reader_history, saved_search, '
  387 |                            'reader_consent, audit_log RESTART IDENTITY')
  388 |     except Exception as e:
  389 |         print('-- compliance pg parity SKIPPED (%s: %s)'
  390 |               % (type(e).__name__, str(e).splitlines()[0]))
  391 |         return
  392 |     print('-- compliance: postgres parity (V1 pgcrypto=%s / V5 / V9)'
  393 |           % getattr(st, '_pgcrypto', None))
  394 | 
  395 |     # V1 — reader_name encrypted at rest, decrypted on read (pgcrypto or token).
  396 |     row = st.review_upsert('pg-1', 'IBIS', 1, 5, 'ok', READER_NAME, 1000.0)
  397 |     check('[pg] review_upsert returns decrypted name', row['reader_name'] == READER_NAME)
  398 |     raw = st.review_name_ciphertext('pg-1', 'IBIS', 1)
  399 |     raw_s = raw.decode('utf-8', 'replace') if isinstance(raw, (bytes, bytearray)) else str(raw)
  400 |     check('[pg] stored reader_name is NOT plaintext', READER_NAME not in (raw_s or ''))
  401 |     check('[pg] reviews_for decrypts name',
  402 |           st.reviews_for('IBIS', 1)[0]['reader_name'] == READER_NAME)
  403 |     check('[pg] review_mine decrypts name',
  404 |           st.review_mine('pg-1', 'IBIS', 1)['reader_name'] == READER_NAME)
```

```py
    4 | Публичная страница продукта Biblio (`/product`) несёт форму-заявку на демодоступ.
```

```py
  219 | def _pg_reachable_dsn():

  228 |         conn = pgstore._admin_conn(dsn)

  230 |         conn.close()
  231 |         return dsn
  232 |     except Exception as e:
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Discovery / façade backend endpoint tests (reader-portal homepage surfaces).
    3 | 
    4 | Covers the four discovery endpoints added to ``core.Api`` that power a richer
```

<!-- ─── страница 456 ─── -->

```py
    5 | reader portal:
    6 | 
    7 |   1. GET /api/showcase   — "new arrivals / featured" brief cards (kind new|popular).
    8 |   2. GET /api/rubricator — browse a classification/navigator dictionary (terms+counts).
    9 |   3. GET /api/databases  — enriched with a per-DB ``count`` (additive, back-compat).
   10 |   4. GET /api/example-queries — seeded example search chips per DB.
   11 | 
   12 | House style mirrors tests/test_flk.py::api_checks — a constructed ``Api`` (whose
   13 | constructor does NOT connect to IRBIS) with a small in-memory ``FakeIrbis`` stub
   14 | injected over ``api.irbis``. So the suite is fully sqlite-green and requires NO
   15 | live ИРБИС server; the IRBIS-dependent branches are exercised against the stub,
   16 | and the "server briefly absent" path is exercised by a stub that raises
   17 | IrbisError — proving each endpoint degrades to an empty list rather than 500.
   18 | 
   19 | Invoked by the tests/test_access.py runner via the generic ``module_checks`` loop
   20 | (it imports the module and calls every ``*_checks()`` it defines, folding the
   21 | PASS/FAIL tally in), so the existing CI step that runs test_access.py also runs
   22 | this suite with no .github/ workflow change.
   23 | 
   24 | Standalone too::
   25 | 
   26 |     py -3.12 tests/test_discovery.py   ->  'ok …' lines + 'N passed, M failed'
   27 | """

   29 | import sys
   30 |

   33 | import importlib

   36 | FAIL = [0]

   45 |         print('  FAIL', name)

   47 | 
   48 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   49 | # A tiny in-memory IRBIS stub: just the SessionManager surface the discovery
   50 | # endpoints touch. Records are shaped like irbis.parser.parse_record output
   51 | # (dict with a 'fields' list) so _brief_item works unchanged.
   52 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   53 | def _fld(tag, value, subfields=None):
   54 |     return {'tag': tag, 'value': value, 'text': value, 'subfields': subfields or {}}
   55 | 
   56 | 
   57 | def _record(mfn, title, author=None, year=None, cover=False):
   58 |     fields = [_fld('200', title, {'A': title})]
   59 |     if author:
```

<!-- ─── страница 457 ─── -->

```py
   60 |         fields.append(_fld('700', author, {'A': author}))
   61 |     if year:
   62 |         fields.append(_fld('210', year, {'D': year}))
   63 |     if cover:
   64 |         fields.append(_fld('953', 'cover', {'A': 'JPG', 'B': 'xx'}))
   65 |     return {'mfn': mfn, 'status': '0', 'version': '1', 'guid': None, 'fields': fields}
   66 | 
   67 | 
   68 | class FakeIrbis:
   69 |     """In-memory stand-in for SessionManager. ``raise_on`` names methods that
   70 |     should raise IrbisError (to exercise the graceful-degradation branches)."""
   71 | 
   72 |     def __init__(self, maxmfn=None, terms=None, dbmenu='', raise_on=()):
   73 |         # max_mfn returns the NEXT free MFN (one past the last real record),
   74 |         # mirroring the real 'O' command — so 5 records => maxmfn 6.
   75 |         self._maxmfn = maxmfn if maxmfn is not None else {}
   76 |         self._terms = terms or {}            # db -> [(count, term), ...] (sorted)
   77 |         self._dbmenu = dbmenu
   78 |         self.raise_on = set(raise_on)
   79 | 
   80 |     def _maybe_raise(self, name):
   81 |         if name in self.raise_on:
   82 |             from irbis.client import IrbisError
   83 |             raise IrbisError(-1, 'stub raise %s' % name)
   84 | 
   85 |     def max_mfn(self, db):
   86 |         self._maybe_raise('max_mfn')
   87 |         return self._maxmfn.get(db, 1)
   88 | 
   89 |     def read_record(self, db, mfn):
   90 |         self._maybe_raise('read_record')
   91 |         return _record(mfn, 'Заглавие %d' % mfn, author='Автор %d' % mfn,
   92 |                        year='20%02d' % (mfn % 100), cover=(mfn % 2 == 0))
   93 | 
   94 |     def read_terms(self, db, start, count=20):
   95 |         self._maybe_raise('read_terms')
   96 |         rows = self._terms.get(db, [])
   97 |         # mimic 'H': return up to ``count`` terms at-or-after ``start``.
   98 |         out = [(c, t) for (c, t) in rows if t >= start]
   99 |         return out[:count]
  100 | 
  101 |     def read_file(self, spec):
  102 |         self._maybe_raise('read_file')
  103 |         return self._dbmenu
  104 | 
  105 |     def format_record(self, db, mfn, pft='@brief'):
  106 |         return ''
  107 | 
  108 | 
  109 | def _api(fake):
  110 |     """Construct an Api (no IRBIS connection happens in __init__) and swap its
  111 |     session manager for the stub. JWT secret pinned for determinism."""
  112 |     os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] = 'discovery-test-secret'
  113 |     import core as _core
  114 |     importlib.reload(_core)
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_encoding.py`  · строк: 57

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Configurable bibliographic encoding (#228).
    3 | 
    4 | The IRBIS client historically hard-coded UTF-8 for both the response decode and
    5 | the request encode. A classic ИРБИС install keeps its inverted index + records in
    6 | CP1251, so UTF-8 decoding garbled the dictionary/records and UTF-8-encoded query
    7 | terms never matched the CP1251 index (cyrillic search -> 0). Encoding is now
    8 | configurable (env IRBIS_ENCODING; Config -> SessionManager -> IrbisClient ->
    9 | Response/_execute). read_file (resources) stays CP1251 regardless (uses raw bytes).
   10 | 
   11 | Standalone:  py tests/test_encoding.py
   12 | """
   13 | import os
   14 | import sys
   15 | 
   16 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   17 | 
   18 | from irbis.client import Response, IrbisClient
   19 | 
   20 | PASS = [0]
   21 | FAIL = [0]
   22 | 
   23 | 
   24 | def check(name, cond):
   25 |     if cond:
   26 |         PASS[0] += 1
```

<!-- ─── страница 463 ─── -->

```py
   27 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   28 |     else:
   29 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   30 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   31 | 
   32 | 
   33 | def encoding_checks():
   34 |     print('-- irbis encoding (#228): configurable bibliographic encoding')
   35 |     cyr = 'ЧЕХОВ'
   36 |     # Minimal frame: 11 leading fields (return_code slot etc.) then the term as data.
   37 |     frame = ('\r\n'.join([''] * 11) + cyr).encode('cp1251')
   38 |     check('Response(cp1251) decodes cyrillic correctly', Response(frame, 'cp1251').lines[-1] == cyr)
   39 |     check('Response(utf-8) garbles cp1251 bytes (the bug)', Response(frame, 'utf-8').lines[-1] != cyr)
   40 |     check('Response default encoding stays utf-8', Response(b'x').lines[0] == 'x')
   41 |     check('IrbisClient stores encoding=cp1251', IrbisClient(encoding='cp1251').encoding == 'cp1251')
   42 |     check('IrbisClient default encoding is utf-8', IrbisClient().encoding == 'utf-8')
   43 |     # Config reads IRBIS_ENCODING from env, default utf-8.
   44 |     import importlib
   45 |     import config as _c
   46 |     os.environ['IRBIS_ENCODING'] = 'cp1251'
   47 |     importlib.reload(_c)
   48 |     check('Config reads IRBIS_ENCODING=cp1251', _c.Config().irbis_encoding == 'cp1251')
   49 |     del os.environ['IRBIS_ENCODING']
   50 |     importlib.reload(_c)
   51 |     check('Config default encoding is utf-8', _c.Config().irbis_encoding == 'utf-8')
   52 | 
   53 | 
   54 | if __name__ == '__main__':
   55 |     encoding_checks()
   56 |     print('\nENCODING: %d passed, %d failed' % (PASS[0], FAIL[0]))
   57 |     sys.exit(1 if FAIL[0] else 0)
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """HTTP-route-тесты движков СВЯЗНОСТИ, выведенных в core.Api.route() (эпик #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Три «готовых» движка landed-ранее (их сами НЕ правим) теперь достижимы по HTTP —
    5 | каждое утверждение идёт через настоящий ``core.Api.route()`` (тот же путь, что у
    6 | server.py / app_aiohttp.py), с СКОНСТРУИРОВАННЫМ ``Api`` (конструктор НЕ
    7 | подключается к ИРБИС).
    8 | 
    9 | Покрыто (контракт задачи):
   10 |   * GET /api/linked/{db}/{mfn}?kind=children|host — обход связи иерархии
   11 |     (catalog.linked_records). ПУБЛИЧНЫЙ: форма ответа {db,mfn,kind,total,items},
   12 |     public-db guard (гость на непубличную БД → 403), мягкая деградация
   13 |     (несуществующий mfn / нет каталог-движка → {total:0,items:[]}, не 500).
   14 |   * GET /api/fulltext/{db}/{mfn} — артефакты ПТ (fulltext.artifacts_for) + уровень
   15 |     доступа (rights.access_level/page_limit, lich.download_budget). ПУБЛИЧНЫЙ:
   16 |     форма {db,mfn,artifacts:[{kind,ref,pages,rightsTemplate}],access:{level,
   17 |     pageLimit,downloadBudget}}, public-db guard, гейтинг по категории читателя
   18 |     (deny/view/download), квота скачивания только для читателя (по билету; чужой
   19 |     LICH-счётчик не светим), мягкая деградация (нет артефактов → []; пустой
   20 |     шаблон → download без 500).
   21 |   * GET /api/bp/provision?discipline=|specialty=|faculty= — быстрый отчёт ККО
   22 |     (bookprovision.*_provision). STAFF-grant: не-staff (гость/читатель) → 403;
   23 |     форма BpProvisionReport (coefficient/status/shortfall/bindings); неизвестный
   24 |     id → 404 (не 500).
```

```py
   90 |             flds.append({'tag': '50', 'value': str(cat), 'subfields': {}})
   91 |         return {'mfn': mfn, 'status': '0', 'version': '1', 'guid': None,
   92 |                 'fields': flds}
   93 | 
   94 |     def format_record(self, db, mfn, pft='@brief'):
   95 |         return ''
   96 | 
   97 | 
   98 | def _api(categories=None, raise_on=()):
   99 |     """Сконструированный Api без живого ИРБИС, со свежими in-memory сторами движков.
  100 | 
  101 |     Каталог — общий seam: linked_records, fulltext (955/955^B) и rights (резолв
  102 |     955^B) ходят в ОДИН CatalogStore. Заглушка irbis отдаёт RDR (категория)."""
  103 |     os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] = 'engine-routes-test-secret'
  104 |     os.environ['ACCESS_DB'] = ':memory:'
  105 |     import core as _core
  106 |     importlib.reload(_core)
  107 |     api = _core.Api()
  108 |     api.irbis = FakeRdrIrbis(categories=categories, raise_on=raise_on)
  109 |     # Свежие in-memory сторы — каждая группа стартует изолированно и пусто.
  110 |     seed_vocab.seed_vocabularies(api.access, from_catalog=False)
  111 |     api.catalog = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=api.access)
  112 |     api.fulltext = _ft.FulltextRegistry(store=_ft.FulltextStore(':memory:'),
  113 |                                         catalog=api.catalog)
  114 |     api.rights = _rights.RightService(store=_rights.RightStore(':memory:'),
  115 |                                       catalog=api.catalog)
  116 |     api.lich = _lich.LichService(_lich.LichStore(':memory:'), rights=api.rights)
  117 |     api.bookprovision = _bp.BookProvisionEngine(':memory:', catalog=api.catalog)
  118 |     return api, _core
  119 | 
  120 | 
  121 | def _staff(api, login='ko'):
  122 |     tok, _ = api._new_session('staff', login, STAFF_GRANTS, tenant='public')
  123 |     return {'authorization': 'Bearer ' + tok}
  124 | 
  125 | 
  126 | def _guest(api):
  127 |     tok, _ = api._new_session('guest', 'guest', GUEST_GRANTS, tenant='public')
  128 |     return {'authorization': 'Bearer ' + tok}
  129 | 
  130 | 
  131 | def _reader(api, ticket='111', rdr_mfn=1):
  132 |     tok, _ = api._new_session('reader', 'RI=%s' % ticket, READER_GRANTS,
  133 |                               tenant='public', rdr_mfn=rdr_mfn)
  134 |     return {'authorization': 'Bearer ' + tok}
```

```py
  331 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  332 | # 3. /api/bp/provision — быстрый отчёт ККО (staff-gate, форма, 404).
  333 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  334 | def _seed_bp(api, students=100, copies=40):
  335 |     """Связка факультет→специальность→дисциплина + привязка литературы.
  336 | 
  337 |     Кко = copies/students. По умолчанию 40/100 = 0.4 (< норма 0.5 → дефицит)."""
  338 |     fid = api.bookprovision.add_faculty('ФВТ', name='Факультет ВТ')
  339 |     sid = api.bookprovision.add_specialty(fid, napr='09.03.01', spec='ИВТ')
  340 |     did = api.bookprovision.add_discipline(sid, 'D-1', name='Программирование',
  341 |                                            students=students)
  342 |     api.bookprovision.bind_literature(did, 'Учебник', kind=_bp.KIND_MAIN, copies=copies)
  343 |     return fid, sid, did
  344 | 
  345 | 
  346 | def bp_provision_route_checks():
  347 |     print('-- bp/provision: отчёт ККО через route() (staff)')

  350 |     H = _staff(api)
  351 | 
  352 |     # discipline scope → дефицит (Кко 0.4 < 0.5).
  353 |     st, p = api.route('GET', '/api/bp/provision', {'discipline': [str(did)]}, None, H)
  354 |     check('GET /api/bp/provision?discipline= -> 200', st == 200)
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_flk.py`  · строк: 289

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Declarative ФЛК validation-engine tests (gap A2, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Covers the first shippable slice of the engine layered on the merged A5 seeding
    5 | (seed_vocab / vocabulary tables), tenancy (#100) and Identity (#101):
    6 | 
    7 |   1. Check digits (pure): the real ISBN-10 / ISBN-13 / ISSN check-digit algorithms
    8 |      accept known-good and reject known-bad identifiers.
    9 |   2. Engine (pure, with an in-memory seeded store): a valid record passes; missing
   10 |      200^a -> severity-1; a bad language code -> dictionary violation (against the
   11 |      A5-seeded jz.mnu); a bad ISBN checksum is caught; 920-branching selects the
   12 |      correct rules; a type-2 warning leaves the record saveable; per-tenant override
   13 |      softens / disables a rule.
   14 |   3. API (pure, constructed Api — no live IRBIS): POST /api/validate is tenant-
   15 |      scoped via the JWT claim and guarded like save_record; returns violations.
   16 | 
   17 | Invoked by the test_access.py runner (flk_checks) so the CI postgres step runs it
   18 | too with NO .github/ change. The sqlite suite stays green locally with no DB; the
   19 | PG-only path skips cleanly when postgres is unavailable.
   20 | """
   21 | import os
```

```py
   48 | 
   49 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   50 | # 1. Check digits — pure, backend-independent.
   51 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   52 | def checksum_checks():
   53 |     print('-- checksums (isbn/issn)')
   54 |     # ISBN-10: valid (5-7654-0001-9 has the correct mod-11 check digit).
   55 |     check('isbn-10 valid', flk.isbn_checksum_ok('5-7654-0001-9'))
   56 |     # Flip the check digit -> invalid.
   57 |     check('isbn-10 bad check digit rejected', not flk.isbn_checksum_ok('5-7654-0001-0'))
   58 |     # 'X' as the check digit is the literal value 10 — valid where it balances mod 11.
   59 |     check('isbn-10 X check digit valid', flk.isbn_checksum_ok('0-8044-2957-X'))
   60 |     # A well-known valid ISBN-10 and ISBN-13.
   61 |     check('isbn-10 0306406152 valid', flk.isbn_checksum_ok('0-306-40615-2'))
   62 |     check('isbn-13 9780306406157 valid', flk.isbn_checksum_ok('978-0-306-40615-7'))
   63 |     check('isbn-13 bad rejected', not flk.isbn_checksum_ok('978-0-306-40615-0'))
   64 |     check('isbn empty rejected', not flk.isbn_checksum_ok(''))
   65 |     check('isbn wrong length rejected', not flk.isbn_checksum_ok('12345'))
   66 |     # ISSN: 0378-5955 is a textbook-valid ISSN.
   67 |     check('issn 0378-5955 valid', flk.issn_checksum_ok('0378-5955'))
   68 |     check('issn bad rejected', not flk.issn_checksum_ok('0378-5950'))
   69 | 
   70 | 
   71 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   72 | # 2. Engine — pure, with a seeded in-memory store.
   73 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   74 | def engine_checks():
   75 |     print('-- engine (validate)')
   76 |     store = _seeded_store()
   77 | 
   78 |     # A clean, valid book record: 920 worklist, title, language, good ISBN, executor.
   79 |     good = {
   80 |         '920': 'PAZK',
   81 |         '200': {'a': 'Основы каталогизации'},
   82 |         '101': 'rus',
   83 |         '10': {'a': '5-7654-0001-9'},
   84 |         '907': {'a': 'Иванова И.И.'},
   85 |     }
   86 |     res = flk.validate(good, phase='save', store=store)
   87 |     check('valid record passes (overall 0)', res['overallSeverity'] == flk.SEV_PASS)
   88 |     check('valid record canSave', res['canSave'] is True)
   89 |     check('valid record has no violations', res['violations'] == [])
   90 | 
   91 |     # Missing 200^a on a non-аналитика book -> severity-1 (непреодолимо), blocks save.
   92 |     no_title = dict(good)
   93 |     no_title.pop('200')
   94 |     res = flk.validate(no_title, phase='save', store=store)
   95 |     ids = [v['ruleId'] for v in res['violations']]
   96 |     check('missing 200^a -> rec.200a.mandatory fires', 'rec.200a.mandatory' in ids)
   97 |     title_v = [v for v in res['violations'] if v['ruleId'] == 'rec.200a.mandatory'][0]
   98 |     check('missing 200^a is severity 1', title_v['severity'] == flk.SEV_HARD)
   99 |     check('missing 200^a blocks save (canSave False)', res['canSave'] is False)
  100 |     check('missing 200^a violation bound to path 200/a', title_v['path'] == '200/a')
  101 | 
  102 |     # Bad language code -> dictionary violation against the A5-seeded jz.mnu.
```

```py
  117 |     res = flk.validate(bad_isbn, phase='fieldExit', field='10', store=store)

  121 |     check('ISBN violation is severity 2 (преодолимо)', isbn_v['severity'] == flk.SEV_SOFT)
  122 |     # Good ISBN passes the same fieldExit run.

  126 |     # 920-branching: rec.200a.mandatory (branch negate NJ*/A*) must NOT fire on an

  132 | 
  133 |     analyt_no_title = {'920': 'ASP', '101': 'rus', '907': {'a': 'X'}}
  134 |     res = flk.validate(analyt_no_title, phase='save', store=store)
  135 |     ids = [v['ruleId'] for v in res['violations']]
  136 |     check('920=ASP (A*) selects analytics-softened title rule',

  138 |     analyt_v = [v for v in res['violations']
  139 |                 if v['ruleId'] == 'rec.200a.mandatory.analyt'][0]
  140 |     check('analytics title rule is severity 2', analyt_v['severity'] == flk.SEV_SOFT)
  141 |

  143 |     # ASP record without 200^a yields ONLY soft (analytics title + nothing hard).
  144 |     check('type-2-only record stays saveable',
  145 |           res['canSave'] is True and res['overallSeverity'] == flk.SEV_SOFT)
  146 |

  155 |     check('violations sorted severity-1 first',
```

```py
  177 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  178 | # 2b. Per-tenant override (SPEC §3).
  179 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  180 | def override_checks():
  181 |     print('-- per-tenant override')
  182 |     store = _seeded_store()
  183 |     no_title = {'920': 'PAZK', '101': 'rus', '907': {'a': 'X'}}
  184 | 
  185 |     # Baseline: missing title is hard (blocks).
  186 |     base = flk.validate(no_title, phase='save', store=store)
  187 |     check('baseline missing title blocks', base['canSave'] is False)
  188 | 
  189 |     # Soften rec.200a.mandatory 1 -> 2: now the record is saveable (with warning).
  190 |     overrides = {'rec.200a.mandatory': {'severity': flk.SEV_SOFT}}
  191 |     rs = flk.load_ruleset(tenant_overrides=overrides)
  192 |     soft = flk.validate(no_title, phase='save', store=store, ruleset=rs)
  193 |     title_v = [v for v in soft['violations'] if v['ruleId'] == 'rec.200a.mandatory'][0]
  194 |     check('override softens 200a to severity 2', title_v['severity'] == flk.SEV_SOFT)
  195 |     check('softened rule no longer blocks save', soft['canSave'] is True)
  196 | 
  197 |     # Hardening is refused by default (canon severity kept).
  198 |     rs_h = flk.load_ruleset(tenant_overrides={'fld.10.isbn.checksum': {'severity': flk.SEV_HARD}})
  199 |     isbn_rule = [r for r in rs_h if r['id'] == 'fld.10.isbn.checksum'][0]
  200 |     check('hardening refused by default (severity kept 2)',
  201 |           isbn_rule['severity'] == flk.SEV_SOFT)
  202 |     # ...unless explicitly allowed.
  203 |     rs_h2 = flk.load_ruleset(tenant_overrides={'fld.10.isbn.checksum': {'severity': flk.SEV_HARD}},
  204 |                              allow_hardening=True)
  205 |     isbn_rule2 = [r for r in rs_h2 if r['id'] == 'fld.10.isbn.checksum'][0]
  206 |     check('hardening applied when allow_hardening', isbn_rule2['severity'] == flk.SEV_HARD)
  207 | 
  208 |     # Disable a rule: it no longer fires.
  209 |     rs_d = flk.load_ruleset(tenant_overrides={'rec.200a.mandatory': {'enabled': False}})
  210 |     disabled = flk.validate(no_title, phase='save', store=store, ruleset=rs_d)
  211 |     check('disabled rule does not fire',
  212 |           'rec.200a.mandatory' not in [v['ruleId'] for v in disabled['violations']])
```

```py
  267 |     try:
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_fulltext.py`  · строк: 382

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """FULLTEXT — реестр артефактов ПТ 951/953/955 (кластер 7, ребро 7.1) — тест.
    3 | 
    4 | Покрывает ``access/fulltext.py`` (хранение/резолв артефактов полных текстов /
    5 | образов на запись каталога — дополняет ПТ↔права-гейтинг rights.py/lich.py):
    6 | 
    7 |   * ``blob_ref`` — blob-референс для 953 (путь+хэш+размер), сырые байты НЕ хранятся;
    8 |   * ``FulltextStore`` — дуальный стор: attach/for_record/delete_record/count (sqlite);
    9 |   * ``FulltextRegistry.attach`` — завести 951 (ссылка) / 953 (blob-реф) / 955
   10 |     (метаданные: файл, страницы, шаблон прав 955^B);
   11 |   * ``artifacts_for`` — единый список (стор + каталог), нормализованная форма
   12 |     (kind/source/ref/pages/rights_template/data); несколько артефактов на запись;
   13 |   * ``page_count`` — число страниц 955^N (максимум среди 955-артефактов);
   14 |   * ``rights_template_for`` — 955^B наружу для связки с rights (гейтинг тут НЕ делается);
   15 |   * резолв 951/953/955 каталожной записи через опц. catalog-handle (get(db,mfn));
   16 |   * back-compat без handle И без стора → пустой список;
   17 |   * PG-ПАРИТЕТ — когда postgres доступен (иначе чисто пропускается).
   18 | 
   19 | Вписан в раннер tests/test_access.py (его module-list) через ``module_checks`` —
   20 | раннер вызывает каждую ``*_checks()`` и складывает PASS/FAIL.
   21 | 
   22 | Standalone:  py tests/test_fulltext.py
   23 | PG:          (set ACCESS_BACKEND=postgres) py -3.12 tests/test_fulltext.py
   24 | """
   25 | import hashlib
   26 | import os
   27 | import sys
   28 | 
   29 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   30 | 
   31 | from access.fulltext import (FulltextStore, FulltextRegistry, blob_ref,
   32 |                              KIND_LINK, KIND_BLOB, KIND_META, KINDS)
   33 |
```

```py
   50 | def blob_ref_checks():

   53 |     ref = blob_ref(data=data, path='s3://bucket/scan.pdf', content_type='application/pdf')

   55 |     check('blob_ref считает sha256 из data',

   57 |     check('blob_ref считает size из data', ref['size'] == len(data))
   58 |     check('blob_ref несёт content-type', ref['contentType'] == 'application/pdf')
   59 |     # Сырые байты НЕ попадают в референс (раздувание БД недопустимо).

   61 |           'data' not in ref and data not in tuple(ref.values()))

   64 |     check('blob_ref берёт готовый sha256', ref2['sha256'] == 'deadbeef')
   65 |     # Пустой вход → пустой dict.
```

```py
  207 | class _FakeCatalog:
  208 |     """Минимальный catalog-handle: get(db, mfn) -> record (как CatalogStore.get)."""
  209 |     def __init__(self, records):
  210 |         self._records = records  # {(db, mfn): record-dict}
  211 | 
  212 |     def get(self, db, mfn):
  213 |         return self._records.get((db, mfn))
```

```py
  313 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  314 | # 7. PG-ПАРИТЕТ — когда postgres доступен; иначе чисто пропускается.
  315 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  316 | def _pg_reachable_dsn():
  317 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
  318 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1'
  319 |     if not want:
  320 |         return None
  321 |     try:
  322 |         from access import pgstore
  323 |         dsn = pgstore.default_pg_dsn()
  324 |         conn = pgstore._admin_conn(dsn)
  325 |         conn.execute('DROP TABLE IF EXISTS fulltext_artifact')
  326 |         conn.close()
  327 |         return dsn
  328 |     except Exception as e:
  329 |         print('-- fulltext: postgres SKIPPED (%s: %s)'
  330 |               % (type(e).__name__, str(e).splitlines()[0]))
  331 |         return None
  332 | 
  333 | 
  334 | def pg_parity_checks():
  335 |     dsn = _pg_reachable_dsn()
  336 |     if dsn is None:
  337 |         return
  338 |     print('-- fulltext: store (postgres parity)')
  339 |     st = FulltextStore(dsn, backend='postgres')
  340 |     reg = FulltextRegistry(store=st)
  341 |     check('[pg] empty count == 0', st.count() == 0)
  342 | 
  343 |     reg.attach('IBIS', 10, KIND_META, file='book.pdf', pages=42, rights_template='R1')
  344 |     reg.attach('IBIS', 10, KIND_LINK, url='https://x/y.pdf')
  345 |     blob = reg.attach('IBIS', 10, KIND_BLOB, res_type='JPG', data=b'bytes', path='s3://b/x')
  346 |     check('[pg] count == 3', st.count() == 3)
  347 |     check('[pg] blob-реф sha256 на PG',
  348 |           blob['data']['blob']['sha256'] == hashlib.sha256(b'bytes').hexdigest())
  349 | 
  350 |     arts = reg.artifacts_for('IBIS', 10)
  351 |     check('[pg] artifacts_for == 3', len(arts) == 3)
  352 |     check('[pg] page_count = 42', reg.page_count('IBIS', 10) == 42)
  353 |     check('[pg] rights_template_for = R1', reg.rights_template_for('IBIS', 10) == 'R1')
  354 |     check('[pg] mfn int round-trip', arts[0]['mfn'] == 10)
  355 | 
  356 |     check('[pg] delete_record == 3', st.delete_record('IBIS', 10) == 3)
  357 |     check('[pg] after delete count == 0', st.count() == 0)
  358 | 
  359 |     # cleanup
  360 |     try:
  361 |         from access import pgstore
  362 |         conn = pgstore._admin_conn(dsn)
  363 |         conn.execute('DROP TABLE IF EXISTS fulltext_artifact')
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_gbl.py`  · строк: 590

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Global-correction ``.gbl`` executor tests (gap A3, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Covers the first shippable slice of the A3 batch-correction engine
    5 | (``access.gbl``), which is self-contained pure stdlib and runs with NO PFT (A1)
    6 | engine present — ФОРМАТ evaluation is injected as a literal/field stub so the
    7 | operator semantics can be exercised in isolation:
    8 | 
    9 |   1. Parser (pure): the parameter header + the 5-line field operators
   10 |      (ADD/REP/CHA/CHAC/DEL) + the IF…FI / REPEAT…UNTIL blocks parse into the AST
   11 |      with the right arity; XXXX… / blank lines normalise to ∅; malformed jobs
   12 |      (unknown operator, unclosed block, %n over the declared count) raise.
   13 |   2. Apply (pure): ADD a field, REP replace a field & a subfield, REP-empty
   14 |      deletes, DEL a field, CHA/CHAC substring replace, IF gates an op, a small
   15 |      multi-operator job — each over a copy (the input record is never mutated).
   16 |   3. format_eval boundary: an explicit ctx hook is used; the stub evaluator
   17 |      resolves when no hook and no access.pft is importable.
   18 |   4. Preview (pure): a dry-run yields a correct field-level diff WITHOUT mutating
   19 |      the input records (SPEC AC5).
   20 | 
   21 | Standalone-runnable in the house style of tests/test_seeding.py:
   22 | ``py -3.12 tests/test_gbl.py`` -> ``ok …`` lines + ``N passed, M failed`` + exit
   23 | code. No DB, no network, no PFT engine required.
   24 | """
   25 | import os
   26 | import sys
   27 | 
   28 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   29 | from access import gbl
   30 | 
   31 | PASS = [0]
   32 | FAIL = [0]
   33 | 
   34 | 
   35 | def check(name, cond):
   36 |     if cond:
```

```py
   43 | 
   44 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   45 | # Literal/field format_eval stub for the tests — so they never depend on a full
   46 | # PFT engine. Understands: '' / '#' -> ∅; quoted literal 'x' -> x (with %n);
   47 | # bare vNNN[^s] -> first repetition value; '1'/'0' flags pass through. This is
   48 | # the same contract access.gbl.stub_format_eval offers, made explicit in ctx.
   49 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   50 | def lit_eval(fmt, record, ctx):
   51 |     return gbl.stub_format_eval(fmt, record, ctx)
   52 | 
   53 | 
   54 | def by_format_eval(mapping):
   55 |     """Build a format_eval that returns a fixed multi-line string for a marker
   56 |     format (so F-mode tests are deterministic without PFT)."""
   57 |     def _e(fmt, record, ctx):
   58 |         if fmt in mapping:
   59 |             return mapping[fmt]
   60 |         return gbl.stub_format_eval(fmt, record, ctx)
   61 |     return _e
   62 | 
   63 | 
   64 | def rec(d):
   65 |     return gbl.normalize_record(d)
   66 | 
   67 | 
   68 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   69 | # 1. Parser.
   70 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   71 | def parser_checks():
   72 |     print('-- parser (.gbl -> AST)')
   73 | 
   74 |     # Header: 0 params, one ADD operator (5 lines).
   75 |     job = "0\nADD\n910\n\n'X'\n\n"
   76 |     prog = gbl.parse(job)
   77 |     check('parse no-params header', prog['params'] == [])
   78 |     check('parse one ADD op', len(prog['body']) == 1 and prog['body'][0]['kind'] == 'ADD')
   79 |     op = prog['body'][0]
   80 |     check('parse ADD tag/occ/fmt1', op['tag'] == '910'
   81 |           and op['occ'][0] == gbl.OCC_ALL and op['fmt1'] == "'X'")
   82 | 
   83 |     # Parameter header: 1 param with a caption, then a field op.
   84 |     job_p = ("1\n\nВведите дату\nDEL\n40\n*\nXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX\n"
   85 |              "XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX\n")
   86 |     prog_p = gbl.parse(job_p)
   87 |     check('parse 1 param', len(prog_p['params']) == 1
   88 |           and prog_p['params'][0]['caption'] == 'Введите дату')
   89 |     check('parse prompt param source', prog_p['params'][0]['source'] == 'prompt')
   90 |     delop = prog_p['body'][0]
   91 |     check('parse DEL op', delop['kind'] == 'DEL' and delop['tag'] == '40')
```

```py
  109 |     check('parse IF block', ifop['kind'] == 'IF' and len(ifop['body']) == 1)
  110 |     check('parse IF body is ADD', ifop['body'][0]['kind'] == 'ADD')
  111 |     check('parse FI not a node', all(o['kind'] != 'FI' for o in prog_if['body']))
  112 | 
  113 |     # REPEAT…UNTIL.
  114 |     job_rep = "0\nREPEAT\nDEL\n910\n1\n\n\nUNTIL\nif p(v910) then '1' fi\n"
  115 |     rop = gbl.parse(job_rep)['body'][0]
  116 |     check('parse REPEAT block', rop['kind'] == 'REPEAT' and len(rop['body']) == 1)
  117 |     check('parse UNTIL captured', "p(v910)" in rop['until'])
  118 | 
  119 |     # PUTLOG and comment.
  120 |     job_misc = "0\n//a comment\nPUTLOG\n'hi'\n"
  121 |     prog_misc = gbl.parse(job_misc)
  122 |     kinds = [o['kind'] for o in prog_misc['body']]
  123 |     check('parse comment node', 'COMMENT' in kinds)
  124 |     check('parse PUTLOG node', 'PUTLOG' in kinds)
  125 | 
  126 |     # CP1251 bytes decode on input.
  127 |     raw = "0\nADD\n900\n\n'тест'\n\n".encode('cp1251')
  128 |     op_b = gbl.parse(raw)['body'][0]
  129 |     check('parse decodes cp1251 bytes', op_b['fmt1'] == "'тест'")
  130 | 
  131 |     # --- error cases ---
  132 |     check('unknown operator raises', _raises(gbl.ParseError, gbl.parse, "0\nFOO\n"))
  133 |     check('unclosed IF raises',
  134 |           _raises(gbl.ParseError, gbl.parse, "0\nIF\nif x then '1' fi\nADD\n9\n\n'a'\n\n"))
  135 |     check('stray FI raises', _raises(gbl.ParseError, gbl.parse, "0\nFI\n"))
  136 |     check('%n over declared count raises',
  137 |           _raises(gbl.ParseError, gbl.parse, "0\nADD\n910\n\n'%1'\n\n"))
  138 |     check('non-int param count raises', _raises(gbl.ParseError, gbl.parse, "x\n"))
  139 | 
  140 |     # NEWMFN…END parses with the db-name format header + validated body (executes).
  141 |     job_nm = "0\nNEWMFN\n'*'\nADD\n920\n\n'SZ'\n\nEND\n"
  142 |     nm = gbl.parse(job_nm)['body'][0]
  143 |     check('parse NEWMFN block', nm['kind'] == 'NEWMFN' and nm.get('supported') is not False)
  144 |     check('parse NEWMFN dbFmt', nm['dbFmt'] == "'*'")
  145 |     check('parse NEWMFN body validated', len(nm['body']) == 1)
  146 | 
```

```py
  147 |     # CORREC…END: db / key→1001 / terms (+opt limit) header, then body to END.

  172 | def _raises(exc, fn, *a):
  173 |     try:
  174 |         fn(*a)
  175 |         return False
  176 |     except exc:
  177 |         return True
  178 |     except Exception:
  179 |         return False

  182 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  183 | # 2. Apply — operator semantics over a copy.
  184 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  185 | def apply_checks():
  186 |     print('-- apply (operators)')
  187 |     ctx = {'format_eval': lit_eval}
  188 | 
  189 |     # ADD a whole field.
  190 |     prog = gbl.parse("0\nADD\n910\n\n'1024365'\n\n")
  191 |     r = gbl.apply(prog, {'200': [{'a': 'Книга'}]}, ctx)
  192 |     check('ADD creates field', gbl.field_values(r, '910') == ['1024365'])
  193 |     check('ADD keeps other fields', gbl.field_values(r, '200', 'a') == ['Книга'])
  194 | 
  195 |     # ADD a subfield to an existing repetition.
  196 |     prog = gbl.parse("0\nADD\n200^f\n1\n'Иванов'\n\n")
  197 |     r = gbl.apply(prog, {'200': [{'a': 'Заглавие'}]}, ctx)
  198 |     check('ADD subfield', gbl.field_values(r, '200', 'f') == ['Иванов']
  199 |           and gbl.field_values(r, '200', 'a') == ['Заглавие'])
  200 | 
  201 |     # REP replaces a whole field.
```

<!-- ─── страница 487 ─── -->

```py
  202 |     prog = gbl.parse("0\nREP\n920\n*\n'PVK'\n\n")
  203 |     r = gbl.apply(prog, {'920': [{'': 'PAZK'}]}, ctx)
  204 |     check('REP whole field', gbl.field_values(r, '920') == ['PVK'])
  205 | 
  206 |     # REP replaces a subfield (1st repetition).
  207 |     prog = gbl.parse("0\nREP\n200^a\n1\n'Новое'\n\n")
  208 |     r = gbl.apply(prog, {'200': [{'a': 'Старое', 'f': 'Автор'}]}, ctx)
  209 |     check('REP subfield', gbl.field_values(r, '200', 'a') == ['Новое'])
  210 |     check('REP subfield keeps sibling', gbl.field_values(r, '200', 'f') == ['Автор'])
  211 | 
  212 |     # REP with empty ФОРМАТ 1 deletes the field (GC §1.3).
  213 |     prog = gbl.parse("0\nREP\n910\n*\n\n\n")
  214 |     r = gbl.apply(prog, {'910': [{'': 'x'}]}, ctx)
  215 |     check('REP empty deletes field', gbl.field_values(r, '910') == [])
  216 | 
  217 |     # DEL removes a whole field repetition.
  218 |     prog = gbl.parse("0\nDEL\n910\n*\n\n\n")
  219 |     r = gbl.apply(prog, {'910': [{'': 'a'}, {'': 'b'}]}, ctx)
  220 |     check('DEL all repetitions', gbl.field_values(r, '910') == [])
  221 | 
  222 |     # DEL the N-th repetition only.
  223 |     prog = gbl.parse("0\nDEL\n910\n1\n\n\n")
  224 |     r = gbl.apply(prog, {'910': [{'': 'a'}, {'': 'b'}]}, ctx)
  225 |     check('DEL nth repetition', gbl.field_values(r, '910') == ['b'])
  226 | 
  227 |     # DEL a subfield (leaves the repetition).
  228 |     prog = gbl.parse("0\nDEL\n200^f\n*\n\n\n")
  229 |     r = gbl.apply(prog, {'200': [{'a': 'T', 'f': 'A'}]}, ctx)
  230 |     check('DEL subfield only', gbl.field_values(r, '200', 'a') == ['T']
  231 |           and gbl.field_values(r, '200', 'f') == [])
  232 | 
  233 |     # CHA replaces a substring, case-insensitively.
  234 |     prog = gbl.parse("0\nCHA\n926^b\n*\n'.'\n'. '\n")
  235 |     r = gbl.apply(prog, {'926': [{'b': 'И.И.'}]}, ctx)
  236 |     check('CHA substring (all)', gbl.field_values(r, '926', 'b') == ['И. И. '])
  237 | 
  238 |     # CHA is case-insensitive; CHAC is case-sensitive.
  239 |     prog_cha = gbl.parse("0\nCHA\n900\n*\n'abc'\n'X'\n")
  240 |     r1 = gbl.apply(prog_cha, {'900': [{'': 'ABCabc'}]}, ctx)
  241 |     check('CHA ignores case', gbl.field_values(r1, '900') == ['XX'])
  242 |     prog_chac = gbl.parse("0\nCHAC\n900\n*\n'abc'\n'X'\n")
  243 |     r2 = gbl.apply(prog_chac, {'900': [{'': 'ABCabc'}]}, ctx)
  244 |     check('CHAC respects case', gbl.field_values(r2, '900') == ['ABCX'])
  245 | 
  246 |     # F-mode DEL: '1' lines delete the matching repetition (GC del40.gbl pattern).
  247 |     ev = by_format_eval({'(flag/)': '0\n1\n0'})
  248 |     prog = gbl.parse("0\nDEL\n910\nF\n(flag/)\n\n")
  249 |     r = gbl.apply(prog, {'910': [{'': 'a'}, {'': 'b'}, {'': 'c'}]},
  250 |                   {'format_eval': ev})
  251 |     check('DEL F-mode deletes flagged', gbl.field_values(r, '910') == ['a', 'c'])
  252 | 
  253 |     # DELR sets the logical-delete flag (record marked, not dropped).
  254 |     prog = gbl.parse("0\nDELR\n")
  255 |     local = {'format_eval': lit_eval}
  256 |     r = gbl.apply(prog, {'200': [{'a': 'x'}]}, local)
```

```py
  266 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  267 | # 2b. IF gating + multi-operator job.
  268 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  269 | def control_flow_checks():
  270 |     print('-- control flow (IF / multi-op)')
  271 | 
  272 |     # IF true -> body runs.  cond evaluator returns '1' iff 200^a present.
  273 |     def cond_eval(fmt, record, ctx):
  274 |         if fmt.startswith('if p(v200)'):
  275 |             return '1' if gbl.field_values(record, '200', 'a') else '0'
  276 |         return gbl.stub_format_eval(fmt, record, ctx)
  277 | 
  278 |     job = "0\nIF\nif p(v200) then '1' fi\nADD\n910\n\n'YES'\n\nFI\n"
  279 |     prog = gbl.parse(job)
  280 |     r_on = gbl.apply(prog, {'200': [{'a': 'T'}]}, {'format_eval': cond_eval})
  281 |     check('IF true runs body', gbl.field_values(r_on, '910') == ['YES'])
  282 |     r_off = gbl.apply(prog, {'300': [{'a': 'x'}]}, {'format_eval': cond_eval})
  283 |     check('IF false skips body', gbl.field_values(r_off, '910') == [])
  284 | 
  285 |     # Multi-operator job: ADD a 910, REP the 920, DEL a 900 — sequential, each
  286 |     # sees the previous result.
  287 |     multi = ("0\n"
  288 |              "ADD\n910\n\n'INV-1'\n\n"
  289 |              "REP\n920\n*\n'PVK'\n\n"
  290 |              "DEL\n900\n*\n\n\n")
  291 |     prog = gbl.parse(multi)
  292 |     start = {'920': [{'': 'PAZK'}], '900': [{'b': 'a'}], '200': [{'a': 'Кн'}]}
  293 |     r = gbl.apply(prog, start, {'format_eval': lit_eval})
  294 |     ok = (gbl.field_values(r, '910') == ['INV-1']
  295 |           and gbl.field_values(r, '920') == ['PVK']
  296 |           and gbl.field_values(r, '900') == []
  297 |           and gbl.field_values(r, '200', 'a') == ['Кн'])
  298 |     check('multi-operator job applies in order', ok)
  299 |

  309 |     picked = gbl.resolve_format_eval({'format_eval': sentinel})
```

```py
  312 |     # With no hook and no access.pft, the built-in stub is selected.
  313 |     # (access.pft does not exist in this slice -> falls through to the stub.)
  314 |     picked2 = gbl.resolve_format_eval({})

  323 |     check('stub empty/#', gbl.stub_format_eval('#', rec({}), {}) == ''
  324 |           and gbl.stub_format_eval('', rec({}), {}) == '')

  328 |           gbl.stub_format_eval("'%1'", rec({}), {'params': {'1': '20240101'}}) == '20240101')

  331 | def _pft_importable():
  332 |     try:
  333 |         from access import pft            # noqa: F401
  334 |         return getattr(pft, 'eval', None) is not None
  335 |     except Exception:
  336 |         return False

  339 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  340 | # 4. Preview — dry-run diff with no mutation.
  341 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  342 | def preview_checks():
  343 |     print('-- preview (dry-run diff)')
  344 |     ctx = {'format_eval': lit_eval}
  345 | 
  346 |     # ADD + REP across two records; verify the diff and that inputs are intact.
  347 |     prog = gbl.parse("0\nADD\n910\n\n'NEW'\n\nREP\n920\n*\n'PVK'\n\n")
  348 |     records = [
  349 |         {'920': [{'': 'PAZK'}]},
  350 |         {'920': [{'': 'PVK'}], '910': [{'': 'NEW'}]},   # ADD still adds a 2nd 910
  351 |     ]
  352 |     snapshot = [dict(records[0]), dict(records[1])]
  353 |     report = gbl.preview(prog, records, ctx)
  354 | 
  355 |     check('preview returns one entry per record', len(report) == 2)
  356 |     r0 = report[0]
  357 |     check('preview rec0 changed', r0['status'] == 'changed')
  358 |     # rec0: 910 added (PAZK->PVK on 920, +910 NEW)
  359 |     add_910 = [c for c in r0['changes'] if c['tag'] == '910' and c['op'] == 'add']
  360 |     mod_920 = [c for c in r0['changes'] if c['tag'] == '920' and c['op'] == 'modify']
  361 |     check('preview diff: 910 added', len(add_910) == 1 and add_910[0]['after'] == 'NEW')
  362 |     check('preview diff: 920 modified PAZK->PVK',
  363 |           len(mod_920) == 1 and mod_920[0]['before'] == 'PAZK'
  364 |           and mod_920[0]['after'] == 'PVK')
  365 | 
  366 |     # No mutation of the inputs (SPEC AC5).
```

```py
  382 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  383 | # 5. Cross-DB execution — NEWMFN / NEWREC / CORREC / ALL / UNDOR (the P0
  384 | # integration unblocker, INTEGRATION_MAP #11.2). All run on in-memory stores; the
  385 | # nested ADD/REP формат is evaluated on the SOURCE record (txt:5988-5991).
  386 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  387 | def crossdb_checks():
  388 |     print('-- cross-db (NEWMFN/NEWREC/CORREC/ALL/UNDOR)')
  389 |     ctx0 = {'format_eval': lit_eval}
  390 | 
  391 |     # NEWMFN: create a record in ANOTHER db; nested ADD formats read the source.
  392 |     job = ("0\nNEWMFN\n'CMPL'\n"
  393 |            "ADD\n920\n\n'SZ'\n\n"
  394 |            "ADD\n200^a\n1\nv200^a\n\n"        # reformat: source 200^a -> new 200^a
  395 |            "END\n")
  396 |     prog = gbl.parse(job)
  397 |     src = {'200': [{'a': 'Источник'}], '920': [{'': 'PAZK'}]}
  398 |     ctx = dict(ctx0)
  399 |     out = gbl.apply(prog, src, ctx)
  400 |     st = ctx['_state']
  401 |     created = st['new_records'].get('CMPL', [])
  402 |     check('NEWMFN creates a record in target db', len(created) == 1)
  403 |     check('NEWMFN nested ADD literal', gbl.field_values(created[0], '920') == ['SZ'])
  404 |     check('NEWMFN format reads SOURCE record',
  405 |           gbl.field_values(created[0], '200', 'a') == ['Источник'])
  406 |     check('NEWMFN does not touch the source record',
  407 |           gbl.field_values(out, '920') == ['PAZK'] and '200' in out)
  408 |     # default in-memory emit records the created record's mfn in the run state.
  409 |     check('NEWMFN emit recorded (default in-memory store)',
  410 |           any(e['op'] == 'NEWMFN' and e['db'] == 'CMPL'
  411 |               for e in st.get('emitted', [])))
  412 | 
  413 |     # NEWMFN db '*' resolves to the source db (ctx['db']).
  414 |     prog_star = gbl.parse("0\nNEWMFN\n'*'\nADD\n920\n\n'SZ'\n\nEND\n")
  415 |     ctx_star = {'format_eval': lit_eval, 'db': 'IBIS'}
  416 |     gbl.apply(prog_star, {'200': [{'a': 'x'}]}, ctx_star)
  417 |     check("NEWMFN '*' resolves to source db",
  418 |           'IBIS' in ctx_star['_state']['new_records'])
  419 | 
  420 |     # ALL: copy every source field into the record under construction.
  421 |     prog_all = gbl.parse("0\nNEWMFN\n'CMPL'\nALL\nADD\n920\n\n'SZ'\n\nEND\n")
```

```py
  431 |     def cond_eval(fmt, record, ctx):

  434 |         return gbl.stub_format_eval(fmt, record, ctx)
  435 |     prog_nr = gbl.parse("0\nNEWREC\nif v920 then '1' fi\nADD\n900\n\n'made'\n\nEND\n")
  436 |     ctx_on = {'format_eval': cond_eval, 'db': 'IBIS'}

  438 |     check('NEWREC condition true creates',

  445 |     # CORREC: edit a FOREIGN record resolved by term; 1001 key planted then stripped.
  446 |     # The foreign CMPL record gains a 910 link copied from the source IBIS draft.
  447 |     foreign = {'CMPL': {'borz42': {'200': [{'a': 'Заказ'}]}}}
  448 |     def resolver(db, term):
  449 |         return foreign.get(db, {}).get(term)
  450 |     job_cr = ("0\nCORREC\n'CMPL'\n'srckey'\n'borz42'\n"
  451 |               "ADD\n910\n\nv910\n\n"          # copy source 910 into the foreign rec
  452 |               "END\n")
  453 |     prog_cr = gbl.parse(job_cr)
  454 |     ctx_cr = {'format_eval': lit_eval, 'resolver': resolver}
  455 |     src_cr = {'910': [{'': 'INV-7'}], '200': [{'a': 'Draft'}]}
  456 |     gbl.apply(prog_cr, src_cr, ctx_cr)
  457 |     correc = ctx_cr['_state']['correc'].get('CMPL', [])
  458 |     check('CORREC edits one foreign record', len(correc) == 1)
  459 |     edited = correc[0]['record']
  460 |     check('CORREC writes source data into foreign record',
  461 |           gbl.field_values(edited, '910') == ['INV-7'])
  462 |     check('CORREC strips the 1001 model field after the body',
  463 |           '1001' not in edited)
  464 |     check('CORREC resolves by term', correc[0]['term'] == 'borz42')
  465 | 
  466 |     # CORREC miss: an unresolved term is recorded, not crashed.
  467 |     ctx_miss = {'format_eval': lit_eval, 'resolver': lambda d, t: None}
  468 |     gbl.apply(prog_cr, src_cr, ctx_miss)
  469 |     check('CORREC unresolved term recorded as miss',
  470 |           len(ctx_miss['_state'].get('correc_misses', [])) == 1
  471 |           and ctx_miss['_state']['correc'].get('CMPL', []) == [])
  472 | 
  473 |     # UNDOR: roll the current record back to a previous copy from the journal.
  474 |     history = [
  475 |         {'907': [{'': 'v0'}]},               # original copy
  476 |         {'907': [{'': 'v1'}]},               # previous copy (1 back)
```

```py
  479 |     ctx_ud = {'format_eval': lit_eval, 'history': history}
  480 |     r_ud = gbl.apply(prog_ud, {'907': [{'': 'cur'}]}, ctx_ud)
  481 |     check('UNDOR N=1 restores previous copy', gbl.field_values(r_ud, '907') == ['v1'])
  482 |     prog_ud0 = gbl.parse("0\nUNDOR\n'*'\n")
  483 |     r_ud0 = gbl.apply(prog_ud0, {'907': [{'': 'cur'}]},
  484 |                       {'format_eval': lit_eval, 'history': history})
  485 |     check('UNDOR * restores original copy', gbl.field_values(r_ud0, '907') == ['v0'])
  486 |     prog_udn = gbl.parse("0\nUNDOR\n#\n")     # empty steps -> no-op
  487 |     r_udn = gbl.apply(prog_udn, {'907': [{'': 'cur'}]},
  488 |                       {'format_eval': lit_eval, 'history': history})
  489 |     check('UNDOR empty steps is a no-op', gbl.field_values(r_udn, '907') == ['cur'])
  490 | 
  491 |     # explicit resolver/emit args (apply signature) wire the cross-DB contract.
  492 |     emitted = []
  493 |     def emit(db, record):
  494 |         emitted.append((db, record)); return 99
  495 |     gbl.apply(prog, src, {'format_eval': lit_eval}, emit=emit)
  496 |     check('emit hook receives created record', len(emitted) == 1
  497 |           and emitted[0][0] == 'CMPL'
  498 |           and gbl.field_values(emitted[0][1], '920') == ['SZ'])
  499 | 
  500 | 
  501 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  502 | # 6. ToCat-shaped end-to-end job: read an IBIS draft БО → NEWMFN into the IBIS
  503 | # catalog with reformatted fields + a 938 subscription link → in-memory stores.
  504 | # Mirrors INTEGRATION_MAP cluster 1 (ToCat 938/910/VD=DEL) without an acquisition
  505 | # host: the executor stays pure, the store is wired via emit/_stores.
  506 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  507 | def tocat_shaped_checks():
  508 |     print('-- ToCat-shaped job (CMPL draft -> IBIS catalog)')
  509 | 
  510 |     # ToCat: a CMPL acquisition draft is reformatted into an IBIS catalog record.
  511 |     #  * 920 reset to the catalog kind 'PAZK'
  512 |     #  * title carried over (200^a -> 200^a) — reformat-on-source
  513 |     #  * the holdings copy carried over (910 -> 910)
  514 |     #  * a 938 link back to the subscription period is planted
  515 |     #  * VD=DEL marks the source draft for deletion (no-dup guarantee, AC3)
  516 |     job = (
  517 |         "0\n"
  518 |         "NEWMFN\n'IBIS'\n"
  519 |         "ADD\n920\n\n'PAZK'\n\n"             # catalog record kind
  520 |         "ADD\n200^a\n1\nv200^a\n\n"          # title reformatted from source
  521 |         "ADD\n910\n\nv910\n\n"               # holdings carried over
  522 |         "ADD\n938\n\nv938\n\n"               # subscription-period link (cluster 1.3)
  523 |         "END\n"
  524 |         "ADD\n2102\n\n'VD=DEL'\n\n"          # mark source draft for deletion (AC3)
  525 |     )
  526 |     prog = gbl.parse(job)
  527 | 
  528 |     # in-memory IBIS catalog store; emit returns the new MFN (store index).
  529 |     stores = {'IBIS': []}
  530 |     def emit(db, record):
  531 |         stores.setdefault(db, []).append(record)
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_identity.py`  · строк: 324

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Identity / Tenancy / Licensing tests (issue #101, I1).
    3 | 
    4 | Covers the three new mechanisms layered on the merged Access store:
    5 | 
    6 |   1. JWT sessions — dependency-free HS256 issue->verify round-trip, and that a
    7 |      TAMPERED or EXPIRED token is rejected (-> no session -> 401 at the edge).
    8 |   2. Tenant context — the JWT carries a `tenant` claim; the session is scoped to
    9 |      it; on PostgreSQL the per-tenant store sees only that tenant's data.
   10 |   3. Entitlements (licensing) — a request to a DISABLED module is refused even
   11 |      with a valid grant (grants = can-do, entitlements = is-licensed). On the
   12 |      dev/`public` path entitlements fail open so the guest flow keeps working.
   13 | 
   14 | Backends:
   15 |   * sqlite / pure  — JWT round-trip, tamper/expiry rejection, public fail-open
   16 |     entitlement, and the _guard entitlement-gating path with a monkeypatched
   17 |     entitlement check (no DB needed). Always run.
   18 |   * postgres only  — tenant-claim data scoping and real `control.tenant_module`
   19 |     gating. Runs when ACCESS_BACKEND=postgres (or ACCESS_TEST_PG=1) AND a PG is
   20 |     reachable; otherwise SKIPPED cleanly (never red-flags the sqlite dev box).
   21 | 
   22 | Invoked by the test_access.py runner (identity_checks) so the CI postgres step
   23 | exercises it with NO .github/ change.
   24 | """
   25 | import os
   26 | import sys
   27 | import time
   28 | 
   29 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   30 | from access import jwt as _jwt
   31 | from access import entitlements
   32 | from access.authz import authorize, GUEST_GRANTS
   33 | 
   34 | PASS = [0]
   35 | FAIL = [0]
   36 | 
   37 | SECRET = 'unit-test-secret'
   38 | 
   39 | 
   40 | def check(name, cond):
   41 |     if cond:
   42 |         PASS[0] += 1
   43 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   44 |     else:
   45 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   46 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   47 | 
   48 | 
   49 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   50 | # 1. JWT — pure, backend-independent.
   51 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
```

```py
  217 | 
  218 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  219 | # 4. PostgreSQL: tenant-claim data scoping + real tenant_module gating.
  220 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  221 | A_SLUG, B_SLUG = 'idn_a', 'idn_b'
  222 | 
  223 | 
  224 | def pg_reachable(dsn):
  225 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
  226 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1'
  227 |     if not want:
  228 |         return False
  229 |     try:
  230 |         from access import pgstore
  231 |         conn = pgstore._admin_conn(dsn)
  232 |         conn.close()
  233 |         return True
  234 |     except Exception as e:
  235 |         print('-- identity PG suite SKIPPED (%s: %s)'
  236 |               % (type(e).__name__, str(e).splitlines()[0]))
  237 |         return False
  238 | 
  239 | 
  240 | def pg_checks(dsn):
  241 |     print('-- identity: postgres', dsn.rsplit('@', 1)[-1])
  242 |     from access import pgstore
  243 | 
  244 |     # clean slate
  245 |     pgstore.deprovision_tenant(A_SLUG, dsn)
  246 |     pgstore.deprovision_tenant(B_SLUG, dsn)
  247 | 
  248 |     sa = pgstore.provision_tenant(A_SLUG, 'Identity A', 'публичная', dsn)
  249 |     sb = pgstore.provision_tenant(B_SLUG, 'Identity B', 'школьная', dsn)
  250 | 
  251 |     # --- tenant-claim data scoping: a distinct account per tenant, invisible across ---
  252 |     sa.create_account('alice', 'pw_a', 'Alice')
  253 |     sb.create_account('bob', 'pw_b', 'Bob')
  254 |     check('tenant A sees its own account', sa.authenticate('alice', 'pw_a') is not None)
  255 |     check('tenant A cannot see B account', sa.get_account('bob') is None)
  256 |     check('tenant B cannot see A account', sb.get_account('alice') is None)
  257 | 
  258 |     # --- entitlements seeded enabled by provisioning ---
  259 |     mods_a = entitlements.enabled_modules(A_SLUG, dsn)
  260 |     check('provisioning seeds default modules enabled',
  261 |           set(entitlements.DEFAULT_MODULES).issubset(set(mods_a)))
  262 |     check('is_module_enabled True for seeded module',
  263 |           entitlements.is_module_enabled(A_SLUG, 'cataloging', dsn))
  264 | 
  265 |     # --- disable one module -> is_module_enabled False, dropped from the list ---
  266 |     entitlements.set_module(A_SLUG, 'cataloging', False, dsn)
  267 |     check('disabled module -> is_module_enabled False',
  268 |           entitlements.is_module_enabled(A_SLUG, 'cataloging', dsn) is False)
  269 |     check('disabled module dropped from enabled list',
  270 |           'cataloging' not in entitlements.enabled_modules(A_SLUG, dsn))
  271 |     # other tenant unaffected (isolation of the licensing axis too)
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_integration.py`  · строк: 625

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Cross-module integration tests — the «связующая ткань» seams (INTEGRATION_MAP).
```

<!-- ─── страница 500 ─── -->

```py
    3 | 
    4 | Wires the two highest-value межмодульные рёбра that the integration map flagged
    5 | as designed-but-not-wired, and proves them end-to-end over the existing in-memory
    6 | stores (no core.py, no network):
    7 | 
    8 |   * Edges 2.1/2.2 — Catalog↔Circulation 910^A flip. ``CirculationEngine`` is given
    9 |     a ``catalog`` handle; ``checkout`` flips the linked catalog exemplar's
   10 |     ``910^A`` 0→1 (issued), ``return_item`` flips it 1→0 (free), and ``place_hold``
   11 |     /availability reads honour the catalog ``910^A``. The item↔record link is
   12 |     resolved by inventory number (``910^b``), as the spec describes.
   13 | 
   14 |   * Edge 6.1 — Authority↔Catalog ^3 on save. ``CatalogStore`` is given an
   15 |     ``authority`` handle; a field instance carrying ``_authority_ref`` (the
   16 |     authority record id) is resolved through ``authority.substitute`` on ``save``,
   17 |     filling ``^a/^b/^g`` + the ``^3`` link before ФЛК/index.
   18 | 
   19 | Both seams are exercised WITH the handle (wired) and the back-compat paths
   20 | (no handle) are re-asserted so nothing regresses.
   21 | 
   22 | Standalone-runnable in the house style of tests/test_catalog.py /
   23 | tests/test_circulation.py::
   24 | 
   25 |     py -3.12 tests/test_integration.py  ->  'ok …' lines + 'N passed, M failed'
   26 | 
   27 | Also wired into tests/test_access.py (the CI runner) via module_checks so the
   28 | existing CI step exercises these seams with no workflow change.
   29 | """
   30 | import os
   31 | import sys
   32 | 
   33 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   34 | 
   35 | from access import flk
   36 | from access import seed_vocab
   37 | from access import authority as A
   38 | from access.authority import AuthorityStore
   39 | from access.catalog import (CatalogStore, EXEMPLAR_FREE, EXEMPLAR_ISSUED)
   40 | from access.circulation import (
   41 |     CirculationStore, CirculationEngine, default_policy,
   42 |     ALLOW, SECONDS_PER_DAY,
   43 | )
   44 | from access.acquisition import AcquisitionEngine, AcquisitionStore
   45 | from access.bookprovision import BookProvisionEngine, KIND_MAIN
   46 | from access.store import AccessStore
   47 | 
   48 | PASS = [0]
   49 | FAIL = [0]
   50 | 
   51 | DAY = SECONDS_PER_DAY
   52 | T0 = 1_700_000_000
   53 | 
   54 | 
   55 | def check(name, cond):
   56 |     if cond:
   57 |         PASS[0] += 1
```

```py
   64 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   65 | # Fixtures.
   66 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   67 | def _seeded_access_store():
   68 |     st = AccessStore(':memory:')
   69 |     seed_vocab.seed_vocabularies(st, from_catalog=False)
   70 |     return st
   71 | 
   72 | 
   73 | def _book_with_copy(inv='1024365', status=EXEMPLAR_FREE):
   74 |     """A valid book carrying one 910 exemplar keyed by inventory ``inv`` whose
   75 |     910^A starts at ``status`` (default free)."""
   76 |     return {
   77 |         '920': 'PAZK',
   78 |         '200': [{'a': 'Основы каталогизации', 'f': 'Иванова И.И.'}],
   79 |         '700': [{'a': 'Петров П.П.'}],
   80 |         '101': 'rus',
   81 |         '910': [{'a': status, 'b': inv}],
   82 |         '907': [{'a': 'Сидорова С.С.'}],
   83 |     }
   84 | 
   85 | 
   86 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   87 | # Edge 2.1/2.2 — Catalog↔Circulation 910^A flip (the original break).
   88 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   89 | def checkout_return_flip_checks():
   90 |     print('-- edge 2.1/2.2: checkout/return flip catalog 910^A')
   91 |     cat = CatalogStore(':memory:', access_store=_seeded_access_store())
   92 |     inv = '1024365'
   93 |     cat.save('IBIS', _book_with_copy(inv, EXEMPLAR_FREE))
   94 | 
   95 |     # the catalog accessors locate + read the exemplar by inventory number (910^b)
   96 |     check('exemplar located by 910^b', cat.find_exemplar('IBIS', inv) is not None)
   97 |     check('exemplar starts FREE (910^A=0)',
   98 |           cat.exemplar_status('IBIS', inv) == EXEMPLAR_FREE)
   99 |     check('is_available True before loan', cat.is_available('IBIS', inv) is True)
  100 | 
  101 |     store = CirculationStore(':memory:')
  102 |     eng = CirculationEngine(store=store, policy=default_policy(),
  103 |                             catalog=cat, catalog_db='IBIS')
  104 |     store.add_reader('R1', category='В01')
  105 | 
  106 |     # CHECKOUT: 910^A flips 0 -> 1 (issued) on the linked catalog record.
  107 |     d = eng.checkout('R1', inv, T0)
  108 |     check('checkout allowed', d.decision == ALLOW)
  109 |     check('checkout flips catalog 910^A 0->1',
  110 |           cat.exemplar_status('IBIS', inv) == EXEMPLAR_ISSUED)
  111 |     check('catalog item not available after checkout',
  112 |           cat.is_available('IBIS', inv) is False)
```

```py
  194 | def _authority_store_with_tolstoy():

  196 |     pid = auth.add_record(
  197 |         'athra',

  199 |          'f': '1828-1910', '9': '1'},

  201 |     sid = auth.add_record(
  202 |         'athrs',
```
