```py
   62 | 
   63 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   64 | # 1. Field substitution.
   65 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   66 | def field_checks():
   67 |     print('-- field substitution')
   68 |     rec = _book()
   69 |     check('v200^a -> title', pft_eval('v200^a', rec) == 'Основы каталогизации')
   70 |     check('v101 whole-field -> rus', pft_eval('v101', rec) == 'rus')
   71 |     check('v200^f -> author', pft_eval('v200^f', rec) == 'Иванова И.И.')
   72 |     check('v200^* -> first subfield', pft_eval('v200^*', rec) == 'Основы каталогизации')
   73 |     check('absent subfield -> empty', pft_eval('v200^z', rec) == '')
   74 |     check('absent field -> empty', pft_eval('v700^a', rec) == '')
   75 |     # fragment v…*off.len (0-based)
   76 |     check('fragment v101*0.2 -> "ru"', pft_eval('v101*0.2', rec) == 'ru')
   77 |     # mfn from ctx
   78 |     check('mfn(6) zero-padded', pft_eval('mfn(6)', rec, {'mfn': 42}) == '000042')
   79 |     check('bare mfn', pft_eval('mfn', rec, {'mfn': 7}) == '7')
   80 | 
   81 | 
   82 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   83 | # 2. Repeating fields.
   84 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   85 | def repeat_checks():
   86 |     print('-- repeating fields')
   87 |     rec = _book()
   88 |     # A bare field command outputs ALL occurrences concatenated (PFT semantics).
   89 |     check('v910^b emits all occurrences', pft_eval('v910^b', rec) == 'INV1INV2INV3')
   90 |     # |x|+ repeatable prefix = separator before each instance except the first.
   91 |     check('|sep|+ separator between repeats',
   92 |           pft_eval('| ; |+v910^b', rec) == 'INV1 ; INV2 ; INV3')
   93 |     # +|x| repeatable suffix = after each instance except the last.
   94 |     check('+|sep| separator after repeats',
   95 |           pft_eval('v910^b+| ; |', rec) == 'INV1 ; INV2 ; INV3')
   96 |     # repeat group emits each instance
   97 |     grp = pft_eval('(v910^b)', rec)
   98 |     check('repeat group over v910 -> all 3', grp == 'INV1INV2INV3')
   99 |     # eval_lines splits the group into one line per instance
  100 |     lines = pft.eval_lines('(v910^b)', rec)
  101 |     check('eval_lines yields 3 lines', lines == ['INV1', 'INV2', 'INV3'])
  102 | 
  103 | 
  104 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  105 | # 3. IF / THEN / ELSE / FI + 920 branching.
  106 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  107 | def if_checks():
  108 |     print('-- if/then/else/fi')
  109 |     rec = _book()                              # 920 = PAZK
  110 |     check("if v920='J' else branch",
  111 |           pft_eval("if v920='J' then 'журнал' else 'книга' fi", rec) == 'книга')
  112 |     jrec = dict(rec, **{'920': 'J'})
  113 |     check("if v920='J' then branch",
  114 |           pft_eval("if v920='J' then 'журнал' else 'книга' fi", jrec) == 'журнал')
  115 |     # ':' contains operator (case-insensitive)
  116 |     njrec = dict(rec, **{'920': 'NJ31'})
```

```py
  243 | def unifor_string_checks():
  244 |     print('-- unifor string ops (E/F/G/T/L/9/X)')
  245 |     rec = {'200': [{'a': 'Hello World Foo Bar'}],
  246 |            '300': 'Москва', '301': 'a  b   c d'}
  247 |     # E — first N words (whitespace-collapsed).
  248 |     check("&unifor('E2'v200^a) -> 'Hello World'",
  249 |           pft_eval("&unifor('E2'v200^a)", rec) == 'Hello World')
  250 |     # F — keep up to Nth word, preserving original spacing (distinct from E).

  257 |     check("&unifor('F0') -> empty",
```

```py
  286 | def unifor_misc_checks():
  287 |     print('-- unifor misc (0 / +6 / +3E / +3D)')
  288 |     rec = {'200': [{'a': 'T'}], '101': 'rus', '300': 'Москва'}
  289 |     # 0 — full-record dump: every field as 'tag#value' lines.
  290 |     dump = pft_eval("&unifor('0')", rec)
  291 |     check("&unifor('0') dumps all tags",
  292 |           '200#T' in dump and '101#rus' in dump and '300#Москва' in dump)
  293 |     check("&unifor('0') on empty record -> ''",
  294 |           pft_eval("&unifor('0')", {}) == '')
  295 |     # +6 — record status: 1 live, 0 logically deleted (from ctx['deleted']).
  296 |     check("&unifor('+6') live record -> 1",
  297 |           pft_eval("&unifor('+6')", rec, {}) == '1')
  298 |     check("&unifor('+6') deleted record -> 0",
  299 |           pft_eval("&unifor('+6')", rec, {'deleted': True}) == '0')
  300 |     # +3E / +3D — URL encode / decode (round-trip).
  301 |     enc = pft_eval("&unifor('+3E'v300)", rec)
  302 |     check("&unifor('+3E') URL-encodes 'Москва'",
  303 |           enc == '%D0%9C%D0%BE%D1%81%D0%BA%D0%B2%D0%B0')
  304 |     check("&unifor('+3D') decodes a literal back to 'Москва'",
  305 |           pft_eval("&unifor('+3D%D0%9C%D0%BE%D1%81%D0%BA%D0%B2%D0%B0')", rec)
  306 |           == 'Москва')
  307 |     check("+3E then +3D round-trips a cyrillic string",
  308 |           pft_eval("&unifor('+3D'&unifor('+3E'v300))", rec) == 'Москва')
  309 | 
  310 | 
  311 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  312 | # 6. Graceful degradation of unsupported constructs.
  313 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  314 | def degrade_checks():
  315 |     print('-- graceful degradation')
  316 |     rec = _book()
  317 |     # ref(l(...)) needs the inverted index — not in this slice; must NOT crash,
  318 |     # and the surrounding text must still render.
  319 |     out = pft_eval("v200^a, ref(l('I='v933), v200^a), ' КОНЕЦ'", rec)
  320 |     check('ref()/l() degrade to empty, rest renders',
  321 |           out == 'Основы каталогизации КОНЕЦ')
  322 |     # unknown UNIFOR code -> '' (Format error 99 parity), no crash.
  323 |     check("unknown unifor code -> empty",
  324 |           pft_eval("'A'&unifor('ZZZ')'B'", rec) == 'AB')
  325 |     # malformed IF (no FI) degrades rather than raising, in non-strict mode.
  326 |     safe = pft_eval("if v920='J' then 'x'", rec)
  327 |     check('malformed IF (no FI) degrades, no crash', isinstance(safe, str))
  328 |     # mode commands mhl/mpl and placement / # are skipped (no-op) not echoed.
  329 |     check('mode command mhl is a no-op',
  330 |           pft_eval("mhl, v101", rec) == 'rus')
  331 |     check('placement / is a no-op text-wise',
  332 |           pft_eval("v101 / v920", rec) == 'rusPAZK')
  333 |     # strict mode DOES raise on a real error (editor/preview path).
  334 |     raised = False
  335 |     try:
  336 |         pft_eval("if v920='J' then 'x'", rec, strict=True)
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_platform.py`  · строк: 528

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Platform suite: tenant PROVISIONING + BILLING skeleton (issue #207/#209, epic
    3 | #223 — MVP Phase 2).
    4 | 
    5 | Three layers, mirroring the rest of the Access suite's structure:
    6 | 
    7 |   * PURE (always run, no DB): the billing plan catalogue — plan_modules / plan_limits,
    8 |     and check_limit allowing under / blocking over a ceiling. Plus the provisioning
    9 |     flow on the sqlite dev path (single 'public' store): provision a tenant → its
   10 |     roles/vocabs/admin account exist and the admin AUTHENTICATES; re-provision is
   11 |     idempotent.
   12 |   * ROUTES (always run, over the real ``core.Api.route()`` dispatcher, sqlite, no
   13 |     live ИРБИС): GET /api/admin/tenants, POST /api/admin/tenant, GET /api/admin/billing,
   14 |     POST /api/admin/billing/plan, POST /api/admin/billing/module — admitted for a
   15 |     super-admin (admin.db@admin) and 403 for non-admin staff / reader / guest; a
   16 |     module toggle reflects in the enabled-module list.
   17 |   * PG (run when postgres is reachable; skipped cleanly otherwise): provision a real
   18 |     isolated ``t_<slug>`` schema → its vocabularies + named admin exist in THAT schema,
   19 |     the admin authenticates against it, and the plan drives ``control.tenant_module``
   20 |     so exactly the plan's modules are enabled (and a non-plan module is disabled).
   21 |     Idempotent re-provision. Deprovision drops the schema.
   22 | 
   23 | Wired into the test_access.py runner (its module list) via ``module_checks`` — the
   24 | runner calls every ``*_checks()`` defined here and folds the PASS/FAIL tally in, so
   25 | the CI sqlite + postgres legs both exercise it.
   26 | 
   27 | Standalone:  py -3.12 tests/test_platform.py
   28 | """
   29 | import os
   30 | import sys
   31 | 
   32 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   33 | 
   34 | from access import billing
```

```py
   48 |     else:

   53 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   54 | # 1. Billing plan catalogue + limit enforcement — pure, no DB.
   55 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   56 | def billing_plan_checks():
   57 |     print('-- billing: plan catalogue (modules + limits)')
   58 |     # Every advertised plan is a known plan with sorted modules + full limit set.
   59 |     for name in ('free', 'standard', 'pro'):
   60 |         check('plan %r is known' % name, billing.is_plan(name))
   61 |         mods = billing.plan_modules(name)
   62 |         check('plan %r modules sorted+nonempty' % name,
   63 |               mods == sorted(mods) and len(mods) >= 1)
   64 |         lims = billing.plan_limits(name)
   65 |         check('plan %r limits cover every resource' % name,
   66 |               set(lims) == set(billing.LIMIT_RESOURCES))
   67 |         # Each module a plan licenses is a real product module.
   68 |         check('plan %r modules subset of ALL_MODULES' % name,
   69 |               all(m in billing.ALL_MODULES for m in mods))
   70 | 
   71 |     # free is a strict subset of standard is a strict subset of pro (monotone tiers).
   72 |     free, std, pro = (set(billing.plan_modules(p)) for p in ('free', 'standard', 'pro'))
   73 |     check('free strict-subset standard', free < std)
   74 |     check('standard strict-subset pro', std < pro)
   75 |     check('pro = ALL_MODULES', pro == set(billing.ALL_MODULES))
   76 | 
   77 |     # cataloging is paid (not in free), acquisition is pro-only.
   78 |     check('free has no cataloging', 'cataloging' not in free)
   79 |     check('acquisition is pro-only',
   80 |           'acquisition' in pro and 'acquisition' not in std)
   81 | 
   82 |     # pro is unlimited; free/standard have integer ceilings.
   83 |     check('pro max_records unlimited',
   84 |           billing.plan_limit('pro', 'max_records') is billing.UNLIMITED)
   85 |     check('free max_records is a finite int',
   86 |           isinstance(billing.plan_limit('free', 'max_records'), int))
   87 |     check('standard max_records > free max_records',
   88 |           billing.plan_limit('standard', 'max_records')
   89 |           > billing.plan_limit('free', 'max_records'))
```

```py
  105 | def check_limit_checks():
  106 |     print('-- billing: check_limit (under allows / over blocks)')
  107 |     st = AccessStore(':memory:')
  108 |     # free.max_records = 1000. 'public' resolves to DEFAULT_PLAN, so pin the plan

  112 |     check('exactly at limit allowed', billing.check_limit(st, 'acme', 'max_records', cap, plan='free'))
  113 |     check('over limit blocked', not billing.check_limit(st, 'acme', 'max_records', cap + 1, plan='free'))
  114 | 
  115 |     # pro is unlimited → any count passes.

  117 |           billing.check_limit(st, 'acme', 'max_records', 10_000_000, plan='pro'))
  118 | 
  119 |     # An unmodelled resource is not enforced (returns True).

  122 | 
  123 |     # hard=True raises instead of returning False when over.

  127 |     except billing.BillingError:
  128 |         check('hard over-limit raises', True)
  129 |     # hard=True under limit does NOT raise.
  130 |     try:
  131 |         ok = billing.check_limit(st, 'acme', 'max_records', cap, plan='free', hard=True)
  132 |         check('hard under-limit returns True', ok is True)
  133 |     except billing.BillingError:
  134 |         check('hard under-limit returns True', False)
```

```py
  216 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  217 | # 3. Routes over the real dispatcher (sqlite, no live ИРБИС).

  219 | _DB_MENU = 'IBIS\nЭлектронный каталог\nRDR\nЧитатели\n*****\n'
  220 | 
  221 | # admin.db@admin is the super-admin grant the platform routes require.
  222 | SUPER_ADMIN_GRANTS = [
  223 |     {'function': 'admin.db', 'db': '*', 'level': 'admin'},
  224 |     {'function': 'admin.users', 'db': '*', 'level': 'admin'},
  225 |     {'function': 'search', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'},
  226 | ]
  227 | # Real staff, but NO admin.* grant.
  228 | NONADMIN_STAFF_GRANTS = [
  229 |     {'function': 'record.write', 'db': '*', 'level': 'write'},
  230 |     {'function': 'search', 'db': '*', 'level': 'read'},
  231 | ]
  232 | 
  233 | 
  234 | def _api():
  235 |     os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] = 'platform-test-secret'
  236 |     os.environ['ACCESS_BACKEND'] = 'sqlite'
  237 |     os.environ['ACCESS_DB'] = ':memory:'
  238 |     import importlib
  239 |     import core as _core
  240 |     importlib.reload(_core)
  241 |     api = _core.Api()
  242 |     api.irbis.read_file = lambda spec: _DB_MENU
  243 |     api.irbis.max_mfn = lambda db: 0
  244 |     return api, _core
  245 | 
  246 | 
  247 | def _super(api, login='super'):
  248 |     tok, _ = api._new_session('staff', login, SUPER_ADMIN_GRANTS, tenant='public')
  249 |     return {'authorization': 'Bearer ' + tok}
  250 | 
  251 | 
  252 | def _nonadmin(api, login='cat'):
  253 |     tok, _ = api._new_session('staff', login, NONADMIN_STAFF_GRANTS, tenant='public')
  254 |     return {'authorization': 'Bearer ' + tok}
```

```py
  355 | def enforcement_route_checks():
  356 |     print('-- enforcement: save_record max_records soft check (fail-open in dev)')
  357 |     api, _core = _api()
```

```py
  365 |     # count path. On the dev 'public' tenant the plan is DEFAULT (standard, 100k cap)
  366 |     # so a create well under it is ALLOWED — proving the wiring fails open / doesn't

  374 |     check('create flagged created', st == 200 and p['data']['created'] is True)
  375 | 
  376 |     # If the count blows past a (hypothetical) tiny cap, check_limit blocks → 402.
  377 |     # We force it by pinning the plan via a direct check: the route uses the tenant's
  378 |     # plan, which on dev is standard; so we assert the helper itself blocks the same
  379 |     # input under 'free' (the route would 402 identically once a free plan is set).
  380 |     over = billing.plan_limit('free', 'max_records') + 1
  381 |     check('over free cap would block (helper)',

  384 | 
  385 | PLATFORM_ROUTES = [
  386 |     ('GET', '/api/admin/tenants', None),
  387 |     ('POST', '/api/admin/tenant', {'slug': 'z', 'name': 'Z'}),
  388 |     ('GET', '/api/admin/billing', None),
  389 |     ('POST', '/api/admin/billing/plan', {'tenant': 'public', 'plan': 'pro'}),
  390 |     ('POST', '/api/admin/billing/module', {'tenant': 'public', 'module': 'opac', 'enabled': True}),
  391 | ]
  392 | 
  393 | 
  394 | def platform_auth_checks():
  395 |     print('-- routes auth: super-admin only; 403 for non-admin/reader/guest/none')
  396 |     api, _core = _api()
  397 | 
  398 |     # Non-admin staff -> 403 on every platform route.
  399 |     HS = _nonadmin(api)
  400 |     for m, path, b in PLATFORM_ROUTES:
  401 |         st, _ = api.route(m, path, {}, b, HS)
  402 |         check('non-admin staff denied %s %s (403)' % (m, path), st == 403)
  403 | 
  404 |     # Guest + reader -> 403 (staff session required) everywhere.
  405 |     for label, headers in (('guest', _guest(api)), ('reader', _reader(api))):
  406 |         for m, path, b in PLATFORM_ROUTES:
  407 |             st, _ = api.route(m, path, {}, b, headers)
  408 |             check('%s denied %s %s (403)' % (label, m, path), st == 403)
  409 | 
  410 |     # No session -> 401/403, never 200.
  411 |     for m, path, b in PLATFORM_ROUTES:
  412 |         st, _ = api.route(m, path, {}, b, {})
  413 |         check('no session %s %s -> 401/403' % (m, path), st in (401, 403))
  414 | 
  415 |     # Sanity: a super-admin IS admitted.
  416 |     H = _super(api)
  417 |     st, _ = api.route('GET', '/api/admin/tenants', {}, None, H)
  418 |     check('super-admin admitted to /api/admin/tenants (200)', st == 200)
  419 |
```

```py
  420 | 
  421 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  422 | # 4. PG leg — real schema-per-tenant provisioning + entitlement-driven billing.
  423 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  424 | def _pg_reachable(dsn):
  425 |     try:
  426 |         import psycopg
  427 |     except Exception:
  428 |         return False
  429 |     try:
  430 |         conn = psycopg.connect(dsn, connect_timeout=2)
  431 |         conn.close()
  432 |         return True
  433 |     except Exception:
  434 |         return False
  435 | 
  436 | 
  437 | def run_pg():
  438 |     """Provision a real tenant on PG: isolated schema + vocabs + admin + plan-driven
  439 |     entitlements. Skips cleanly when postgres/psycopg unavailable."""
  440 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
  441 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1'
  442 |     if not want:
  443 |         return
  444 |     from access import pgstore
  445 |     dsn = pgstore.default_pg_dsn()
  446 |     if not _pg_reachable(dsn):
  447 |         print('-- platform PG SKIPPED (postgres unreachable at %s)' % dsn.rsplit('@', 1)[-1])
  448 |         return
  449 |     print('-- platform: postgres', dsn.rsplit('@', 1)[-1])
  450 |     os.environ['ACCESS_BACKEND'] = 'postgres'
  451 |     slug = 'plat_test'
  452 |     # Clean slate.
  453 |     provision.deprovision_tenant(slug, dsn=dsn)
  454 |     try:
  455 |         public = pgstore.PgAccessStore(dsn)
  456 |         report = provision.provision_tenant(
  457 |             public, slug, 'Platform Test Lib', 'padmin',
  458 |             admin_password='pw-padmin', plan='standard', dsn=dsn)
  459 |         check('[pg] provision postgres flag True', report['postgres'] is True)
  460 | 
  461 |         # Isolated tenant schema: vocabs + admin live in t_<slug>.
  462 |         tstore = pgstore.make_tenant_store(slug, dsn)
  463 |         vocabs = {v['name'] for v in tstore.list_vocabularies()}
  464 |         check('[pg] system vocab seeded in tenant schema', 'jz.mnu' in vocabs)
  465 |         check('[pg] institution vocab created empty', 'kv.mnu' in vocabs
  466 |               and len(tstore.vocabulary_values('kv.mnu')) == 0)
  467 |         acc = tstore.get_account('padmin')
  468 |         check('[pg] named admin in tenant schema', acc is not None)
  469 |         check('[pg] admin has administrator role',
  470 |               'administrator' in tstore.account_roles(acc['id']))
  471 |         check('[pg] admin authenticates', tstore.authenticate('padmin', 'pw-padmin') is not None)
  472 | 
  473 |         # Plan drove control.tenant_module: standard's modules enabled, others off.
  474 |         enabled = set(entitlements.enabled_modules(slug, dsn))
```

<!-- ─── страница 582 ─── -->

```py
    2 | """Reader-portal #222 BACKEND tests — holds + queue, notification inbox, shelves.
    3 | 
    4 | Exercises the real endpoints on a constructed ``core.Api`` (the constructor does
    5 | NOT connect to ИРБИС), with the title-resolution seam stubbed so no live server is
    6 | touched. All state is persisted in OUR access store / notification queue, reader-
    7 | scoped by ticket — nothing is written to the live ИРБИС server.
    8 | 
    9 | Covered (per the #222 contract):
   10 |   * HOLDS: place -> ``ready`` when a copy is free, ``queued``+position when taken; a
   11 |     second reader gets position 2; idempotent re-hold; list; cancel.
   12 |   * INBOX: a circulation-dispatched notice (real ``CirculationEngine._emit`` ->
   13 |     persistent queue) appears in the addressed reader's inbox; unread count drops
   14 |     after marking read (one + all); reader-scoped (one reader can't see another's).
   15 |   * SHELVES: the two system lists seed lazily on first GET; add / remove / dedup;
   16 |     create a custom list.
   17 |   * AUTH: a guest session is refused (401/403) on every reader-portal route.
   18 | 
   19 | Wired into the test_access.py runner (module list) so the CI sqlite + postgres
   20 | legs both run it. Standalone-runnable in the house style:
   21 |   py -3.12 tests/test_portal222.py  -> ok ... + "N passed, M failed" + exit code.
   22 | """
   23 | import os
   24 | import sys
   25 | 
   26 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   27 | 
   28 | from access.store import AccessStore
   29 | from access import notifications as nt
   30 | from access import circulation as circ
   31 | from access.holds import HoldService
   32 | from access.shelves import ShelfService, SYSTEM_IDS
   33 | from access.authz import READER_GRANTS, GUEST_GRANTS
   34 | 
   35 | PASS = [0]
   36 | FAIL = [0]
   37 | 
   38 | 
   39 | def check(name, cond):
   40 |     if cond:
   41 |         PASS[0] += 1
   42 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   43 |     else:
   44 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   45 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   46 | 
   47 | 
   48 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   49 | # A constructed Api with NO live ИРБИС: stub the brief-read seam, and rebuild the
   50 | # reader-portal services over a fresh in-memory access store + queue so each test
   51 | # group is isolated and fast.
   52 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   53 | def _api(brief=None):
   54 |     os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] = 'portal222-test-secret'
   55 |     import importlib
   56 |     import core as _core
```

```py
   57 |     importlib.reload(_core)
   58 |     api = _core.Api()
   59 |     # Fresh, isolated, in-memory persistence (no live server, no temp files).
   60 |     api.access = AccessStore(':memory:')
   61 |     api.notifications = nt.NotificationQueue(':memory:')
   62 |     api.circulation = circ.CirculationEngine(
   63 |         store=circ.CirculationStore(':memory:'), notifications=api.notifications)
   64 |     seam = brief or (lambda db, mfn: {'title': 'Заглавие %d' % mfn})
   65 |     api.holds = HoldService(api.access, catalog=None, brief_read=seam)
   66 |     api.shelves = ShelfService(api.access, brief_read=seam)
   67 |     return api, _core
   68 | 
   69 | 
   70 | def _reader(api, ticket='111'):
   71 |     _tok, sess = api._new_session('reader', 'RI=%s' % ticket, READER_GRANTS,
   72 |                                   tenant='public', rdr_mfn=1)
   73 |     return sess
   74 | 
   75 | 
   76 | def _guest(api):
   77 |     _tok, sess = api._new_session('guest', 'guest', GUEST_GRANTS, tenant='public')
   78 |     return sess
   79 | 
   80 | 
   81 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   82 | # 1. Holds — ready / queued / position / idempotency / list / cancel.
   83 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   84 | def holds_checks():
   85 |     print('-- holds: place (ready/queued), position, cancel')
   86 |     api, _core = _api()
   87 |     r1 = _reader(api, '111')
   88 | 
   89 |     # First hold on a free item (no catalog opinion -> first-in-line surfaced ready).
   90 |     st, payload = api.place_hold(r1, {'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 42})
   91 |     check('place hold returns 200', st == 200)
   92 |     d = payload['data']
   93 |     check('first hold is ready', d['status'] == 'ready')
   94 |     check('first hold position 1', d['position'] == 1)
   95 |     hold1 = d['holdId']
   96 | 
   97 |     # Idempotent re-hold: same reader, same item -> existing hold returned.
   98 |     st, payload = api.place_hold(r1, {'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 42})
   99 |     check('re-hold idempotent (same holdId)', payload['data']['holdId'] == hold1)
  100 |     check('re-hold keeps position 1', payload['data']['position'] == 1)
  101 | 
  102 |     # Second reader on the SAME item -> queued at position 2.
  103 |     r2 = _reader(api, '222')
  104 |     st, payload = api.place_hold(r2, {'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 42})
  105 |     check('second reader queued', payload['data']['status'] == 'queued')
  106 |     check('second reader position 2', payload['data']['position'] == 2)
  107 | 
  108 |     # GET /api/holds for r1: one live hold, title resolved, position present.
  109 |     st, payload = api.list_holds(r1)
  110 |     items = payload['data']['items']
  111 |     check('list_holds returns the reader hold', len(items) == 1)
```

```py
  167 |     ticket = '111'
  168 |     r1 = _reader(api, ticket)
  169 | 
  170 |     # Drive a REAL circulation event addressed to the reader ticket: a return that
  171 |     # frees a queued item enqueues hold_ready to the queue-head reader (= ticket).
  172 |     cstore = api.circulation.store
  173 |     cstore.add_reader('owner', category='В01')
  174 |     cstore.add_reader(ticket, category='В01')      # the reader, keyed by ticket
  175 |     DAY = circ.SECONDS_PER_DAY
  176 |     T0 = 1_700_000_000
  177 |     loan = api.circulation.checkout('owner', 'BK-1', T0).computed['loan']
  178 |     api.circulation.place_hold(ticket, 'BK-1', T0 + DAY)   # reader queues
  179 |     api.circulation.return_item(loan['id'], T0 + 2 * DAY)  # -> hold_ready to ticket
  180 | 
  181 |     # The dispatched notice is now in the persistent queue, addressed to ticket.
  182 |     st, payload = api.notifications_inbox(r1, False)

  187 |     check('notice rendered (non-empty subject)', bool(items[0]['subject']))
  188 |     check('notice starts unread', items[0]['read'] is False)

  190 |     notif_id = items[0]['id']

  195 |     other = _reader(api, '999')

  199 |
```

```py
  260 |           and new_id not in SYSTEM_IDS)
  261 |     api.shelf_add_item(r1, {'listId': new_id, 'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 5})
  262 |     st, payload = api.shelves_list(r1)
  263 |     custom = {l['id']: l for l in payload['data']['lists']}[new_id]
  264 |     check('custom list is not a system list', custom['system'] is False)
  265 |     check('custom list holds the added item',
  266 |           [it['mfn'] for it in custom['items']] == [5])
  267 | 
  268 |     # Reader-scoped: a different reader gets its OWN fresh system lists, no items.
  269 |     r2 = _reader(api, '222')
  270 |     st, payload = api.shelves_list(r2)
  271 |     r2lists = {l['id']: l for l in payload['data']['lists']}
  272 |     check('shelves reader-scoped (r2 has fresh empty system lists)',
  273 |           r2lists['want']['items'] == [] and new_id not in r2lists)
  274 | 
  275 | 
  276 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
```

```py
  277 | # 4. Auth — guest is refused (401/403) on every reader-portal route.
  278 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  279 | def auth_checks():
  280 |     print('-- auth: guest 401/403 on all reader-portal routes')
  281 |     api, _core = _api()
  282 |     guest = _guest(api)
  283 | 
  284 |     routes = [
  285 |         ('POST', '/api/hold', {'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 1}),
  286 |         ('GET', '/api/holds', None),
  287 |         ('POST', '/api/hold/cancel', {'holdId': 1}),
  288 |         ('GET', '/api/notifications', None),
  289 |         ('POST', '/api/notifications/read', {'all': True}),
  290 |         ('GET', '/api/shelves', None),
  291 |         ('POST', '/api/shelves', {'name': 'x'}),
  292 |         ('POST', '/api/shelves/item', {'listId': 'want', 'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 1}),
  293 |         ('POST', '/api/shelves/item/remove', {'listId': 'want', 'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 1}),
  294 |     ]
  295 |     # Direct handler calls raise Denied(403) for a guest (reader session required).
  296 |     handlers = [
  297 |         lambda: api.place_hold(guest, {'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 1}),
  298 |         lambda: api.list_holds(guest),
  299 |         lambda: api.cancel_hold(guest, {'holdId': 1}),
  300 |         lambda: api.notifications_inbox(guest, False),
  301 |         lambda: api.notifications_read(guest, {'all': True}),
  302 |         lambda: api.shelves_list(guest),
  303 |         lambda: api.shelf_create(guest, {'name': 'x'}),
  304 |         lambda: api.shelf_add_item(guest, {'listId': 'want', 'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 1}),
  305 |         lambda: api.shelf_remove_item(guest, {'listId': 'want', 'db': 'IBIS', 'mfn': 1}),
  306 |     ]
  307 |     for (m, p, _b), fn in zip(routes, handlers):
  308 |         denied = False
  309 |         try:
  310 |             fn()
  311 |         except _core.Denied as d:
  312 |             denied = d.status in (401, 403)
  313 |         check('guest denied on %s %s (403)' % (m, p), denied)
  314 | 
  315 |     # And through the real dispatcher: a guest token -> 401/403 status, never 200.
  316 |     headers = {}   # no bearer at all -> no session
  317 |     for m, p, b in routes:
  318 |         st, _payload = api.route(m, p, {}, b, headers)
  319 |         check('route %s %s with no session -> 401/403' % (m, p), st in (401, 403))
  320 | 
  321 |     # A reader, by contrast, is admitted (sanity: the guard isn't blanket-denying).
  322 |     r1 = _reader(api, '111')
  323 |     st, _payload = api.shelves_list(r1)
  324 |     check('reader admitted to /api/shelves (200)', st == 200)
  325 | 
  326 | 
  327 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  328 | # 5. Postgres parity — the reader_hold / reader_shelf store CRUD on real PG.
  329 | # Exercises the %s-style SQL + ON CONFLICT against PostgreSQL when reachable;
  330 | # skips cleanly (not a failure) on the sqlite dev box / when PG/psycopg is absent.
  331 | # This is what proves the schema_postgres.sql DDL + PgAccessStore methods work on
```

```py
  332 | # the CI postgres leg, alongside the sqlite coverage above.
  333 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  334 | def pg_parity_checks():
  335 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
  336 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1'
  337 |     if not want:
  338 |         return
  339 |     try:
  340 |         from access.pgstore import PgAccessStore, default_pg_dsn
  341 |         st = PgAccessStore(os.environ.get('ACCESS_PG_DSN', default_pg_dsn()))
  342 |         st._conn().execute('TRUNCATE reader_hold, reader_shelf, reader_shelf_item '
  343 |                            'RESTART IDENTITY')
  344 |     except Exception as e:
  345 |         print('-- portal222 pg parity SKIPPED (%s: %s)'
  346 |               % (type(e).__name__, str(e).splitlines()[0]))
  347 |         return
  348 |     print('-- portal222: postgres parity (reader_hold / reader_shelf CRUD)')
  349 | 
  350 |     # Holds CRUD on PG via the HoldService (no catalog → first-in-line ready).
  351 |     holds = HoldService(st, catalog=None, brief_read=lambda db, mfn: {'title': 'PG%d' % mfn})
  352 |     r1 = holds.place('pg-1', 'IBIS', 5)
  353 |     check('[pg] first hold ready position 1', r1['status'] == 'ready' and r1['position'] == 1)
  354 |     check('[pg] re-hold idempotent', holds.place('pg-1', 'IBIS', 5)['holdId'] == r1['holdId'])
  355 |     r2 = holds.place('pg-2', 'IBIS', 5)
  356 |     check('[pg] second reader queued position 2',
  357 |           r2['status'] == 'queued' and r2['position'] == 2)
  358 |     check('[pg] list_for is reader-scoped', len(holds.list_for('pg-1')) == 1)
  359 |     check('[pg] cancel works', holds.cancel('pg-1', r1['holdId'])['status'] == 'cancelled')
  360 |     check('[pg] cancelled hold gone from list', holds.list_for('pg-1') == [])
  361 | 
  362 |     # Shelves CRUD on PG: lazy system seed + add/dedup/remove/create.
  363 |     shelves = ShelfService(st, brief_read=lambda db, mfn: {'title': 'PG%d' % mfn})
  364 |     lists = shelves.lists('pg-1')
  365 |     check('[pg] system lists seeded', SYSTEM_IDS <= {l['id'] for l in lists})
  366 |     shelves.add_item('pg-1', 'want', 'IBIS', 10)
  367 |     shelves.add_item('pg-1', 'want', 'IBIS', 10)   # dup
  368 |     want = {l['id']: l for l in shelves.lists('pg-1')}['want']
  369 |     check('[pg] add deduped', len(want['items']) == 1)
  370 |     cust = shelves.create('pg-1', 'PG-список')
  371 |     check('[pg] custom list created', cust['id'] not in SYSTEM_IDS)
  372 |     check('[pg] add to unknown list -> None', shelves.add_item('pg-1', 'nope', 'IBIS', 1) is None)
  373 |     shelves.remove_item('pg-1', 'want', 'IBIS', 10)
  374 |     want = {l['id']: l for l in shelves.lists('pg-1')}['want']
  375 |     check('[pg] item removed', want['items'] == [])
  376 | 
  377 | 
  378 | def main():
  379 |     holds_checks()
  380 |     holds_availability_checks()
  381 |     inbox_checks()
  382 |     shelves_checks()
  383 |     auth_checks()
  384 |     pg_parity_checks()
  385 |     print('\n%d passed, %d failed' % (PASS[0], FAIL[0]))
  386 |     sys.exit(1 if FAIL[0] else 0)
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_rdr40.py`  · строк: 408

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Тесты материализации поля 40 RDR + архива RDR_ARH (рёбра 2.4/2.5).
    3 | 
    4 | Покрывает «связующую ткань» INTEGRATION_MAP, кластер 2:
    5 | 
    6 |   * [2.4] Материализация поля 40 — выдача отдаётся в структуре поля 40 RDR
    7 |     (``RQSTRDR.pft``): подполя ``^A``=903 (шифр), ``^B``=910^B (инв.№),
    8 |     ``^H``=910^H (штрих-код), ``^K``=910^D (место хранения), ``^D``=41 (дата
    9 |     выдачи), ``^E``=42 (срок), ``^F``=дата факт. возврата / ``******`` (долг),
   10 |     ``^G``=имя БД, ``^C``=brief, ``^U``=утеря. Это ДОП. представление поверх
   11 |     существующей loan-модели — её колонки/сценарии не меняются.
   12 | 
   13 |   * [2.5] Архив RDR_ARH — при возврате завершённая выдача **opt-in** (флаг
   14 |     ``archive_on_return``) переносится в архив ``rdr_arh`` (152-ФЗ ретенция) В
   15 |     ДОПОЛНЕНИЕ к ``returned=1``. Без флага — поведение прежнее.
   16 | 
   17 | Back-compat: материализация работает и без каталога (поле 40 строится из
   18 | loan-модели, обогащение из ЭК пропускается); архив выключен по умолчанию;
   19 | checkout/return/renew/лимиты/долг не затронуты.
   20 | 
   21 | Всё гоняется на in-memory store с инъекцией ``today`` (epoch) — детерминизм без
   22 | часов. Standalone-запуск в стиле tests/test_circulation.py::
   23 | 
   24 |     py -3.12 tests/test_rdr40.py   ->  'ok …' + 'N passed, M failed' + код выхода
   25 | 
   26 | sqlite-путь выполняется всегда; PG-паритет (:func:`pg_parity_checks`) повторяет
   27 | те же ассерты поля 40 на каталоге, чья ФЛК питается из PG-access-стора, и
   28 | пропускается чисто, когда PG недоступен. Подключён в раннер tests/test_access.py
   29 | через module_checks.
   30 | """
   31 | import os
   32 | import sys
   33 | 
   34 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   35 | 
   36 | from access import seed_vocab
   37 | from access.catalog import CatalogStore, EXEMPLAR_FREE, EXEMPLAR_ISSUED
   38 | from access.circulation import (
   39 |     CirculationStore, CirculationEngine, default_policy,
   40 |     ALLOW, DENY, REQUIRE_OVERRIDE, SECONDS_PER_DAY, DEBT_MARKER,
   41 | )
   42 | from access.store import AccessStore
   43 | 
   44 | PASS = [0]
   45 | FAIL = [0]
   46 | 
   47 | DAY = SECONDS_PER_DAY
   48 | T0 = 1_700_000_000  # фиксированный epoch-якорь — детерминизм
   49 | 
   50 | 
   51 | def check(name, cond):
```

```py
   63 | def _seeded_access_store():
   64 |     st = AccessStore(':memory:')
   65 |     seed_vocab.seed_vocabularies(st, from_catalog=False)
   66 |     return st
   67 | 
   68 | 
   69 | def _book_with_copy(inv='1024365', shifr='АБ/Я45', barcode='BC-77',
   70 |                     store_place='ЧЗ-1', status=EXEMPLAR_FREE):
   71 |     """Книга с одним экземпляром 910 (инв.``inv``) + 903 шифр.
   72 | 
   73 |     910^a=статус, 910^b=инв.№, 910^h=штрих-код, 910^d=место хранения; 903=шифр.
   74 |     """
   75 |     return {
   76 |         '920': 'PAZK',
   77 |         '200': [{'a': 'Основы каталогизации', 'f': 'Иванова И.И.'}],
   78 |         '700': [{'a': 'Петров П.П.'}],
   79 |         '101': 'rus',
   80 |         '903': shifr,
   81 |         '910': [{'a': status, 'b': inv, 'h': barcode, 'd': store_place}],
   82 |         '907': [{'a': 'Сидорова С.С.'}],
   83 |     }
   84 | 
   85 | 
   86 | def _seed_catalog(cat, db, record):
   87 |     """Положить запись в каталог, изолировав тест от ФЛК-на-save.
   88 | 
   89 |     Тест проверяет материализацию поля 40 (резолв 903/910 → подполя), а НЕ ФЛК.
   90 |     Параллельные сиблинг-агенты правят ФЛК/каталог-save (рёбра 6.2/6.3), что
   91 |     может транзиторно ломать save-пайплайн. Чтобы корректность ЭТОГО теста не
   92 |     зависела от их таймингов, на время засева подменяем ``cat.validate`` на
   93 |     разрешающий результат — запись просто кладётся в стор, дальше работают
   94 |     read-аксессоры (``find_exemplar``/``get``/``brief``)."""
   95 |     orig = cat.validate
   96 |     cat.validate = lambda *a, **k: {
   97 |         'violations': [], 'canSave': True, 'overallSeverity': 0}
   98 |     try:
   99 |         return cat.save(db, record)
  100 |     finally:
  101 |         cat.validate = orig
  102 | 
  103 | 
  104 | def _wired(access_store=None, inv='1024365', shifr='АБ/Я45',
  105 |            barcode='BC-77', store_place='ЧЗ-1', archive_on_return=False):
  106 |     """Каталог с книгой + движок книговыдачи, связанный с каталогом."""
```

```py
  127 |     check('выдача разрешена', d.decision == ALLOW)
  128 |     loan = d.computed['loan']
  129 | 
  130 |     f40 = eng.loan_field40(loan)
  131 | 
  132 |     # ^A = 903 шифр (резолв ЭК по инв.№)

  134 |     # ^B = 910^B инвентарный номер (= loan.item)
  135 |     check('40^B = 910^B инв.№', f40['B'] == inv)
  136 |     # ^H = 910^H штрих-код

  138 |     # ^K = 910^D место хранения

  140 |     # ^D = 41 дата выдачи (epoch checked_out_at)

  142 |     # ^E = 42 срок (due)
  143 |     check('40^E = 42 срок', f40['E'] == loan['due'])
  144 |     check('40^E срок = выдача + max_return_days',
  145 |           f40['E'] == T0 + 20 * DAY)  # В01 max_return_days=20
  146 |     # ^F = маркер долга «на руках»
  147 |     check('40^F = ****** (на руках)', f40['F'] == DEBT_MARKER == '******')
  148 |     # ^G = имя БД ЭК
  149 |     check('40^G = имя БД (IBIS)', f40['G'] == 'IBIS')
  150 |     # ^C = brief (краткое описание)
  151 |     check('40^C = brief непустой', bool(f40['C']))
  152 |     # пока не утеряна — ^U отсутствует
  153 |     check('40^U отсутствует у обычной выдачи', 'U' not in f40)
  154 |
```

```py
  285 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  286 | # Back-compat: checkout/return/renew/лимиты/долг целы под новым кодом.
  287 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  288 | def backcompat_flow_checks():
  289 |     print('-- back-compat: checkout/return/renew/лимиты/долг целы')
  290 |     store = CirculationStore(':memory:')
  291 |     eng = CirculationEngine(store=store, policy=default_policy())
  292 |     store.add_reader('R1', category='В01')  # max_books=5
  293 | 
  294 |     # лимит max_books=5: 6-я требует override
  295 |     for i in range(5):
  296 |         eng.checkout('R1', 'BK-%d' % i, T0)
  297 |     check('5 книг на руках', store.count_on_hand('R1') == 5)
  298 |     d6 = eng.checkout('R1', 'BK-6', T0)
  299 |     check('6-я выдача — require_override (лимит)',
  300 |           d6.decision == REQUIRE_OVERRIDE and 'limit_exceeded' in d6.reasons)
  301 | 
  302 |     # возврат закрывает выдачу
  303 |     loan0 = store.loans_on_hand('R1')[0]
  304 |     dr = eng.return_item(loan0['id'], T0 + 2 * DAY)
  305 |     check('возврат разрешён', dr.decision == ALLOW)
  306 |     check('4 книги на руках после возврата', store.count_on_hand('R1') == 4)
  307 |     check('повторный возврат отклонён',
  308 |           eng.return_item(loan0['id'], T0 + 3 * DAY).decision == DENY)
  309 | 
  310 |     # продление: cap max_prolong=5, потом deny
  311 |     d2 = eng.checkout('R1', 'PRO', T0)
  312 |     pid = d2.computed['loan']['id']
  313 |     for i in range(5):
  314 |         rr = eng.renew(pid, T0 + (i + 1) * DAY)
  315 |         check('продление %d разрешено' % (i + 1), rr.decision == ALLOW) if i == 0 else None
  316 |     check('5 продлений выполнено', store.get_loan(pid)['renewals'] == 5)
  317 |     cap = eng.renew(pid, T0 + 6 * DAY)
  318 |     check('6-е продление — deny (max_prolong)',
  319 |           cap.decision == DENY and 'max_prolong_reached' in cap.reasons)
  320 | 
  321 |     # долг: твёрдый должник блокируется на выдаче
  322 |     store.add_reader('D1', category='В01')
  323 |     store.add_loan('D1', 'OLD', T0 - 30 * DAY, T0 - 50 * DAY)  # 30 дн. просрочки
  324 |     dd = eng.checkout('D1', 'NEW', T0)
  325 |     check('твёрдый должник заблокирован',
  326 |           dd.decision == REQUIRE_OVERRIDE and 'reader_has_debt' in dd.reasons)
```

```py
  342 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  343 | # PG-паритет: те же ассерты поля 40 на каталоге с PG-access-стором (ФЛК), либо
  344 | # чистый пропуск, когда PG недоступен.
  345 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  346 | def _pg_reachable(dsn):
  347 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
  348 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1'
  349 |     if not want:
  350 |         return False
  351 |     try:
  352 |         from access import pgstore
  353 |         conn = pgstore._admin_conn(dsn)
  354 |         conn.close()
  355 |         return True
  356 |     except Exception as e:
  357 |         print('-- rdr40 PG-паритет ПРОПУЩЕН (%s: %s)'
  358 |               % (type(e).__name__, str(e).splitlines()[0]))
  359 |         return False
  360 | 
  361 | 
  362 | def pg_parity_checks():
  363 |     print('-- PG-паритет: поле 40 backend-независимо')
  364 |     from access import pgstore
  365 |     dsn = pgstore.default_pg_dsn()
  366 |     if not _pg_reachable(dsn):
  367 |         return
  368 |     print('-- rdr40: postgres', dsn.rsplit('@', 1)[-1])
  369 |     slug = 'rdr40_pg'
  370 |     try:
  371 |         pgstore.deprovision_tenant(slug, dsn)
  372 |     except Exception:
  373 |         pass
  374 |     sa = pgstore.provision_tenant(slug, 'RDR40 PG', 'публичная', dsn)
  375 |     # каталог с ФЛК, питаемой PG-стором; круг материализации поля 40 тот же —
  376 |     # store/каталог остаются sqlite (own-store ADR-004), а паритет проверяет, что
  377 |     # подполя поля 40 идентичны при PG-питаемой валидации.
  378 |     cat, store, eng, inv = _wired(access_store=sa)
  379 |     d = eng.checkout('R1', inv, T0)
  380 |     f40 = eng.loan_field40(d.computed['loan'])
  381 |     check('[pg] 40^A = 903 шифр', f40['A'] == 'АБ/Я45')
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_reader_auth.py`  · строк: 403

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Тесты двух reader-гэпов под пилот замены jirbis (бэкенд):
    3 | 
    4 |   1. Аутентификация читателя С ПАРОЛЕМ (security-critical): билет + пароль,
    5 |      сверка с полем пароля записи RDR (130/100), открытый текст ИЛИ несолёный
    6 |      MD5; флаг REQUIRE_READER_PASSWORD; неверный пароль -> 401.
    7 |   2. «Вы имели в виду» (GET /api/suggest): похожие термины словаря на опечатку,
    8 |      ранжирование по близости, публичный доступ (гость), деградация без 500;
    9 |      плюс ветка didYouMean в /api/search на пустую выдачу.
   10 | 
   11 | Стиль — как tests/test_discovery.py: сконструированный ``Api`` (его конструктор
   12 | НЕ подключается к ИРБИС) с маленькой in-memory заглушкой ``FakeIrbis``,
   13 | подменяющей ``api.irbis``. Живой ИРБИС-сервер НЕ нужен; sqlite-стор берётся
   14 | in-memory (``ACCESS_DB=:memory:``), так что набор полностью зелёный без БД на диске.
   15 | 
   16 | Подключён к раннеру tests/test_access.py через generic ``module_checks`` (он
   17 | импортирует модуль и зовёт каждый ``*_checks()``, складывая PASS/FAIL), поэтому
   18 | тот же CI-шаг, что гоняет test_access.py, гоняет и этот набор без правок .github/.
   19 | 
   20 | Отдельно тоже::
   21 | 
   22 |     py -3.12 tests/test_reader_auth.py   ->  'ok …' + 'N passed, M failed'
   23 | """
   24 | import hashlib
   25 | import importlib
   26 | import os
   27 | import sys
   28 | 
```

```py
   44 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   45 | # Заглушка ИРБИС: только поверхность, которую трогают auth_reader / suggest.
   46 | # Записи в форме irbis.parser.parse_record (dict со списком 'fields').
   47 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   48 | def _fld(tag, value, subfields=None):
   49 |     return {'tag': tag, 'value': value, 'text': value, 'subfields': subfields or {}}
   50 | 
   51 | 
   52 | class FakeIrbis:
   53 |     """In-memory стенд под SessionManager.
   54 | 
   55 |     * ``readers``: {ticket: {'fields': [...], 'mfn': N}} — записи RDR по билету.
   56 |     * ``terms``: {db: [(count, term), ...]} — словарь для read_terms (отсортирован).
   57 |     * ``raise_on``: имена методов, которые должны бросать IrbisError (ветки
   58 |       деградации)."""
   59 | 
   60 |     def __init__(self, readers=None, terms=None, raise_on=()):
   61 |         self._readers = readers or {}
   62 |         self._terms = terms or {}
   63 |         self.raise_on = set(raise_on)
   64 | 
   65 |     def _maybe_raise(self, name):
   66 |         if name in self.raise_on:
   67 |             from irbis.client import IrbisError
   68 |             raise IrbisError(-1, 'stub raise %s' % name)
   69 | 
   70 |     def search(self, db, expr, first=1, maxn=0):
   71 |         self._maybe_raise('search')
   72 |         # Только запросы вида '"RI=<ticket>"' и '"<prefix>=<value>$"' нас интересуют.
   73 |         inner = expr.strip().strip('"')
   74 |         if inner.startswith('RI='):
   75 |             ticket = inner[3:]
   76 |             rec = self._readers.get(ticket)
   77 |             if rec:
   78 |                 return 1, [rec['mfn']]
   79 |             return 0, []
   80 |         # обычный поиск по словарю: считаем «попадание» по точному термину
   81 |         for _db, rows in self._terms.items():
   82 |             for cnt, term in rows:
   83 |                 if term == inner.rstrip('$'):
```

```py
   87 |     def read_record(self, db, mfn):

   89 |         for rec in self._readers.values():

   95 |     def read_terms(self, db, start, count=20):

   97 |         rows = self._terms.get(db, [])
   98 |         out = [(c, t) for (c, t) in rows if t >= start]
   99 |         return out[:count]

  119 | def _guest(api):
  120 |     from access.authz import GUEST_GRANTS
  121 |     _tok, sess = api._new_session('guest', 'guest', GUEST_GRANTS, tenant='public')

  125 | def _reader_rec(mfn, name='Иванов', pwd_tag=None, pwd_value=None, extra=None):
  126 |     fields = [_fld('30', 'RI=', {'RI': str(mfn)}), _fld('10', name)]
  127 |     if pwd_tag and pwd_value is not None:
  128 |         fields.append(_fld(pwd_tag, pwd_value))
  129 |     for f in (extra or []):
  130 |         fields.append(f)
  131 |     return {'mfn': mfn, 'fields': fields}
  132 |
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3

    3 | 
    4 | Связь иерархии хранится зеркальной парой ``HOST=``/``LINK=`` в ``record_index``:
    5 | издание-хозяин несёт ключ-идентичность под ``HOST=`` (200^a заглавие / 11^a ISSN /
    6 | 903 шифр), зависимая запись — тот же ключ как отсылку под ``LINK=`` (463/461/481/
    7 | 963). ``CatalogStore.linked_records(db, mfn, kind)`` обходит ребро в обе стороны:
    8 | ``kind='children'`` от хозяина → зависимые; ``kind='host'`` от зависимой → хозяин.
    9 | 
   10 | Что проверяется:
```

```py
   11 |   1. 9.1 статья↔журнал (463): хозяин 'children' → статьи; статья 'host' → журнал;
   12 |      посторонний журнал не примешивает чужую статью.
   13 |   2. 9.2 номер↔журнал (461) — обе стороны.

   50 | _INV = [0]

   53 | def _rec(title, **extra):
   54 |     """Валидная книжная запись (проходит ФЛК на сохранении) с уникальным экз.№."""
   55 |     _INV[0] += 1
   56 |     base = {
   57 |         '920': 'PAZK',
   58 |         '200': [{'a': title}],
   59 |         '700': [{'a': 'Автор'}],
   60 |         '610': [{'': 'тест'}],
   61 |         '101': 'rus',
   62 |         '910': [{'a': '0', 'b': 'INV%05d' % _INV[0]}],
   63 |     }
   64 |     base.update(extra)
   65 |     return base
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_resilience.py`  · строк: 525

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Backend hardening tests (issue: harden ИРБИС connection + public-only OPAC).
    3 | 
    4 | Two independent concerns, both fully sqlite-green and requiring NO live ИРБИС:
    5 | 
    6 |   A. SELF-HEAL — ``core.ResilientIrbis`` wraps the SessionManager so a STALE
    7 |      registered session (after a server Стоп/Старт, or the demo "max 3 clients"
    8 |      cap evicting us) RE-REGISTERS and retries instead of failing every request
    9 |      until a manual process restart. We assert: a first-call connection error
   10 |      re-registers and succeeds on retry; a stale-session IRBIS return code
```

<!-- ─── страница 607 ─── -->

```py
   11 |      (-3337/-3338/-33xx) self-heals the same way; a DATA-level code (-140 missing
   12 |      MFN) propagates unchanged (it's a real answer, not a dead session); retries
   13 |      are bounded (exhaustion raises); health()/ping() reflect reachability.
   14 | 
   15 |   B. PUBLIC-ONLY OPAC — a guest/reader session may only touch the configured
   16 |      PUBLIC bibliographic bases (Config.public_dbs, default {IBIS}); a non-public
   17 |      db (RDR readers, LICH/PAY/RIGHT ПДн, RQST/CMPL service, LOG*) is refused 403
   18 |      on search/record/render/terms/showcase/rubricator/facets/cover/
   19 |      example-queries, and is hidden from /api/databases. STAFF keep access per
   20 |      their grants (the public confinement does not apply to them).
   21 | 
   22 | House style mirrors tests/test_discovery.py — a constructed ``Api`` (whose
   23 | constructor does NOT connect to ИРБИС) with stubs injected over ``api.irbis``.
   24 | Invoked by the tests/test_access.py runner via the generic ``module_checks``
   25 | loop, so the existing CI step that runs test_access.py also runs this suite with
   26 | no .github/ workflow change.
   27 | 
   28 | Standalone too::
   29 | 
   30 |     py -3.12 tests/test_resilience.py   ->  'ok …' lines + 'N passed, M failed'
   31 | """
   32 | import os
   33 | import sys
   34 | 
   35 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   36 | 
   37 | import importlib
   38 | 
   39 | PASS = [0]
   40 | FAIL = [0]
   41 | 
   42 | 
   43 | def check(name, cond):
   44 |     if cond:
   45 |         PASS[0] += 1
   46 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   47 |     else:
   48 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   49 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   50 | 
   51 | 
   52 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   53 | # Stubs.
   54 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   55 | class _FakeClient:
   56 |     """The bits of irbis.client.IrbisClient that ResilientIrbis._reset_session
   57 |     touches (so we can observe the client_id rotation on re-register)."""
   58 | 
   59 |     def __init__(self):
   60 |         self.connected = True
   61 |         self.client_id = 123456
   62 | 
   63 | 
   64 | class FlakySession:
   65 |     """Stand-in for irbis.SessionManager.
```

<!-- ─── страница 608 ─── -->

```py
   66 | 
   67 |     ``fail_plan`` maps a method name -> a list of exceptions to raise on the first
   68 |     N calls (one per entry), after which the call succeeds and returns
   69 |     ``results[name]``. ``register_count`` counts how often the wrapper forced a
   70 |     re-register (via _reset_session marking us disconnected, the way a real
   71 |     re-register is observed)."""
   72 | 
   73 |     def __init__(self, fail_plan=None, results=None):
   74 |         self._fail_plan = {k: list(v) for k, v in (fail_plan or {}).items()}
   75 |         self._results = results or {}
   76 |         self._calls = {}
   77 |         self.connected = True
   78 |         self._client = _FakeClient()
   79 |         self.reset_count = 0
   80 |         self.closed = False
   81 | 
   82 |     def _dispatch(self, name):
   83 |         self._calls[name] = self._calls.get(name, 0) + 1
   84 |         plan = self._fail_plan.get(name)
   85 |         if plan:
   86 |             raise plan.pop(0)
   87 |         return self._results.get(name, ('OK', name))
   88 | 
   89 |     # SessionManager surface that ResilientIrbis proxies / touches
   90 |     def max_mfn(self, db):
   91 |         return self._dispatch('max_mfn')
   92 | 
   93 |     def search(self, db, expr, first=1, maxn=0):
   94 |         return self._dispatch('search')
   95 | 
   96 |     def read_record(self, db, mfn):
   97 |         return self._dispatch('read_record')
   98 | 
   99 |     def format_record(self, db, mfn, pft='@brief'):
  100 |         return self._dispatch('format_record')
  101 | 
  102 |     def read_terms(self, db, start, count=20):
  103 |         return self._dispatch('read_terms')
  104 | 
  105 |     def read_file(self, spec):
  106 |         return self._dispatch('read_file')
  107 | 
  108 |     def update_record(self, db, lines, lock=0, actualize=1):
  109 |         return self._dispatch('update_record')
  110 | 
  111 |     def server_version(self):
  112 |         return self._dispatch('server_version')
  113 | 
  114 |     def close(self):
  115 |         self.closed = True
  116 | 
  117 |     def calls(self, name):
  118 |         return self._calls.get(name, 0)
  119 | 
  120 | 
```

```py
  121 | def _irbis_error(code):
  122 |     from irbis.client import IrbisError
  123 |     return IrbisError(code, 'stub %s' % code)
  124 | 
  125 | 
  126 | def _resilient(fail_plan=None, results=None, retries=2, backoff=0.0):
  127 |     """Build a ResilientIrbis over a FlakySession (backoff 0 keeps tests fast)."""
  128 |     import core as _core
  129 |     importlib.reload(_core)
  130 |     sm = FlakySession(fail_plan=fail_plan, results=results)
  131 |     wrapper = _core.ResilientIrbis(sm, retries=retries, backoff=backoff)
  132 |     return wrapper, sm, _core
  133 | 
  134 | 
  135 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  136 | # A. ResilientIrbis self-heal.
  137 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  138 | def reconnect_on_socket_error_checks():
  139 |     print('-- ResilientIrbis: socket error -> re-register + retry')
  140 |     # First search() raises a ConnectionError (dropped TCP / server restart),
  141 |     # the retry succeeds.
  142 |     w, sm, _ = _resilient(fail_plan={'search': [ConnectionError('reset by peer')]},
  143 |                           results={'search': (7, [1, 2, 3])})
  144 |     cid_before = sm._client.client_id
  145 |     out = w.search('IBIS', '"K=x"')
  146 |     check('search retried after socket error -> success', out == (7, [1, 2, 3]))
  147 |     check('search was attempted twice (fail + retry)', sm.calls('search') == 2)
  148 |     check('re-register rotated client_id (new server session)',
  149 |           sm._client.client_id != cid_before)
  150 |     check('reset marked session disconnected before retry',
  151 |           sm.connected is False)
  152 |     check('last_error cleared after the successful retry', w.last_error is None)
  153 |     check('last_ok_ts recorded after success', w.last_ok_ts is not None)
  154 | 
  155 | 
  156 | def reconnect_on_stale_session_code_checks():
  157 |     print('-- ResilientIrbis: stale-session IRBIS code -> re-register + retry')
  158 |     # A -3338 (CLIENT_NOT_ALLOWED, also seen when the server forgot our client_id
  159 |     # after a restart) self-heals: drop the session, re-register, retry.
  160 |     for code in (-3337, -3338, -3310):
  161 |         w, sm, _ = _resilient(fail_plan={'read_record': [_irbis_error(code)]},
  162 |                               results={'read_record': {'mfn': 5}})
  163 |         out = w.read_record('IBIS', 5)
  164 |         check('stale code %d self-heals on retry' % code, out == {'mfn': 5})
  165 |         check('stale code %d retried once' % code, sm.calls('read_record') == 2)
  166 | 
  167 | 
  168 | def data_level_code_propagates_checks():
  169 |     print('-- ResilientIrbis: data-level IRBIS code propagates (NOT a dead session)')
  170 |     from irbis.client import IrbisError
  171 |     # -140 (no such MFN) and -603 (deleted) are real answers — must NOT be retried
  172 |     # or swallowed; they propagate unchanged for the endpoint to handle.
  173 |     for code in (-140, -603):
  174 |         w, sm, _ = _resilient(fail_plan={'read_record': [_irbis_error(code),
  175 |                                                           _irbis_error(code)]})
```

```py
  176 |         raised = None
  177 |         try:
  178 |             w.read_record('IBIS', 999)
  179 |         except IrbisError as e:
  180 |             raised = e.code
  181 |         check('data code %d propagates unchanged' % code, raised == code)
  182 |         check('data code %d NOT retried (single attempt)' % code,
  183 |               sm.calls('read_record') == 1)

  186 | def retries_are_bounded_checks():
  187 |     print('-- ResilientIrbis: bounded retries (exhaustion raises)')
  188 |     from irbis.client import IrbisError
  189 |     # Every attempt fails with a connection error -> after retries exhausted, raise.
  190 |     w, sm, _ = _resilient(
  191 |         fail_plan={'max_mfn': [ConnectionError('down')] * 9}, retries=2)
  192 |     raised = False
  193 |     try:
  194 |         w.max_mfn('IBIS')
  195 |     except IrbisError:
  196 |         raised = True
  197 |     check('exhausted reconnect raises IrbisError', raised)
  198 |     check('attempted exactly retries+1 (3) times', sm.calls('max_mfn') == 3)
  199 |     check('last_error retained after exhaustion', bool(w.last_error))
  200 | 
  201 |     # Stale IRBIS code that never clears also exhausts and re-raises that error.
  202 |     w2, sm2, _ = _resilient(
  203 |         fail_plan={'search': [_irbis_error(-3338)] * 9}, retries=1)
  204 |     raised2 = None
  205 |     try:
  206 |         w2.search('IBIS', '"K=x"')
  207 |     except IrbisError as e:
  208 |         raised2 = e.code
  209 |     check('persistent stale code exhausts and re-raises it', raised2 == -3338)
  210 |     check('persistent stale code attempted retries+1 (2) times',
  211 |           sm2.calls('search') == 2)
  212 | 
  213 | 
  214 | def health_and_ping_checks():
  215 |     print('-- ResilientIrbis: health()/ping() reflect reachability')
  216 |     from irbis.client import IrbisError
  217 |     w, sm, _ = _resilient(results={'server_version': '2022.1'})
  218 |     check('ping() True when server reachable', w.ping() is True)
  219 |     h = w.health()
  220 |     check('health() exposes lastError/lastOkTs/retries',
  221 |           set(('lastError', 'lastOkTs', 'retries')) <= set(h))
  222 |     check('health() lastError None when healthy', h['lastError'] is None)
  223 | 
  224 |     # server unreachable on every attempt -> ping False, never raises
  225 |     w2, sm2, _ = _resilient(fail_plan={'server_version': [ConnectionError('x')] * 9},
  226 |                             retries=1)
  227 |     check('ping() False when server unreachable (no raise)', w2.ping() is False)
  228 |     check('health() records lastError when down', bool(w2.health()['lastError']))
  229 | 
  230 |     # close() proxies to the underlying SessionManager
```

```py
  235 | def is_stale_session_helper_checks():
  236 |     print('-- _is_stale_session classifier')
  237 |     import core as _core
  238 |     importlib.reload(_core)
  239 |     f = _core._is_stale_session
  240 |     check('-3337 is stale', f(-3337))
  241 |     check('-3338 is stale', f(-3338))
  242 |     check('-3300/-3349 range is stale', f(-3300) and f(-3349) and f(-3325))
  243 |     check('-140 (missing MFN) is NOT stale', not f(-140))

  250 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  251 | # B. Public-only OPAC enforcement (constructed Api + discovery-style FakeIrbis).
  252 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  253 | def _fld(tag, value, subfields=None):
  254 |     return {'tag': tag, 'value': value, 'text': value, 'subfields': subfields or {}}
  255 | 
  256 | 
  257 | class OpacFake:
  258 |     """Minimal ИРБИС stub for the Api OPAC endpoints (records/terms/menu/maxmfn).
  259 |     The public-DB guard must reject a non-public db BEFORE any of these run, so a
  260 |     403 path never reaches the stub — but a public db must flow through to it."""
  261 | 
  262 |     def __init__(self, dbmenu=''):
  263 |         self._dbmenu = dbmenu
  264 |         self.touched = []          # dbs that actually reached IRBIS (public path)
  265 | 
  266 |     def max_mfn(self, db):
  267 |         self.touched.append(db)
  268 |         return 6
  269 | 
  270 |     def read_record(self, db, mfn):
  271 |         self.touched.append(db)
  272 |         return {'mfn': mfn, 'status': '0', 'version': '1', 'guid': None,
  273 |                 'fields': [_fld('200', 'T %d' % mfn, {'A': 'T %d' % mfn})]}
  274 | 
  275 |     def search(self, db, expr, first=1, maxn=0):
  276 |         self.touched.append(db)
  277 |         return 1, [1]
  278 | 
  279 |     def read_terms(self, db, start, count=20):
  280 |         self.touched.append(db)
  281 |         return [(3, '%s%s' % (start, 'X'))]
  282 | 
  283 |     def read_file(self, spec):
  284 |         return self._dbmenu
  285 | 
```

```py
  515 |     public_db_guard_staff_allowed_checks()
  516 |     databases_visibility_checks()
  517 |     public_db_config_override_checks()
  518 |     route_public_db_checks()
  519 |     health_endpoint_checks()
  520 |     print('\n%d passed, %d failed' % (PASS[0], FAIL[0]))
  521 |     sys.exit(1 if FAIL[0] else 0)
  522 | 
  523 | 
  524 | if __name__ == '__main__':
  525 |     main()
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_rights_lich.py`  · строк: 493

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """RIGHT/LICH — права и личный кабинет ПТ (кластер 7, рёбра 7.2/7.3/7.4/7.5) — тест.
    3 | 
    4 | Покрывает:
    5 | 
    6 |   * RIGHT (``access/rights.py``):
    7 |       - ``access_level`` — гейтинг по категории читателя: deny/view/download,
    8 |         выбор МАКСИМАЛЬНОГО разрешённого уровня среди правил, период
    9 |         общий/правила, пустой шаблон → полный доступ (955^B пусто), нет правил →
   10 |         download в периоде / deny вне периода (ребро 7.3);
   11 |       - ``RightStore`` — upsert/get/list/delete шаблонов;
   12 |       - ``RightService`` — резолв 955^B каталожной записи через опц. catalog-handle
   13 |         → id шаблона → уровень доступа (ребро 7.2); back-compat без handle
   14 |         (template_id/template напрямую);
   15 |       - ``page_limit`` — лимит страниц ^F по категории.
   16 |   * LICH (``access/lich.py``):
   17 |       - закладки (поле 3): add/remove идемпотентно, отсортированы (ребро 7.4);
   18 |       - рейтинг (поле 7): 0..5, выход за диапазон → ValueError (ребро 7.4);
   19 |       - счётчик скачанных (поле 4): инкремент (ребро 7.4);
   20 |       - ``download_budget = RIGHT.^F − LICH.v4`` и ``can_download`` (ребро 7.5).
   21 |   * PG-ПАРИТЕТ — когда postgres доступен (иначе чисто пропускается): тот же
   22 |     upsert/get RIGHT и закладки/скачано/рейтинг LICH на PG-DSN.
   23 | 
   24 | Вписан в раннер tests/test_access.py (его module-list) через ``module_checks`` —
   25 | раннер вызывает каждую ``*_checks()`` и складывает PASS/FAIL.
   26 | 
   27 | Standalone:  py tests/test_rights_lich.py
   28 | PG:          (set ACCESS_BACKEND=postgres) py -3.12 tests/test_rights_lich.py
   29 | """
   30 | import os
   31 | import sys
   32 | 
   33 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   34 | 
   35 | from access import rights as rights_mod
```

```py
  222 | # 4. RightService — резолв 955^B через catalog-handle (ребро 7.2) + back-compat.
  223 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  224 | class _FakeCatalog:
  225 |     """Минимальный catalog-handle: get(db, mfn) -> record (как CatalogStore.get)."""
  226 |     def __init__(self, records):
  227 |         self._records = records  # {(db, mfn): record-dict}
  228 | 
  229 |     def get(self, db, mfn):
  230 |         return self._records.get((db, mfn))
  231 | 
  232 | 
  233 | def right_service_checks():
  234 |     print('-- rights: RightService (955^B resolve via catalog handle / back-compat)')

  236 |     st.upsert('R1', period=TEMPLATE['period'], rules=TEMPLATE['rules'])
  237 | 
  238 |     # Каталожные записи: ресурс с 955^B=R1; ресурс без 955^B; повторяющееся 955.
  239 |     cat = _FakeCatalog({
  240 |         ('IBIS', 10): {'955': {'a': 'book.pdf', 'b': 'R1'}},        # 955^B → R1
  241 |         ('IBIS', 11): {'955': {'a': 'free.pdf'}},                   # 955 без ^B
  242 |         ('IBIS', 12): {'200': {'a': 'Нет 955 вовсе'}},              # нет 955
  243 |         ('IBIS', 13): {'955': [{'a': 'x.pdf'}, {'b': 'R1'}]},       # ^B во 2-м повторении
  244 |     })
  245 |     svc = RightService(store=st, catalog=cat, now=lambda: _ts(TODAY))
  246 | 
  247 |     # 7.2: резолв 955^B → id шаблона.
  248 |     check('955^B → template id (R1)', svc.template_id_for_record('IBIS', 10) == 'R1')
  249 |     check('955 без ^B → None', svc.template_id_for_record('IBIS', 11) is None)
  250 |     check('нет 955 → None', svc.template_id_for_record('IBIS', 12) is None)
  251 |     check('^B в повторении 955 → R1', svc.template_id_for_record('IBIS', 13) == 'R1')
  252 | 
  253 |     # 7.2+7.3: уровень доступа по записи (handle + стор + категория).
  254 |     check('по записи: преподаватель → download',
```

```py
  289 | # 5. LICH — закладки/рейтинг/счётчик (ребро 7.4).
  290 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  291 | def lich_basics_checks():

  293 |     st = LichStore(':memory:')
  294 |     svc = LichService(st)
  295 |     R, T = 'RI=111', 'IBIS/АБ123'

  298 |     check('нет записи: закладок []', svc.bookmarks(R, T) == [])
  299 |     check('нет записи: рейтинг None', svc.rating(R, T) is None)
  300 |     check('нет записи: скачано 0', svc.downloaded(R, T) == 0)
  301 | 
  302 |     # Закладки (поле 3): add идемпотентно и отсортировано.

  304 |     check('add закладку 2 → [2,5]', svc.add_bookmark(R, T, 2) == [2, 5])
  305 |     check('add дубль 5 → [2,5] (идемпотентно)', svc.add_bookmark(R, T, 5) == [2, 5])
  306 |     check('bookmarks читает [2,5]', svc.bookmarks(R, T) == [2, 5])
  307 |     check('remove 2 → [5]', svc.remove_bookmark(R, T, 2) == [5])
  308 |     check('remove отсутствующей → no-op [5]', svc.remove_bookmark(R, T, 99) == [5])
  309 |     # Невалидный номер страницы → ValueError.
  310 |     bad = False
```

```py
  408 | def _pg_reachable_dsn():
  409 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
  410 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1'
  411 |     if not want:
  412 |         return None
  413 |     try:
  414 |         from access import pgstore
  415 |         dsn = pgstore.default_pg_dsn()
  416 |         conn = pgstore._admin_conn(dsn)
  417 |         conn.execute('DROP TABLE IF EXISTS right_template')
  418 |         conn.execute('DROP TABLE IF EXISTS lich_entry')
  419 |         conn.close()
  420 |         return dsn
```

### Файл: `irbis-web/backend/tests/test_search_expr.py`  · строк: 48

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Multi-field default search expression (#245).
    3 | 
    4 | The portal used to default to the K= (keyword) index, which is unreliable on the
    5 | СПб ГТБ server (dictionary shows postings but search returns 0). The default is now
    6 | a multi-field OR across title + author + keywords, uppercased to match the
    7 | dictionary form, so a cyrillic query returns hits regardless of the K= index state.
    8 | 
    9 | Standalone:  py tests/test_search_expr.py
   10 | """
   11 | import sys
   12 | import os
   13 | 
   14 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   15 | 
   16 | from core import build_expr
   17 | 
   18 | PASS = [0]
   19 | FAIL = [0]
   20 | 
   21 | 
   22 | def check(name, cond):
   23 |     if cond:
   24 |         PASS[0] += 1
   25 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   26 |     else:
   27 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   28 |         print('  FAIL', name)
   29 | 
   30 | 
   31 | def search_expr_checks():
   32 |     print('-- build_expr multi-field default (#245)')
   33 |     che = ''.join(chr(x) for x in [0x447, 0x435, 0x445, 0x43e, 0x432])  # 'чехов' lowercase
   34 |     CHE = che.upper()
   35 |     allx = build_expr({'q': [che], 'prefix': ['ALL']})
   36 |     check('ALL -> uppercased OR across T/A/K',
   37 |           allx == '"T=%s$" + "A=%s$" + "K=%s$"' % (CHE, CHE, CHE))
```

<!-- ─── страница 626 ─── -->

```py
   38 |     check('no prefix defaults to multi-field', build_expr({'q': [che]}) == allx)
   39 |     check('sentinel * also multi-field', build_expr({'q': [che], 'prefix': ['*']}) == allx)
   40 |     check('single prefix T still single', build_expr({'q': [che], 'prefix': ['T']}).startswith('"T=%s' % che))
   41 |     check('explicit expr passthrough', build_expr({'expr': ['"A=X"']}) == '"A=X"')
   42 |     check('empty q -> None', build_expr({'q': ['']}) is None)
   43 | 
   44 | 
   45 | if __name__ == '__main__':
   46 |     search_expr_checks()
   47 |     print('\nSEARCH-EXPR: %d passed, %d failed' % (PASS[0], FAIL[0]))
   48 |     sys.exit(1 if FAIL[0] else 0)
```

```py
    1 | #!/usr/bin/env python3
    2 | """Per-tenant vocabulary seeding tests (gap A5, epic #188).
    3 | 
    4 | Covers the seeding engine layered on the merged tenancy (#100) + Identity (#101):
    5 | 
    6 |   1. Parsers (pure, always run): ``parse_mnu`` round-trips incl. CP1251 bytes and
    7 |      the ``*****`` terminator (recon #VOC-03 empty final pair filtered);
    8 |      ``parse_tre`` recovers depth + parent from leading dots.
    9 |   2. sqlite dev seed (pure, always run): seeding the single-tenant ``public`` store
   10 |      populates SYSTEM vocabs with values and creates INSTITUTION vocabs EMPTY;
   11 |      re-seed is idempotent.
   12 |   3. PostgreSQL (skipped cleanly when PG unavailable): provision a tenant ->
   13 |      system vocabs present & populated, institution vocabs present & empty;
   14 |      per-tenant isolation (tenant A's vocab edit invisible to B); re-provision
   15 |      idempotent.
   16 | 
   17 | Invoked by the test_access.py runner (seeding_checks) so the CI postgres step
   18 | exercises the PG path with NO .github/ change. The sqlite suite stays green
   19 | locally with no DB.
   20 | """
   21 | import os
   22 | import sys
   23 | 
   24 | sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))

   27 | from access import seed_vocab

   32 | 
   33 |

   36 |         PASS[0] += 1
   37 |         print('  ok  ', name)
   38 |     else:
   39 |         FAIL[0] += 1
   40 |         print('  FAIL', name)
```

```py
   45 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   46 | def parser_checks():
   47 |     print('-- parsers (.mnu / .tre)')
   48 | 
   49 |     # Basic round-trip from a str menu with the ***** terminator.
   50 |     text = 'KN\nКниги в целом\n05\nОднотомное издание\n*****\n'
   51 |     pairs = _mnu.parse_mnu(text)
   52 |     check('parse_mnu pairs count', pairs == [('KN', 'Книги в целом'),
   53 |                                              ('05', 'Однотомное издание')])
   54 | 
   55 |     # CP1251 BYTES decode -> UTF-8 (the on-disk ИРБИS encoding).
   56 |     raw = ('rus\nРусский\neng\nАнглийский\n*****\n').encode('cp1251')
   57 |     pairs_cp = _mnu.parse_mnu(raw)
   58 |     check('parse_mnu decodes cp1251 bytes',
   59 |           pairs_cp == [('rus', 'Русский'), ('eng', 'Английский')])
   60 | 
   61 |     # Empty final pair before ***** is dropped (recon #VOC-03).
   62 |     artefact = 'a\nAlpha\nb\nBeta\n\n\n*****\n'
   63 |     check('parse_mnu drops empty final pair',
   64 |           _mnu.parse_mnu(artefact) == [('a', 'Alpha'), ('b', 'Beta')])
   65 | 
   66 |     # Stops at ***** — nothing after the terminator leaks in.
   67 |     after = 'a\nAlpha\n*****\nGARBAGE\nMORE\n'
   68 |     check('parse_mnu stops at terminator', _mnu.parse_mnu(after) == [('a', 'Alpha')])
   69 | 
   70 |     # Real seed data round-trips through a serialize->parse cycle (ste.mnu, 14).
   71 |     ste = seed_data.SYSTEM_VOCABS['ste.mnu']['values']
   72 |     serialized = ''.join('%s\n%s\n' % (c, l) for c, l in ste) + '*****\n'
   73 |     check('parse_mnu round-trips ste.mnu (14)',
   74 |           _mnu.parse_mnu(serialized.encode('cp1251')) == ste)
   75 | 
   76 |     # .tre: leading dots -> depth; parent reconstructed by the level stack.
   77 |     tre = 'Root\n.Child A\n..Leaf A1\n.Child B\n'
   78 |     nodes = _mnu.parse_tre(tre)
   79 |     check('parse_tre node count', len(nodes) == 4)
   80 |     check('parse_tre depths', [n['depth'] for n in nodes] == [0, 1, 2, 1])
   81 |     check('parse_tre root has no parent', nodes[0]['parent'] is None)
   82 |     check('parse_tre child parent is root', nodes[1]['parent'] == 0)
   83 |     check('parse_tre leaf parent is child A', nodes[2]['parent'] == 1)
   84 |     check('parse_tre second child reparents to root', nodes[3]['parent'] == 0)
   85 | 
   86 |     # live extraction is a documented stub, must raise (not silently no-op)
   87 |     raised = False
   88 |     try:
   89 |         _mnu.import_mnu_from_source('kv.mnu')
   90 |     except NotImplementedError:
   91 |         raised = True
   92 |     check('source-ИРБИS extraction is a documented stub', raised)
   93 | 
   94 |

   96 | # 2. sqlite dev seed — pure (in-memory db, no server).
   97 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   98 | def sqlite_seed_checks():
   99 |     print('-- sqlite seed (single-tenant public)')
```

```py
  155 | # 3. PostgreSQL — provision -> seeded; isolation; idempotent re-provision.
  156 | # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
  157 | A_SLUG, B_SLUG = 'seed_a', 'seed_b'
  158 | 
  159 | 
  160 | def pg_reachable(dsn):
  161 |     want = os.environ.get('ACCESS_BACKEND', '').lower() in ('postgres', 'pg') \
  162 |         or os.environ.get('ACCESS_TEST_PG') == '1'
  163 |     if not want:
  164 |         return False
  165 |     try:
  166 |         from access import pgstore
  167 |         conn = pgstore._admin_conn(dsn)
  168 |         conn.close()
  169 |         return True
  170 |     except Exception as e:
  171 |         print('-- seeding PG suite SKIPPED (%s: %s)'
  172 |               % (type(e).__name__, str(e).splitlines()[0]))
  173 |         return False
  174 | 
  175 | 
  176 | def pg_checks(dsn):
  177 |     print('-- seeding: postgres', dsn.rsplit('@', 1)[-1])
  178 |     from access import pgstore
  179 | 
  180 |     # clean slate
  181 |     pgstore.deprovision_tenant(A_SLUG, dsn)
  182 |     pgstore.deprovision_tenant(B_SLUG, dsn)
  183 | 
  184 |     # provision_tenant runs seed_vocabularies internally
  185 |     sa = pgstore.provision_tenant(A_SLUG, 'Seed A', 'публичная', dsn)
  186 |     sb = pgstore.provision_tenant(B_SLUG, 'Seed B', 'школьная', dsn)
  187 | 
  188 |     # --- system vocabs present & populated in the tenant schema ---
  189 |     vd = sa.get_vocabulary('vd.mnu')
  190 |     check('PG: system vd.mnu seeded', vd is not None and vd['kind'] == 'system')
  191 |     check('PG: vd.mnu has seed_version', vd['seed_version'] == seed_data.SEED_VERSION)
  192 |     check('PG: vd.mnu populated from catalog',
  193 |           len(sa.vocabulary_values('vd.mnu')) == len(seed_data.SYSTEM_VOCABS['vd.mnu']['values']))
  194 |     check('PG: ste.mnu full (14)', len(sa.vocabulary_values('ste.mnu')) == 14)
  195 |     check('PG: reservstatus.mnu full (5)', len(sa.vocabulary_values('reservstatus.mnu')) == 5)
  196 | 
  197 |     # --- institution vocabs present & EMPTY ---
  198 |     kv = sa.get_vocabulary('kv.mnu')
  199 |     check('PG: institution kv.mnu present', kv is not None and kv['kind'] == 'institution')
  200 |     check('PG: institution kv.mnu empty', sa.vocabulary_values('kv.mnu') == []
  201 |           and kv['seed_version'] is None)
  202 | 
  203 |     # --- per-tenant ISOLATION: edit A's vocab, B never sees it ---
  204 |     sa.upsert_vocabulary_value('kv.mnu', 'AB', 'Абонемент (A only)', 0, 'custom')
  205 |     a_kv = sa.vocabulary_values('kv.mnu')
  206 |     b_kv = sb.vocabulary_values('kv.mnu')
  207 |     check('PG: A sees its own kv edit', any(v['code'] == 'AB' for v in a_kv))
  208 |     check('PG: B does NOT see A kv edit (isolation)', b_kv == [])
  209 |     # And a system-vocab edit in A is invisible to B too
```

```py
  211 |     b_kn = [v for v in sb.vocabulary_values('vd.mnu') if v['code'] == 'KN']
  212 |     check('PG: B keeps original KN label (isolation)',
```
